Read with caution. {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Azure Flames'''}} {{Quote| The League... Shigaraki.. I've never cared about them. Did you know the world can be forever altered because of one person's singular obsession? There's no such thing as a true hero in this society. You frauds must be killed. Do you get it now? I'll be the one to carry Hero Killer's will!|- [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dabi Dabi] AKA Toya Todoroki as he stomps on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Keigo_Takami Keigo Takami]}} * {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Azure Flames|image1= New Dabi Icon.png|New Icon Flames.webp|Old Icon Azure Spawn 2.gif|Spawn Animation Spitt.png|Base DABBB.png|Awakening AzureFlamesAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name=Spite|cost=Free|caption-image1=Ultra blue flames|NameOfCharacter=Azure Flames}} ==Description== {{Azure Flames}} is a free-to-play character in Heroes Battlegrounds, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dabi Toya Todoroki, also known as Dabi]. He is the third character added to the game and the elder brother of {{Split Ice}}. Driven by hatred, this character utilizes a mix of close-to-long-range combat tactics. While his base damage output is relatively low, he possesses excellent combo potential and spam capabilities. {{Azure Flames}} arguably has one of the most potent [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimates]], dealing significant damage with strong combo opportunities. Players can master this character to unlock {{Mastered Azure Flames}}. Achieving {{Mastered Azure Flames}} requires gaining [[mastery]]. Mastery points are earned by defeating opponents while playing as Azure Flames (0.50%), completing quests from the [[NPC's|Daily Quest Giver]] (4-5%), and defeating {{The Prototype}} boss (5-13%). == Pros & Cons == === '''Pros''' === * '''Ember Eruption''' has a large Area of Effect (AoE) and is difficult to avoid * The [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]], {{Spite}}, deals substantial damage over time. * The base moveset offers significant combo potential. * '''Infernal Burn''' functions as a long-range attack. * The burn status effect can disrupt inexperienced players. * Effective in team fights due to AoE moves like '''Ember Eruption''' and '''Infernal Burn''', and moves like '''Azure Grab''' and '''Ember Assault''' that can separate opponents. * Ultimate moves possess good range and AoE coverage. * Good overall combo potential, with moves having relatively low cooldowns. * Even in Ultimate, there is some combo potential, '''Flamewave Burst''' (if you are close enough to the opponent) can allow you to combo into any other move in the Ultimate if you follow up with a front dash. === '''Cons''' === * The knockback distance of '''Infernal Burn''' can be inconsistent due to player latency. * '''Peaking Scorch''' does not bypass block. * Can be challenging for beginners, though basic combos are manageable. * '''Ember Assault''' is easily punishable if blocked. * '''Ember Eruption''' can be interrupted during its startup animation. * No capability for true combos after '''Ember Assault's''' rework and '''Azure Grab's''' changes |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" They then charge blue flames into their left hand, delivering one last smash that breaks the trashcan, leaving the enemy charred. |- |[[File:Dabi v1 burst.gif|border|frameless|center]]'''Burst''' |30.5% Total ----8.5% Initial burst ----10% Throw ----12% Burn Damage |The user does a small blast of blue fire, stunning everyone near, before the user lunges using their blue fire towards the last opponent who hit them, and blasts them away. ---- * Initial burst causes burn. |- |} ==Passives== {| class="fandom-table" !Passive Name !DMG Increase !Info |- |[[File:Azure Passive M1.gif|thumb]]'''Azure Burst''' |Increases M1 damage to ~25% total |Successfully landing a finisher engulfs the user's fists in blue flames for a short duration, increasing M1 damage. *This passive also inexplicably increases the travel distance and impact damage of '''Ember Assault'''. |- |'''Cooldown Reset''' |N/A |Defeating an opponent resets all ability cooldowns. * This applies to all characters. |} == Trivia == * '''Azure Grab''' was the first move in the game to be completely reworked from a previous concept. ** It replaced the original "Azure Burst," which was intended to grant the passive effect (now triggered by finishers) for an extended duration. ** The original '''Azure Burst''' move data might still exist and be obtainable via [[Gamepasses|Private Server +]] commands, but it is reportedly non-functional. * '''Infernal Burn''' was also completely reworked. It was originally a short-range AoE combo extender called '''Jet Burn'''. ** The '''Old Infernal Burn''' (Jet Burn) data might also be obtainable via [[Gamepasses|Private Server +]] commands. * The character {{Azure Flames}} (Dabi) being the brother of {{Split Ice}} (Shoto Todoroki) marks the first instance of sibling characters being playable in Heroes Battlegrounds. * '''Azure Flames Blitz''' features one of the shortest cutscene animations among grab moves in the game. * '''Azure Flames Blitz'''’s name is a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Misnomer misnomer], as the move is rather slow, despite having “Blitz” in its name. * '''Peaking Scorch''' is based on Dabi's imitation/version of his father Endeavor's signature move, "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Prominence_Burn PROMINENCE BURN]". The move was previously named "PROMINENCE BURN" in-game. ** Similarly, '''Azure Flames Blitz''' was originally named "BLUE FLAMES BLITZ". * The "X" shape formed by the flames on the user's torso during the '''{{Spite}}''' ultimate activation references Dabi copying his brother Shoto's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Phosphor Phosphor]" technique during their fight. * During the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Todoroki_Family_vs._Dabi Todoroki Family vs. Dabi fight] in the manga, Dabi awakened dormant elements of [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Frost his mother Rei's ice Quirk]. This is referenced by the ultimate move '''Frostflame Eruption'''. * The first variation of '''Ember Assault''' was potentially the first move added to the game designed to deal less than 10% damage (inflicting only minor burn damage). * The music track used during the '''{{Spite}}''' ultimate is an excerpt from "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHx-4d43404&ab_channel=EpicPlay All For One]" by EpicPlay (specifically, near the end of the track). * The character was initially named "Blue Flames" before being changed to {{Azure Flames}}. * '''Ember Assault''' used to M1 extend into 2 debuffed M1s and had a tech to allow a true M1 reset, but was recently reworked to be a rushdown combo ender, gaining a new finisher animation and dealing proper damage as well * The finisher for '''Ember Assault''' features the second-longest animations out of all finishers in their base moveset, being roughly 12,94 seconds long (measured from activation to the end of the animation, not including endlag). Only behind {{Warp Portal}}'s finisher for '''Dimensional Volley''' being 13,02 seconds long. [[Category:Missing information]] [[Category:Characters]] {{Characters}}

Characters: [[File:Character Selection Menu.png|thumb|The Character selection menu.]] '''Characters''' are based off Heroes and Villains from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki ''My Hero Academia'']. Each character usually has 8 moves, 4 in their base form and 4 in their [[Ultimate Mode]], but this can vary depending on the character. While most characters are accessible to all, some must be unlocked via special conditions in order to be played, and only one character ({{The Prototype}} boss) cannot be used. == Free Characters == ''Main articles: [[Heroes]] and [[Villains]]'' These are characters that anyone can use without any special conditions to play them. Cosmetics:
{{Stub}}

== What are Cosmetics?==

Cosmetics are clothing that applies to your Roblox character, you get  comestics by killing players, buying the '''Cosmestics''' Gamepass, Trading Ornaments with Santa or Trading Candies to Halloween NPC's (The Mysterious Witch, The Moonlight Ghost)

Some of the cosmetics are, in fact, capable of changing their color via '''[[VIP]]''' Gamepass. E.g. if your color is light-yellow, then '''Cursed Chains''' (Halloween-exclusive cosmetic) would emit its respective effects in a light-yellow color instead of red.

== Cosmetics Gamepass ==
The Cosmetics Gamepass has been removed from the game because the developers of Heroes Battlegrounds are currently reworking the gamepass. Since the cosmetics system got reworked, they feel like the cosmetics gamepass does not really provide that it is intended for. The gamepass will come back soon that has a different functionality, with previous gamepass owners also obtaining this.

Custom Kill Sound:
{{Gamepass|title1=Custom Kill Sound|image1=Custom Kill Sound.jpg|cost=200 Robux}}

'''Custom Kill Sound''' is a [[Gamepasses|gamepass]] in the game. This gamepass lets you play whatever sound you want anytime you kill a player. The sounds that can be played are always from the [https://create.roblox.com/store/audio Roblox sound catalog].
Custom Move Text:
{{Gamepass|title1=Custom Move Text|image1=CustomMoveGamepass.png|cost=199 Robux}}

'''Custom Move Text''' is a [[Gamepasses|gamepass]] in [[Heroes Battlegrounds]] that allows you to display a custom text message above your head whenever you use any move. Customize in the top left settings! This gamepass will show a message above your head upon the usage of any move. The message activates only if you hit someone, but the exception is being [[Symbol of Fear|Vanquish]], because it activates the message instantly without need to hit your opponent.
Dash: =='''Overview'''== '''Dash''' is an extra ability that aims for great mobility while also having it's own variants. By pressing '''Q''', you can activate the ability, which will propel your avatar forward normally, though depending on if you are using any other keys while dashing, it will replace the forward '''dash''' with anything else. When your avatar reaches any subject '''(Player, Weakest Dummy, Leaderboard #1-3 Avatars)''', your avatar will deliver a swift punch (in case of {{Decaying Hatred}}, will cross their arms into the '''"X"''' letter before spreading them in the exact same directions) to the opponent's left, temporarily stunning them, and while so, leaving them wide open for your '''M1's'''. Also, whenever you are using any of the '''Dash Variations''', you can use your '''Shift Lock''', so that way, dashing into the desired direction will be much easier while also giving you the ability to '''redirect''' yourself. =='''Dash Variations'''== * '''Front Dash''' - Your avatar lunges '''5 ground tiles forward''' before delivering a swift jab to the one's left. Achieved by simply using '''Q''' without anything else or while holding '''W'''. By using '''Shift Lock''', you can achieve "'''Surprise Dash'''" by dashing into one's back, usually being helpful against '''Block'''. * '''Side Dash''' - Your avatar will dodge any incoming attack to either left or right, depending on what key was pressed in order to achieve '''Side Dash'''. Achieved by using the key combination of '''Q + A or D''', depending on where you want to dodge, left or right, usually helpful against '''Unblockable''' attacks that require dodging. * '''Back Dash''' - Your avatar flips itself backwards twice, travelling a considerable distance while retreating. Achievable by using keys combination of '''Q + S''' and only it. Is useful for retreating and confusing your opponent. * '''Side Dash Downslam''' - This is a technique move that is made of '''3 M1's away from the opponent + Side Dash to the opponent while having your avatar jump + M1'''. This technique can be used to catch opponents by surprise with a '''Downslam''' that is unblockable. * Retreat Combo - If you want to retreat as fast as possible, then use this combination of all dashes - '''"Side Dash + Back Dash + Side Dash + Front Dash + Side Dash + Aerial Downslam (M1 in air) + Side Dash," and then do the same whenever Back Dash cooldown ends.''' * '''All techniques that are relying on M1's (ex. '''Loop Dash''') are described here - [[Techniques]].''' '''(As the page declares, the original author of the page is djghfxdcjor. The page was reworked by''' Sovereign '''//''' Mono.)

{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Decaying Hatred'''}}{{Quote|"It's a corrupt, vicious cycle. Everything I've witnessed, this whole system you've built has always rejected me. Now I'm ready to reject it. That's why I destroy."|- [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tomura_Shigaraki Tomura Shigaraki] to the heroes in [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Jaku_City Jaku]}} {{Deku|title1=Decaying Hatred|awakening_name=Everything For One|Cost=Free|Herogreen.png= New Shigaraki Icon.png|New Icon Rots.webp|Old Icon Fear Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Fear base.png|Base Shigik.png|Awakening SymbolOfFearAura.gif|Awakening Aura }} ==Description== {{Decaying Hatred}} is the 9th character (11th if counting {{Huge Lady}} and {{The Strongest}} as PS+ exclusive characters) and the [[Villains|fourth villain character]] added to Heroes Battlegrounds. He is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tomura_Shigaraki Tenko Shimura], currently known as Tomura Shigaraki, who is the main [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Paranormal_Liberation_Front villain] and primary antagonist of the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga) My Hero Academia series]. ==Pros and Cons== ==='''Pros'''=== * All base moves have finisher animations that execute at relatively high opponent health, enabling rapid kills. * '''Vanquish''' possesses a 360° hitbox and bypasses block. * '''Decaying Counter''' deals significant damage (22%) and can extend combos with an Aerial Slam, '''Vanquish''', or '''Void Steps''' due to the spin-knockback. (Knockback distance is similar of that of {{Warp Portal}}'s old 3rd move, [[Removed Content|'''Portal Combo'''.]]) * '''Deteriorate''' is an effective and easy-to-use combo extender. * Capable of dealing high damage with combos. * Relatively simple to use, with the exception of '''Void Steps'''. * While '''Void Steps''' is difficult to land consistently, its potential damage output is substantial. * The base moveset deals a total of 59% damage (excluding M1s), making it one of the highest-damage base kits in the game. * '''Deteriorate''' covers a distance similar to a side dash. * If '''Radio Blast''' successfully hits, the opponent loses access to their moveset for 10 seconds. * '''Scatter''' has sufficient range to extend combos after '''Radio Blast''' if timed correctly. * '''Reflective Counter''' steals a move from the target character's moveset. If the countered attack is not a specific move (e.g., a front dash), a random move from that character is stolen. * '''Reflective Counter''' can counter both melee and ranged attacks. * '''Reflective Counter''' can counter all moves in the game except for other counter-type abilities (e.g., Indomitable Will), awakening activations, Tenth Torch, and Nuke. * '''Scatter''' can extend combos after '''Radio Blast''' and '''Reflective Counter'''. * Possesses the strongest single ultimate move in terms of potential damage (excluding {{The Strongest}}), requiring the user to hit all Quick Time Events (QTEs) and the target to miss all of theirs for a one-shot (110% max damage). * '''TRUE EVIL''' deals either 46% or 110% damage, depending on the success of QTE inputs by both the user and the target. * '''Vanquish''' has an AOE and can also be used in a combo, this makes it good for teamers due to being able to combo and also use an AOE to prevent being interrupted. * '''Decaying Counter''' can combo extend with '''Void Steps''' making it a very rewarding counter to land. * Killing certain opponents can be useful due to the '''Quirk Snatch''' passive. ===='''Cons'''==== * Cannot gain passive effects by defeating dummies or {{The Prototype}} boss. * Reliant on successfully executing a finisher on another player to activate the '''Quirk Snatch''' passive. * The follow-up hits of '''Void Steps''' are challenging to execute, requiring precise timing for a total of six inputs to achieve worthwhile damage, which can be difficult for mobile players. * The follow-up hits of '''Void Steps''' lack I-frames or hyper-armor even if the initial hit lands, making the move vulnerable to interruption in team fights. * Has only two low-damage True Combos in the base kit. * Moves are easily punishable if missed. * In exchange for its high potential damage, '''TRUE EVIL''' does not pause the ultimate bar's depletion during its animation. * Stealing {{Split Ice}}'s '''Spear of Ice''' passive grants a shorter duration for the ice javelin buff. * Opponents can easily evade '''TRUE EVIL''' due to its slow walking startup, making it less effective unless the opponent's evasive is baited or they are caught off guard (e.g., after a downslam or against new players). * Heavily relies enemy to waste evasive to use to maximum effectiveness (Base & Ultimate) * Enemy players can evasive out of void steps to avoid the damage. * '''Radio Blast''' is one of the ultimate moves with the lowest amount of damage. * '''Radio Blast''' does not disable m1 attacks. * Combo extending off '''Radio Blast''' is very inconsistent. * All moves (especially Vanquish) have moderate to high amounts of endlag if missed. ==Moveset== |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" Villain M1 Chain.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Hooked Decay (M1)''' |1 hit: 4% Damage All hits: 16% |0.5 seconds |The user delivers three basic hook punches before kneeing them in the stomach and otherwise, it is very punishable if missed. |- |[[File:Deteriorate Move.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Deteriorate''' |8% |20 seconds |The user rushes forward with their hand extended. *Applies to all characters. |- | '''Health Pack''' |Securing a kill heals the user for a set amount of health. *Applies to all characters. |} [[Category:Characters]] ==Trivia/Notes == * This character is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tomura_Shigaraki Tomura Shigaraki] from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki My Hero Academia], a villain whose [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Decay Quirk, Decay,] allows him to disintegrate anything he touches with all five fingers. * The character was originally named "Rot Villain" during development. ** The character was also renamed as a "Symbol of Fear" afterward, but in May 19th got renamed as "Decaying Hatred". * The Awakening name, {{Everything For One}}, is a reference to the primary antagonist of My Hero Academia, "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/All_For_One All For One]," and his [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/All_For_One_(Quirk) Quirk of the same name], which allows him to steal and bestow Quirks. * '''Radio Blast''' combines elements of Shigaraki's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Rivet_Stab Rivet Stab]" and "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Radio_Waves Radio Waves]" Quirks. * '''Scatter''' is a reference to All For One's [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Ultimate_Move ultimate move,] "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GuICI8topGs Ultimate Quirk Combination]," used against [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Toshinori_Yagi All Might] during their [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Toshinori_Yagi_vs._All_For_One:_Rematch rematch]. * '''Void Steps''' appears to be heavily inspired by the "[https://streetfighter.fandom.com/wiki/Wrath_of_the_Raging_Demon Wrath of the Raging Demon]/[https://streetfighter.fandom.com/wiki/Shun_Goku_Satsu Shun Goku Satsu]" [https://streetfighter.fandom.com/wiki/Critical_Art Critical Art] from the [https://streetfighter.fandom.com/wiki/Street_Fighter_Wiki Street Fighter] series, as both involve teleporting around a target and attacking from various angles. * '''TRUE EVIL''' may reference the [https://youtu.be/NvcYfsSTzG4?t=116 Sukuna vs. Jogo fight in Jujutsu Kaisen]. * In the first variant of '''TRUE EVIL''', the final impact frame briefly displays the kanji "{{Ruby|撃滅|げきめつ}}"(Gekimetsu), which translates to "Annihilation" or "Destruction". * The '''Quirk Snatch''' passive is based on Shigaraki's [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tomura_Shigaraki_vs._Cathleen_Bate fight] with [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Cathleen_Bate Star and Stripe], where he brutally defeats her and (nearly) steals her [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/New_Order Quirk, New Order]. * The ultimate move '''Radio Blast''' (incorrectly listed as first ultimate move previously, it's a regular ultimate move) combines the Quirks "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Rivet_Stab Rivet Stab]" and "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Radio_Waves Radio Waves]". * This character predominantly uses their left arm for attacks. The colorful circles in '''Reflective Counter''' are visually similar to the shield ability of the character [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Nine Nine] from the movie My Hero Academia: [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia:_Heroes:_Rising Heroes: Rising]. * The environment during the ultimate activation and the '''Reflective Counter''' finisher is based on the "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/One_For_All Vestige World]". The spectral figures that appear are intended to represent [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/All_For_One All For One]. * During the finisher animation of '''Reflective Counter''', the song "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XTywMOGKh0U Supercharger]" by Sybrid can be heard, possibly as a reference to the game Combat Gods II. * '''Reflective Counter''' can steal an opponent's ultimate move if its finisher is performed on them (e.g., it could steal {{Mastered Green Hero}}'s 120%). {{Characters}}
[[File:{{{File|Shiggy's Revamp Back.webp}}}|{{{Size|1800px}}}]]
Dullahan: {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''DullahanDullahan'''}} {{Quote|Dullahan rises once again... Kill {{USERNAME}}, the Pumpkin King, for rewards!|In-game system message when {{USERNAME}} collects all four pieces.}} [[Category:Characters]] {{Character heros|Dullahan='''DullahanDullahan'''|image1= DullahanFigure.png|Base DullahanInTheMoonlight.png|Awakening |awakening_name={{Moonlight}}|cost=Collect The Pieces of Him|caption-image1=|NameOfCharacter={{Dullahan}}|Archetype=Playable Boss}} ==Description== {{Dullahan}}, also known as Pumpkin King, was a Halloween 2025 Boss in [[Heroes Battlegrounds]] a player can play the game with upon a certain condition. To become {{Dullahan}}, you need to wait for the Pumpkin Moon to rise ''(Every 75 Minutes in Public Servers)'', which is seen by skybox turning orange. After it does, you need to collect four pieces of him: his armor, his head, his greatsword and his horse. Once Dullahan dies, the system will note: ''"{{Dullahan}}'s ghost rests... {{USERNAME}}, the Pumpkin King, has died! They managed to gather (Kills during {{Dullahan}}) Souls!"'' == Pros and Cons== === Pros === * All of Dullahan's moves are uninterruptable when start-up is not interrupted. * False Sun has auto aim and deals a lot of damage. * The minions from using the Rise of Pumpkins can interrupt other players attempts to attack you. === Cons === * Lack of i-Frames after move end leaves Dullahan vulnerable. * Some moves are punishable after they end. ==Movesets== |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background: #ad5900;" |'''Attack''' ! style="background: #ad5900;" |'''Damage''' ! style="background: #ad5900;" |'''Cooldown''' ! style="background: #ad5900;" |'''Info''' |- |[[File:DullahanM1s.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Harrowing Tearing (M1)''' |20% |~0.5 seconds |Dullahan swings upward, then horizontally to the left before thrusting his sword into the ground and ending with spinning and swinging his sword diagonally. |- |[[File:DullahanArise.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Rise of Pumpkins''' |34,1% |45 seconds |Dullahan plunges his sword into the ground, flaming it until he pulls it out, creating a orange/blue explosion that sends bystanders flying and summons two pumpkin clones to aid him. |- |[[File:DullahanHarvest.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Soul Harvest''' |15% (First Slash) ---- 25% (Second Slash w/o '''Burn''') |45 seconds |Dullahan surrounds themselves with flames in start-up before swinging their sword to the left. Once hit, Dullahan raises their sword, collecting the souls and finishing off with a demolishing spin with their sword. ---- * Can serve as a combo-extender if missing second slash on purpose |- |[[File:DullahanHorse.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Ghost Rider''' |12,7% (Per Swing) ---- 43,6% (Final Land) |60 seconds |Dullahan's horse appears and roars as Dullahan rides it. Upon repeatedly using Ghost Rider, Dullahan will swing his sword left and right, flinging outward those who have been caught in. Upon the move ending, Dullahan's horse roars as Dullahan himself soars high in the air, landing down with plunging his sword into the ground, making a orange/blue explosion that sends enemies flying away. ---- * Dullahan's horse's first roar knocks nearby enemies away, yet which deals zero damage. |- |[[File:DullahanCruelSun.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''False Sun''' |21% (Per Pumpkin) |40 seconds |Dullahan raises his head as his eyes glow orange/blue, projecting flame pumpkins that auto-aim on nearby opponents and explode on contact as long as Dullahan user holds the move. If used one-tap, Dullahan's head's eyes will simply glow and nothing will happen. ---- * Inflicts '''Burn''' on contact |- |} |-|Moonlight= {| class="fandom-table" style="text-align:center" ! style="background: #4f97bf;" |'''Attack''' ! style="background: #4f97bf;" |'''Damage''' ! style="background: #4f97bf;" |'''Cooldown''' ! style="background: #4f97bf;" |'''Info''' |- |[[File:DullahanMoonlight.gif|border|center|frameless]]{{Moonlight}} |N/A |N/A |Dullahan raises his sword skyward as he forms a miniature projection of moon, making it descend upon him and grant him a newfound look - changing his armor and sword color, as well as making his sword scorch. ---- * Dullahan's moves does not change, but only their effect color - from orange to blue. Activating it resets moves cooldown, and False Sun will spawn more pumpkins. |- |[[File:DullahanMoonM1s.gif|border|center|frameless]]'''Lunar Blaze (M1s)''' |32% |~0.5 seconds |Dullahan performs the same M1 Chain as before, but with a icy white flaming sword. ---- * M1 Chain now inflicts '''Burn''' |- |} ==Trivia/Notes== * If you try to perform an [[Emotes|emote]] with {{Dullahan}} used, a notification will pop up and note: ''"Sorry, you cannot emote as {{Dullahan}}. Sorry, too many bugs."'' * When gaining a 10 Killstreak on {{Dullahan}}, you get a special emote called '''Horseman's Call'''. * {{Dullahan}}'s moves can be customized via '''''VIP Effect Color'''''. * The minions uses {{Winged Assassin}}'s '''M1''' sound effect when they attack the player. * '''False Sun''' may be a reference to Demon Slayer, as Hinokami Kagura is known as the '''False Sun''' by the fanbase. It also may be a reference to '''False Rainbow'''. ** ''For some, '''False Sun''' might refer to '''[https://nanatsu-no-taizai.fandom.com/wiki/Escanor Cruel Sun]'''.'' * This character was removed after the Christmas update, due to being Halloween-exclusive. ** Could be brought back in the 2026 Halloween event. * {{Dullahan}} has much more Health Points than {{The Prototype}}, according to the first surviving '''NUKE''' without critical health drop. ** Theoretically, {{Dullahan}}'s Health Points might be '1,000'. * The best character to use in order to find the pieces to access this character may be {{Mastered Green Hero}} due to its awakening, as its move can dash you forward at accelerating speeds. ** Much better alternative is {{Full Throttle}}, due to the later having the moves that push player into a set direction both in base and awakening. * '''Soul Harvest'''' can drain a small portion of the target's ultimate meter, both if the target is in ultimate mode or not. {{Characters}} [[Category:Books]] [[Category:Special Characters]]
Early Access:
{{Gamepass|title1=Early Access|image1=Early Access.jpg|cost=245 Robux}}

The '''Early Access''' [[Gamepasses|gamepass]] from [[Heroes Battlegrounds]]. It costs 245 Robux.

== What you obtain ==
'''There are 2 ways to obtain the [[Gamepasses|Gamepass]]'''.

Buying Early Access gives you a unreleased character that has not been released to the public. It will only be available to people who have this gamepass. You will have the character until the character is official released and you will not have to pay for Early Access again for the next characters.

There's another way to get the gamepass is by gifting the player, most likely people who don't have Robux to buy the gamepass.


'''''As of Jan 10th 2026, the Early Access character you would get will be the "{{Winged Assassin}}".'''''
Emote Pages:
{{Gamepass|title1=Emote Pages|image1=Emote Pages.png|cost=95 Robux}}

'''Emote Pages''' is a [[Gamepasses|gamepass]] in [[Heroes Battlegrounds]].

== What You Obtain ==

* '''Extra Rows''': Emote Pages gives you four extra rows of emotes, allowing you to easily pick between 20 emote slots and 40 if you have [[VIP]]!
* '''Emotes''': Unlocks 3 emotes on purchase (if you already have max emotes, lets you redeem 3 free emotes next time they add emotes)
Emotes:
{{Stub}}

'''(This article is not finished as over 520 emotes are missing from this page! This page will be updated overtime though.)'''

=='''How to Obtain Emotes'''==
*Obtainable through 50 kills (35 if you have [[VIP|VIP Gamepass)]]
*Purchasing with Robux. (25 for one emote, 115 for 5 emotes, 225 for 10 emotes, 550 for 25 emotes, and 1,000 for 50 emotes.)
*Being Gifted Emotes (Same prices as above, with the exception that you can also gift specific emotes, which cost 75)
*There are also purchasable Limited Time Emotes, these typically cost from 99 Robux to 299 Robux. After 2 weeks have passed, unless under specific circumstances, the emotes will be removed and no longer available, typically forever / for a very long time unless brought back, like what was recently shown with Combat Gods and a few others .

*Event limited time emotes usually obtainable through event currency (candy & ornaments).
* Doing the most or the second most damage on [[The Prototype]] before killing it (1st: 2 emotes, 2nd: 1 emote).

There were 4 codes that gave emote(s):

*!code 100k (Gives 3 random emotes)
*!code Halloween (Gives 1 random emote)
*!code flamesmastery (also gives 10% [[mastery]] on [[Azure Flames]])
*!code 100kviolet (also gives 15% mastery on Azure Flames)
*!code slayerult
*!code chunchieparty
* !Code chunchiepartycontinue (if used code shroom activates like a part of chunchieparty)
* !Code SorryForDelay
No current codes  

You can activate some codes without chat, use the Code Shroom (codes in code shroom updates every update).

=='''Trivia/Notes'''==

[https://blox-fruits.fandom.com/wiki/User:CRY0Mandoid coding credits]
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Explosion Hero'''}} {{Quote|"If you keep looking down on everyone, you're never gonna notice your own weaknesses."|- [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Katsuki_Bakugo Katsuki Bakugo] sharing the lessons he's learned for himself to a group of troublesome kids.}} {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Explosion Hero|image1= New Bakugo Icon.png|New Icon Fire.webp|Old Icon Explosion_Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation BaseEHero.png|Base Bakk.png|Fury Ignition Tenth.png|The Tenth Torch ExplosionHeroAura1.gif|Fury Ignition Aura ExplosionHeroAura2.gif|The Tenth Torch Aura |awakening_name=Fury Ignition TENTH TORCH|cost=Free|caption-image1=God dynamite Bomb|NameOfCharacter=Explosion Hero}} ==Description== {{Explosion Hero}} is the 7th character added to Heroes Battlegrounds (10th character overall, including [[Private Server Commands|Private Server]] exclusives). This character is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Katsuki_Bakugo Katsuki Bakugo] from the series [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki My Hero Academia]. His abilities revolve around his [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Explosion Quirk], which allows him to create explosions from his nitroglycerin-like sweat. {{Explosion Hero}} is unique as the first character to possess two distinct Ultimate Modes: '''Fury Ignition''' and '''TENTH TORCH'''. To activate '''TENTH TORCH''', the user must deal sufficient damage while in the '''Fury Ignition''' state (approximately 85% of an opponent's health bar or equivalent damage dealt to any target) and then use the fourth ability slot within the '''Fury Ignition''' moveset. This multi-stage ultimate system contributes to {{Explosion Hero}} being considered a highly effective yet the strongest choice against the '''[[The Prototype]]''' boss. ==Pros and Cons == ===Pros=== * Possesses very simple yet high-damage [[combos]]. * The character offers significant mix-up potential and [[Combos|combo]] opportunities, particularly with his first and second base moves. * Features two Ultimate forms, allowing the user to enter two separate invincibility-frame (I-frame) states, potentially avoiding high-damage enemy attacks. * '''Exploding Star''' pauses the Ultimate bar drain for its entire cinematic duration, enabling the user to maximize their [[Ultimate Mode|ultimate mode]] uptime and safely wait for other ability cooldowns. * '''Ground Cannon''' can extend combos into every other base move, including '''Blast Catapult'''. * '''Fury Ignition''' is effective against multiple opponents (teamers). * All moves in the '''Fury Ignition''' ultimate have low cooldowns. * Every base move has a finisher, facilitating easier activation of the character's passives. * '''Fury Ignition''' provides high mobility with the '''Eruption Dive''' ability. * '''Eruption Dive''' has a large Area of Effect (AoE), making it effective for damaging multiple opponents. * '''THE TENTH TORCH''', when all its moves and the transformation sequence are landed, can deal astonishing damage (up to approximately 348%). * Due to it's higher damage output of both [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]] activations and their subsequent moves makes {{Explosion Hero}} a stronger choice for defeating the '''[[The Prototype]]''' boss. * '''BLAST AWAY''' is almost a guaranteed kill if landed after three M1s against an opponent with no evasive options remaining. * '''BLAST AWAY''' grants I-Frames during its animation, even if it does not hit a target. * All base moves can combo-extend into each other. * '''NUKE''' preserves the user's killstreak despite the self-destruct mechanic. * Due to having two ultimates, using both on teamers is very effective and rewarding. * Since you do not have to use '''Powered Shot''' and '''Ground Cannon's''' second use right away, you can use the second use as another move in a combo. * His passive allows for more reliable combos with moves like '''Explosion Combo.''' * '''Powered Shot's''' second use has a large amount of range. * Every move can be used after '''Explosion Combo.''' ===Cons=== * '''Blast Catapult''' can sometimes launch targets too far for a reliable follow-up, or occasionally fail to launch them at all. * The base moveset arguably has one of the lowest total damage outputs per individual hit among all characters. * The first use of '''Powered Shot''' has recoil, which can cause the user to miss follow-up attacks (e.g., a frontal dash). The second use of '''Powered Shot''' also has specific timing requirements. * If the user does not deal enough damage to unlock '''THE TENTH TORCH''', '''Fury Ignition''' effectively only has three offensive ultimate moves. * The character initially struggles with consistent, high-damage [[combos]] as it lacks true combo extenders in its base kit, relying on opponents wasting their evasive maneuvers. * Although '''TENTH TORCH''' deals immense damage, its duration is shorter than other ultimates, and its moves can be punished if misused. * The startup animation of '''NUKE''' does not have I-Frames, allowing opponents to interrupt it. * '''BLITZ KICK''' is easily punishable if used without setup (raw) or if it misses. * '''Ground Cannon''''s timer was decreased, forcing you to use it quickly. == Moves == |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background: #ff9d00;" |'''Moves''' ! style="background: #ff9d00;" |'''Damage''' ! style="background: However, using only the first part of '''Ground Cannon''' or '''Powered Shot''' will not apply this damage boost unless the second part of the move is also used and connects. |- |'''Cooldown Reset''' |N/A |Defeating an opponent with credit resets all ability cooldowns. * This applies to all characters. |} ==Trivia/Notes== * The theme music for the '''{{Fury Ignition}}''' ultimate is [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d1AVAjKHayI "Violence" by Will Stetson]. * The theme music for the '''{{TENTH TORCH}}''' ultimate is [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P3HvdBkJQxI "Might+U" by Makayla Phillips]. * The theme music that plays during the '''Exploding Star''' move is [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3Hzqne-bJbw "Bombing King" by Paul Owen Music]. * The term "Soft Ragdoll," as it applies to this character, describes a state where an opponent is ragdolled by the first use of '''Ground Cannon''' but remains vulnerable to M1 attacks. * '''Ground Cannon''' is likely a reference to Bakugo's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Land_Mine_Blast Land Mine Blast]" technique from the source material. * '''Blast Catapult''' is likely a reference to Bakugo's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Explode-A-Pult Explode-A-Pult/X-Catapult]" technique. * '''Eruption Dive''' appears to be a reference to Bakugo's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Howitzer_Impact Howitzer Impact]." The startup animation, involving explosive propulsion, may also reference his "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Explosive_Speed Explosive Speed]". * '''Infernal Shot''' is likely based on Bakugo's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/AP_Shot AP Shot]" or "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Stun_Grenade Stun Grenade]" techniques, or a combination of both. * The ultimate move '''BLAST AWAY''' is likely a reference to Bakugo's "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Howitzer_Impact:_Cluster Howitzer Impact: Cluster]". * The cutscene for activating '''{{TENTH TORCH}}''' is a direct reference to a scene in the movie "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia:_Heroes:_Rising My Hero Academia: Heroes Rising]". In this scene, [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya] temporarily transfers a portion of the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/One_For_All One For All] Quirk's power to Katsuki Bakugo through their injuries, enabling them to defeat the villain [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Nine Nine]. The referenced scene can be viewed [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J-5UP1w99BQ here] (relevant part ends around the 1-minute mark). * During an impact frame of the punch that initiates the '''{{TENTH TORCH}}''' activation, a rectangle displaying the word "SMASH" can be briefly seen. * In another impact frame during the '''{{TENTH TORCH}}''' activation punch, Japanese symbols appear, which translate to "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/One_For_All ONE FOR ALL]". * In the final impact frame of the '''NUKE''' explosion, orange text reading "BOOM!" is visible. * When an opponent is successfully grabbed by the '''BLAST AWAY''' move, there is a small chance their character will audibly exclaim “HELP!” or "SOMEONE HELP ME!". * A gameplay quirk exists with '''Blast Catapult''': while its finisher typically activates at 10% health, if the thrown opponent collides with a wall or another solid object, it can register as a kill at 14% health. This may display a yellow damage number (if the Combo Counter is enabled) as if a standard finisher occurred, but it might not consistently grant the passive effect. Full Throttle:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Full Throttle'''}}{{Quote|'''Ingenium will dash across the land to take a lost child by the hand, because giving a help that's not asked for.. is what makes a true hero!''' | - Full Throttle ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tenya_Ida Tenya Ida])}}{{Character_heros|NameOfCharacter=Full Throttle|image1= New Ida Icon.png|New Icon Jet icon.png|Old Icon Jet Spawn Animation.gif|Spawn Animation Jet engine.png|Base JetUlt.png|Awakening JetEngineAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name=Beyond Speed|cost=Free}} ==Description== {{Full Throttle}} is the 10th character released in Heroes Battlegrounds (12th, including [[The Strongest]] and [[Huge Lady]], who are [[Private Server Commands|PS+]] characters), added on December 1st, 2024. This character is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tenya_Ida Tenya Ida] from the series [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga) My Hero Academia.] ==Pros and Cons== ==='''Pros''' === * '''Recipro Assault''' can hit grounded players, allowing for maximum damage if the user is quick enough. * The variants of '''Recipro Assault''' stun opponents long enough for an uppercut follow-up. * '''Recipro Assault’s''' range allows the user to hit opponents even if they attempt to evasive with a side dash, due to the final kick in the dash portion (requires quick execution). * '''Sound Breaker''' (Air Variant) is an excellent combo extender. * '''Turbine Drive''' (Ground Variant) can be used as a combo starter or following the second or third gear of '''Recipro Assault'''. * The Air Variant of '''Turbine Drive''' resets the user's M1s and can function as an aerial combo extender (unblockable for the same reason as other M1 resets). * All variants of '''Recipro Assault''' propel the user further than most base moves in the game, making Full Throttle effective for travel and chasing down opponents, especially those with low health. * Despite their considerable startups, the first and second gears of '''Recipro Assault''' and '''Sound Breaker''' are quick once active, useful for catching opponents off guard. * The majority of moves can hit grounded opponents. * Despite its long startup, '''Jet Stream (Held Variant)''' allows the user to combo extend with '''Recipro Assault (Third Gear)''', '''Turbine Drive (Air Variant for M1s)''', or '''Sound Breaker (Air Variant)'''. * '''Recipro Assault''' (Second and Third Gear) can also combo extend with [[IWO|I.W.O.]] interactions (particularly [[Structures and Objects|Tree]], [[Structures and Objects|Trash Cans]], and [[Structures and Objects|Pillars]]) when followed by a front dash. * Possesses high-damage combos, as all moves can chain into each other, and long ragdoll times facilitate linking certain moves. * Aerial combos consistently utilize '''Recipro Assault's''' Third Gear, allowing for safer, high-damage play. * Aerial combos, while having a longer setup time, are effective against teamers, as the user can float above them, making it difficult for other opponents to hit while dishing out damage. * Ground combos are also strong against teamers, as moves like '''Sound Breaker''' and '''Recipro Assault (First and Second Gear)''' deal significant AoE damage and can leave opponents vulnerable to air combos. ===='''Cons'''==== * The first and second gears of '''Recipro Assault''' have significant endlag (due to covering long distances quickly). * The first gear of '''Recipro Assault''' have a very strict hitbox, making it unreliable on neutral. * The '''Motor Combo''' passive only lasts for 25 seconds after defeating an opponent. * '''Sound Breaker''' is avoidable due to its long startup animation. * '''Jetstream (Held)''' have a significant endlag on top of being extremely predictable. * Most moves have extreme endlag if they miss. * '''Turbo Release''' is harder to hit due to its smaller hitbox unless the opponent's evasive has been baited. * If an opponent is familiar with {{Full Throttle}}'s ultimate, '''Chronos''' can be easily dodged or interrupted. * '''Turbo Release''' Finisher does not pause the ultimate bar. * '''Chronos''' can be avoided by standing on environmental objects like rocks. * The Aerial Variant of '''Turbine Drive''' is somewhat buggy. == Moveset == |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" (This is currently bugged, often increasing damage from 7% to 14% if used with a move already on cooldown). *Can be used as a Combo Starter or Extender while the passive is active. *Ragdolls long enough for a follow-up downslam or uppercut. |- |'''Cooldown Reset''' |N/A |After defeating an opponent with credit, all of the user's cooldowns reset. ---- *This applies to all characters. |} ==Trivia/Notes== * {{Full Throttle}} is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tenya_Ida Tenya Iida] from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga) My Hero Academia]. Tenya is the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Class_1-A Class 1-A] Representative, and his [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Engine Quirk] grants him engine-like boosters in his calves, allowing for incredible speed and powerful kicks. * The finisher animation for '''Recipro Assault''' may be a reference to the scene where [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tenya_Ida/Image_Gallery?file=Recipro_Burst.gif#Gifs Tenya Iida regained his heroic conviction while attempting to save Shoto Todoroki]. * {{Full Throttle}}'s M1 attacks exclusively use kicks, reflecting Tenya Iida's combat style in the source material, where he primarily relies on his legs. ** There are only two instances where he uses his hands/arms, coincidentally, both are finishers. First is the finisher for '''Turbine Drive''' (Aerial), while the other is '''Jet Storm'''. * {{Full Throttle}} is the only character in the game to primarily use leg-based attacks for their M1s. * An intended mechanic: Using '''Recipro Assault''' immediately after the second gear of '''Jet Stream''' will always execute the Third Gear variant of '''Recipro Assault''', regardless of charge time. * The startup animation for '''Chronos''' is slightly similar to '''Warm Up''' emote. * The finisher animation for '''Sound Breaker''' bears a resemblance to a scene from [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XEtHpGSSTOU Combat Gods II], where the character [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XEtHpGSSTOU&t=277 Jun blocks Shinji's rapid attacks]. * {{Full Throttle}} has the lowest damage output from its [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]] activation (7%) compared to other characters. * The finisher for '''Turbo Release''' is based on [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zQyR89ZVsNg The Flash's "Speed Zone" super move] from the fighting game [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Injustice:_Gods_Among_Us Injustice: Gods Among Us]. * The finisher for '''Jet Storm''' might be a reference to the movie "Sonic The Hedgehog 3". * The startup of the Dropkick delivered in '''Recipro Assault''''s finisher is similar to [https://stickmanfanon.fandom.com/wiki/KJ KJ]'s 20-20-20 Dropkick as seen [https://youtu.be/JdDJ1H-ZV2o?si=5Z1lYM_5DCMKmBx6&t=108 here]. * You can see the player below the victim during '''Recipro Assault''''s finisher when performed in the air. * The in-game character name has a potential, but unlikely, reference to the game called identically the same name. * In one of the game's previous updates, the character's name was changed from "Jet Engine" to "Full Throttle." The reason is unknown but may be related to avoiding copyright, as Iida’s quirk is referred to as both “Jet” and “Engine” in the source material. * When you spawn as {{Full Throttle}} and during the spawn animation, the user is seen wearing glasses for a brief moment before the animation ends. {{Characters}}

Green Hero:
{{Quote|I have to work harder than anyone else to make it!|- [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya] and his desire to be a hero.}} [[Category:Characters]] {{Character heros|GreenHero=Green Hero |image1= File:Green Hero Icon.webp|New Icon Hero green.webp|Old Icon Green Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Green hero in game.png|Base Dekus.png|Awakening GreenHeroBaseAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name=Maximum Overdrive |cost=Free|caption-image1=|NameOfCharacter={{Green Hero}}|Archetype=Rushdown}} ==Description== {{Green Hero}} is the first character developed in Heroes Battlegrounds, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya], and is generally considered the easiest to use. {{Green Hero}} is a free character, serving as the default choice for new players. {{Green Hero}} possesses decent combo potential and a powerful ultimate when utilized effectively. Players can master this character to unlock {{Mastered Green Hero}}. Achieving {{Mastered Green Hero}} requires gaining [[mastery]]. Mastery points are earned by defeating opponents while playing as {{Green Hero}} (0.50%), completing quests from the Daily Quest Giver (4-5%), and defeating {{The Prototype}} (5-13%). == Pros and Cons== ===Pros=== * Can deal somewhat high damage. * The Ultimate ability can inflict significant damage against both passive and aggressive playstyles. * Players have ample time to execute [[Combos|combos]] on grounded opponents. * '''Hyper''' grants hyper armor during its animation. * Several moves can extend combos: '''Hyper (Air Variant)''', '''Focus Shot (Air Variant)''', and '''Blitz.''' * '''Hyper (Air Variant)''' deals increased damage based on the user's height. * '''{{Maximum Overdrive}}''', offers excellent mobility options with '''OVERCHARGE''' (9 tiles), '''Cosmic Smash''' (12 tiles), and '''1,000,000%''' (37 tiles). * Following the '''Overkick''' rework, all base moves became unblockable. * Opponents hit by '''Cosmic Smash''' cannot use ragdoll cancel to escape the move's effect. * '''Hyper (Air Variant)''' stuns long enough to allow for a follow-up downslam, enabling potential triple downslam combos. * '''Blitz''' can hit opponents out of ragdoll state, useful for initiating moves like '''Hyper''' or '''Focus Shot''' on opponents who were previously grounded. * The '''Full Cowling''' passive allows for significant Ultimate bar gain upon kills, with minimal recoil damage taken by the player. * Both '''Hyper''' and '''Focus Shot''' deal high amounts of damage to players. * '''Hyper''' can be used as a prediction tool for predicting front dashes, due to it having super-armor. ===Cons=== * {{Green Hero}}'s [[Ultimate Mode|Awakening]], {{Maximum Overdrive}}, is predictable and easily countered by experienced opponents. * '''Hyper''' and '''Overkick''' have telegraphed startups, making them easy to predict and counter. * The reworked '''Overkick''' animation is highly predictable. * '''1,000,000%''' lacks invincibility frames (I-frames) during its startup, meaning the user can be interrupted, cancelling the move entirely and wasting the Ultimate activation for its duration. * '''Cosmic Smash''' is punishable if blocked. * '''OVERCHARGE''' is difficult to aim accurately, cannot hit grounded opponents and is vulnerable to interruption during its startup phase. ** '''OVERCHARGE''' also has an unreliable hitbox. * '''Overkick''''s prominent visual effects can obscure the player's view, potentially causing misses. * Successful combos often depend on baiting out the opponent's evasive maneuvers. A failed bait can leave the player vulnerable to counter-attack. * '''Focus Shot''', while unblockable close up, is blockable at farther distances. * The base moveset is very basic and does not have This was changed for balance reasons to the current series of zigzag movements ending in a teleport. * This character is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya], the main protagonist from the series [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki My Hero Academia], or MHA for short. * The base move names in-game used to be shortened versions of their full names from the source material (However, all the base moves had their names changed): * [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Detroit_Smash Detroit Smash] * [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Delaware_Smash Delaware Smash] * [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Manchester_Smash Manchester Smash] * [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Texas_Smash Texas Smash] (The move in-game resembles a flurry, perhaps referencing rapid attacks rather than the single punch 'Texas Smash'). * Originally, achieving mastery for this character was significantly harder, as defeating opponents only granted 0.34% [[mastery]]. * Using a finisher also inflicts minor damage on the user. This references Izuku Midoriya's early struggles controlling "One For All", where using its power at 100% would break his bones, such as [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IFkMMeYpw3w here]. * The pink lightning effect during the spawn animation references the 'Full Cowling' technique, which involves circulating "One For All" throughout the body, often depicted with similar visual cues [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=osmrqKIN2pg seen here]. * The name "1,000,000%" references Deku's climactic attack, "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tT7cacukPFY 1,000,000% Delaware Detroit Smash]," although the animation in-game differs from the source material. Notably, the '1,000,000%' power level in the anime/manga isn't literal but represents Deku pushing beyond his limits fueled by emotion. * The '''1,000,000%''' finisher animation appears inspired by All Might's "PLUS ULTRA" punch against [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Nomu_(U.S.J.) the Nomu (The Prototype)] at the U.S.J., as seen [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_ED6Jdzr9c8 here]. * '''OVERCHARGE''' seems visually inspired by the fight between Deku and Overhaul. * Deku's '''Overkick''' attack was significantly reworked; its previous version was a simpler spinning kick. * '''Green Hero's''' [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]] ability was previously named just '''Ultimate''' before being changed to {{Maximum Overdrive}}. * This was the first character known to have an infinite ultimate duration bug (now patched, documented in videos like [https://www.youtube.com/@smoothiedude5919 Smoothiedude's]). * Fun fact: 1,000,000% can bug out of the map (you will get teleported in the map after that). {{Characters}}

HBTV:
[[File:HBTV Logo.png|thumb|The logo of HBTV as seen in their episodes.]]'''Heroes Battlegrounds TV''', commonly abbreviated as '''HBTV''', is a dedicated video series that delivers exciting previews, teasers, and behind-the-scenes insights into what's coming next in the world of ''Heroes Battlegrounds''. Designed to keep the community engaged and up to date, each episode offers a sneak peek at upcoming features, newly added characters, gameplay improvements, balance changes, event announcements, and more.

The face of HBTV is none other than '''HBTV Reporter Cam''', who guides viewers through each update with energy and clarity. Episodes air weekly, giving fans something to look forward to regularly. Whether you're a casual player or a hardcore fan, HBTV is the go-to source for staying informed, hyped, and connected to the ''Heroes Battlegrounds'' universe. Also, every week, they show a sneak peek of what's coming in the next update or a few updates.

== Clip of the Week ==
Every week, the people who run HBTV pick their favourite clip (sent in through the Discord) and showcase it as the main '''Clip of the Week'''. This list will show who won Clip of the Week for each HBTV episode. If your clip gets chosen to be a '''Clip of the Week''', the clips you submit next <u>cannot</u> be chosen as the '''Clip of the Week''' for at least the next episode.

Congratulations to everyone who sent in clips! Your effort helps to keep the HBTV community alive and thriving. 

* '''HBTV #2''' - @officialmadderlive
* '''HBTV #3''' - @solerius1325
* '''HBTV #4''' - @cv91d
* '''HBTV #5''' - @diizaster
* '''HBTV #5.5''' - @calamity_marii
* '''HBTV #6''' - @technoflahcat

'''''Spoilers Ahead!'''''
'''Hero Slayer'''}} {{Quote|No matter what you want to accomplish, it is necessary to have conviction and desire.| - [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Chizome Akaguro] AKA Stain the Hero Killer to [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tomura_Shigaraki Tomura Shigaraki] as he insults Shigaraki for his childish nature.}} * {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Hero Slayer|image1= New Stain Icon.png|New Icon Hero killer icons.webp|Old Icon Slayer Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Norm slayer game.png|Base Stains.png|Awakening HeroSlayerAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name=Conviction|cost=Free|NameOfCharacter=Hero Slayer}} ==Description== {{Hero Slayer}} is the second character released in Heroes Battlegrounds, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Chizome Akaguro] (also known as Hero Killer Stain). He is the first villain character added to the game. {{Hero Slayer}} is relatively easy to learn but challenging to master, featuring a moveset capable of executing deadly combos despite its longer cooldowns. This character can be mastered to unlock {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}. Achieving {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} requires gaining [[mastery]]. Mastery points are earned by defeating opponents while playing as {{Hero Slayer}} (0.50%), completing quests from the Daily Quest button (4-5%), and defeating {{The Prototype}} (5-13%). ==Pros and Cons== ===Pros=== * '''Thousand Slashes''' or '''Triple Dagger Throw''' can substitute for a side dash if the M1 attack range is insufficient. * '''Curdle Pierce''' and '''Thousand Slashes''' can cancel a forward dash if timed correctly. * '''Blackout Clutch''' grants invincibility frames (I-frames), making it useful against multiple opponents (teamers). * The Ultimate Mode, '''{{Conviction}}''', is highly effective against teamers. * High combo potential, allowing for freestyle gameplay. * Beginner-friendly; relatively easy character to pick up initially. * Can stun an opponent upon the second activation of '''Curdle Pierce''' by applying the "Blood Curdle" status (requires landing the first hit). * '''Thousand Slashes''' and '''Triple Dagger Throw''' are effective combo extenders due to their speed and strong stun properties. * '''Blackout Clutch''' provides temporary safety against teamers, granting I-Frames upon landing the move. * '''Curdle Pierce''' is a strong tool for countering or initiating aggression due to its hyper armor. * '''Triple Dagger Throw''' can extend combos off '''Blackout Clutch''' when followed by a side dash. * Possesses one of the fastest Ultimate activation animations currently in the game. * Capable of dealing high damage with True Combos (approximately 45-60%). * '''{{Conviction}}''' ([[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]]) moves have very low cooldowns. * Can consistently extend combos after catching an opponent with an [[IWO|Interaction With Objects (IWO)]] attack (unless used after the fourth M1). * Capable of performing relatively easy One-Shot combos with proper timing. * Effective character against {{The Prototype}} boss due to high Ultimate move damage and the ability to gain ultimate charge with '''Indomitable Aura'''. * '''Crimson Parry''' has an insanely long counter window out of every counter in the game, making it incredibly easy to fall for if you are impatient. * '''Curdle Pierce''' is an effectively good combo starter. * Even if '''Curdle Pierce''' is missed, it doesn't have enough endlag to prevent you from blocking afterwards. ===Cons=== * May cause performance issues (lag) on lower-end devices. * Most moves are punishable if missed or blocked. * Difficult to fully master (player dependent). * Utilizing advanced techniques requires significant practice and precise timing. * The forward dash has a vulnerable hitbox for a few frames. * '''Curdle Pierce''' has significant endlag, often leading to punishment if missed. * A basic combo (Thousand Slashes > Triple Dagger Throw > Blackout Clutch) can deal around 50% damage but is predictable. * '''{{Conviction}}''' has no actual mobility moves to catch runners. * Attempting the '''Triple Dagger Throw''' wakeup It was then followed by its [[Mastered Hero Slayer|Mastered Version]] and {{Winged Assassin}}. * Can be mastered to unlock {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}. Mastery is gained by defeating players (0.50%), completing daily quests (4-5%) from the [[NPC's|Daily Quest Giver]], or defeating the Namu boss (up to 13%). * The Awakening music is titled ''[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4lBjqNZCUA0&ab_channel=HeroesBattlegrounds Crimson Justice]''. * The previous Ultimate music was [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IAkxHLUS7fI&ab_channel=N00BIX Mind of Evil]. * When '''Indomitable Will''' is activated, a short snippet of the old Ultimate music ("Mind Of Evil") plays. * '''Triple Dagger Throw''' has a faster startup animation when used immediately after ragdolling an opponent. * '''{{Hero Slayer}}'s''' Ultimate duration can be extended using '''Indomitable Will''', but not indefinitely. Each use of '''Indomitable Will''' increases the rate at which the Ultimate bar drains. * '''Triple Dagger Throw''' was the first move capable of activating its finisher on a ragdolled (defeated) opponent's body. * In the source material, Stain's [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Quirk Quirk], [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Bloodcurdle?so=search Bloodcurdle], allows him to paralyze opponents by ingesting their [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blood blood]. The paralysis duration depends on the victim's [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blood_type blood type] (Type B lasts longest, followed by AB, A, and O). For game balance, the paralysis duration in Heroes Battlegrounds is standardized. * '''Thousand Slashes''' and '''Curdle Pierce''' had the longest period without receiving finisher animations among offensive moves (excluding the utility move '''Ember Assault'''). * First character considered to have a "true" 100% damage combo achievable within the Ultimate state. * Using '''Triple Dagger Throw''' quickly after landing the third M1 (or potentially any M1) can cause the next M1 to reset to the third hit animation. This is likely an unintended bug and may be patched. * Currently, one of the only characters in the game where the passive can be obtained without performing a finisher. ** Similarly, it is also currently the only character where the passive is stackable. {{Characters}}

Heroes:
Heroes are characters based off of the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Heroes Heroes] from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki My Hero Academia] (pretty self-explanatory). Below are some key features of the heroes:

* [[Mastery]] quests often involve defeating specifically heroes to complete them.
* Certain world events such as the Halloween Event trick-or-treat doors have given specific unique animations to heroes.
* Heroes make up 50% of the roster on average (this changes every new character, but Heroes and [[Villains]] stay equal to each other one after another ''generally.)''

The list of Heroes Battlegrounds' current heroes include: 

* {{Green Hero}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku "Deku" Midoriya]
* [[Mastered Green Hero|Verdant Hope]] ({{Mastered Green Hero}}) based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku "Deku" Midoriya] during the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Hero_Arc Dark Hero Arc]
* {{Split Ice}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Shoto_Todoroki Shoto "Shoto" Todoroki]
* [[Mastered Split Ice|Scorching Winter]] ({{Mastered Split Ice}}) based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Shoto_Todoroki Shoto "Shoto" Todoroki], however the character's arc reference is unknown, potentially during the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia:_World_Heroes%27_Mission World Heroes' Mission (WHM) Arc] or [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Final_War_Arc The Final War Arc.]
* {{Explosion Hero}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Katsuki_Bakugo Katsuki "Great Explosion Murder God Dynamight" Bakugo]
* {{Full Throttle}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tenya_Ida Tenya “Ingenium” Iida]
* {{Winged Assassin}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Keigo_Takami Keigo “Hawks” Takami]
* {{Huge Lady}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Yu_Takeyama Yu "Mt. Lady" Takeyama] but is only accessible through [[Private Server Commands]]

== Trivia ==

* {{Huge Lady}} was the first Pro Hero added into Heroes Battlegrounds, and {{Winged Assassin}} goes as second.
Heroes Battlegrounds:
{{Heroes Battlegrounds|title1=Heroes Battlegrounds|image1=Jet Icon Game.webp|owner=[[more awesome games yo]]|date_created=April 10, 2023|server_size=15|genre=Action|sub-genre=Battlegrounds & Fighting|voice_chat=Supported|camera=Not Supported|visits=about 1B|favorites=about 1M}}'''''Heroes Battlegrounds''''' is a ROBLOX game that was created on April 10, 2023. The game was made by a group of people that go by the name of [[more awesome games yo]].

== Game Description ==
This update: NEW JET ENGINE ULT MOVES AND BASE MOVE FINISHERS! Big Balancing Update, Cosmetics QOL, Bug Fixes, and Optimization!

--

Fight to become the #1 Hero or Villain.

🎮 This experience works on all devices (CONSOLE, PC, MOBILE).

❤️ Please consider liking and supporting this experience to speed up our updates!

💪 Fight bosses, and unlock mastered versions of characters to become the strongest hero or villain.

a Battleground experience

== Controls ==
Main page: Controls

* G - [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]]
* F - Block
* Q - [[Dash]]
* Q (while Ragdolled) - [[Techniques|Recovery Dash]]/Evasive
* Left Click - Punch
* Jump + Left Click - Downslam
* B - [[Emotes|Emote]] Menu

== Gameplay ==
Heroes Battlegrounds has many characters available to play and some are special characters. You are able to select your character using the character selection menu. Some of the character abilities are long-ranged and some are short-ranged. When you kill someone you will gain some health from it. Players can emote by pressing B and selecting a emote.

== Other ==
Heroes Battlegrounds also has some other features within the game, such as [[IWO|Interaction With Objects]], the [[Emotes|Emote Page]], and [[Ultimate Mode]].

Huge Lady:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Huge Lady}} {{Stub}} {{Quote|"Hey everyone, hope you don't mind if I...BUTT in!"|Yu Takeyama in her first appearance.|Hello=}} {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Huge lady|image1= File:MT.webp|thumb|Icon File:IMG 0521.png|Base File:Mttttt.png|Awakening |cost=499 robux (PS+)|NameOfCharacter=Huge Lady|awakening_name=Rumbling|caption-image1=}} == Description == {{Huge Lady}} is a character in Heroes Battlegrounds based on the Pro Hero [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Yu_Takeyama Mt. Lady] (Yu Takeyama) from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga)?so=search My Hero Academia]. This character is exclusive to [[Gamepasses|Private Servers]] that have the Private Server+ (PS+) gamepass active. Huge Lady's core gimmick revolves around her ability to drastically increase her size using her [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Gigantification "Gigantification" quirk.] Starting at a normal size, the user can activate '''Transform''' to grow larger, unlocking new moves and altering existing ones. The move '''Growing By The Day''' allows for continuous size increase with no apparent limit, significantly boosting the damage and AoE of her attacks, particularly '''Stomp''' and her M1s, at the cost of attack speed and mobility. Her ultimate, '''Rumbling''', unleashes a devastating, map-wide attack. == Pros and Cons == ====Pros:==== * Deals immense damage, especially at larger sizes. * M1 damage can be enhanced with '''Growing by the Day''' to the point where they can one-shot opponents. * The damage and range of '''Stomp''' increase significantly as the user grows larger. ====Cons:==== * Exclusive to Private Servers with the PS+ gamepass. * The '''Rumbling''' ultimate can be dodged by constantly moving and waiting out its duration. * If {{The Prototype}} boss spawns while Huge Lady is active, the user will be forcibly transformed into either {{Green Hero}} or {{Mastered Green Hero}} * Almost no traditional combo potential; the primary "combo" is a simple M1 chain into '''Stomp'''. * The '''Rumbling''' ultimate may cause the user to experience glitches or become stuck. * Possesses only one primary damaging move ('''Stomp''') outside of M1s in her transformed state. * The forward dash does not have an attack/punch component. * Mobility is severely limited while transformed, as standard front, side, and back dashes have no effect or are significantly hindered. ==Moveset== {| class="fandom-table wikitable" style="text-align:center" !Moves !Damage !Cooldown !Information |- |[[File:Mt M1.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Giant Punches (M1)''' |Base: 23% (full chain) |2-3 seconds |Standard punches. The fourth M1 hit creates a small shockwave dealing an additional 4% damage. |- |[[File:Transformed M1s.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Seismic Smash (Transformed M1)''' |10% per hit (scales with size) |TBA (Attack speed decreases with size) |A slow, powerful punch to the floor that creates huge cracks in the ground. Damage and AoE increase with size. (Only available after using '''Transform''') |- |[[File:Transform_.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Transform''' |0% |4 sec |The user of the "Gigantification" quirk bites their arm and grows significantly larger, unlocking the '''Stomp''' and '''Growing By The Day''' abilities, and changing M1s to '''Seismic Smash'''. ----- *Cannot revert to normal size unless the user resets their character. |- |[[File:Stomp.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Stomp''' |Base: 23% (scales with size) |5 sec |The user stomps their foot on the ground, creating cracks similar to those from '''Seismic Smash'''. Damage and AoE increase with size. (Only available after using '''Transform''') |- |[[File:Growing By The Day.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Growing By The Day''' |0% |7 sec |The user grows even larger after a golden flash engulfs the screen. This increases the size, AoE, and damage of '''Seismic Smash''' (M1s) and '''Stomp'''. There is no known limit to the size increase. M1 attack speed decreases as size increases. (Only available after using '''Transform''') |- |'''Rumbling''' (Ultimate) |One-shot kill |N/A (Ultimate Charge) |Numerous "Weakest Dummies" (with old Roblox logo faces) spawn and slowly converge towards the center of the arena. Any player touched by a dummy is instantly defeated. The user of this move is unaffected. ----- *Known ways to avoid this include using flight commands (if available) or abilities that provide extended invulnerability or out-of-bounds states. Other methods may exist. |} ==Trivia/Notes== * The ultimate ability, '''Rumbling''', and the '''Transform''' move (specifically the act of biting the arm to transform) are visual and thematic references to the popular anime and manga series [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attack_on_Titan_(TV_series) Attack on Titan], particularly the Titan transformation process and the "Rumbling" event. * This character requires the [[Gamepasses|Private Server Plus (PS+)]] gamepass to be unlocked and can only be used within private servers where this gamepass is active. * When transformed, Huge Lady's movement speed is significantly reduced, and standard dash mechanics are disabled or ineffective, emphasizing her colossal but cumbersome nature. * Although this character is meant to be based off of Mt. Lady, the moveset itself seems to be based off of the Titans in the series [https://attackontitan.fandom.com/wiki/Attack_on_Titan_Wiki Attack on Titan], specifically [https://attackontitan.fandom.com/wiki/Eren_Jaeger_(Anime) Eren Jaeger]. {{Characters}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Special Characters]] [[Category:Private Server+ Characters]]

Human Architect:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Human Architect'''}} {{Quote|'''There are so many capable men, and all they can think to do is rob a convenience store? Kind of weird, you'd think a group this big might just, y'know... have bigger goals than that. All of you are sick, and need a cure.'''|Kai Chisaki to Team Reservoir Dogs.}} * * {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Human Architect|image1= New Overhaul Icon.png|New Icon BirdMan.webp|Old Icon HumanSpawn.gif|Spawn Animation Base Human.png|Base HumanAwak.png|Awakening HumanAura.gif|Awakening Aura HumanAwakWalk.gif|Unique Awakening Walk OverhaulAwak.png|Second Awakening FusionAura.gif|Second Awakening Aura FusionWalk.gif|Second Unique Awakening Walk |awakening_name='''Flesh Chimera'''|cost=Free|caption-image1=Ultra blue flames|NameOfCharacter={{Human Architect}}}} ==Description== {{Human Architect}} is the twelfth character released in Heroes Battlegrounds, based on the antagonist '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kai_Chisaki Kai Chisaki]''' (Overhaul) from '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga) My Hero Academia.]''' His core gimmick is a highly adaptive and versatile moveset centered around the '''Conjure''' ability. Using other base moves ('''Renovation''', '''Dirty Work''', '''Stone Weaponry''') before '''Conjure''' changes its properties, offering different offensive and combo-extending options. This rewards strategic sequencing of abilities. Furthermore, Human Architect possesses a unique, multi-stage awakening. The first stage, '''Flesh Chimera''', enhances his abilities. By meeting specific conditions within this form, the user can then activate '''Final Fusion''', a second awakening that temporarily transforms them into a giant, Kaiju-like monster with immense power, before transitioning into a final, powered-up humanoid form with a devastating new moveset. ==Pros and Cons== ==='''Pros''' === * Offer an excellent combo potential, with most moves flowing seamlessly into one another, especially with the different variants of '''Conjure'''. * '''Renovation''' disables a random move from the opponent's own moveset, creating a significant disadvantage for them in the battle. * The pillar created by '''Dirty Work''' can be used for an [[IWO|I.W.O.]] interaction. * '''Dirty Work''''s endlag can be oddly deceptive, allowing for an easy block if the opponent punishes slightly late. * '''Conjure''' (after '''Renovation''') is an easy follow-up to the said move. * '''Conjure''' (after '''Dirty Work''') has one of the highest finisher execution thresholds for a base move, finishing at 20% health. * Using '''Conjure''' after '''Stone Weaponry''' offers a seamless and deadly combo. * '''Calamity''' has a fast startup, and can be followed up with '''Creation Field''' seamlessly. * During the '''Kaiju''' form (achieved via '''Final Fusion'''), the user is seemingly invulnerable to most attacks, with {{Split Ice}}'s '''Blazing Soul and''' {{Decaying Hatred}}'s '''Reflective Counter''' being an exception. This, along with the powerful moves in the final awakened state, makes the character's ultimate state exceptionally strong. * Doubtlessly, '''Kaiju''' form is the character against teamers, since use of '''Final Fusion''', and then '''CREATE''', can provide invulnerability (if not counting moves that make you attack unwillingly, e.g. '''BUTCHER''') for a really long time. * The '''Kaiju''' form lasts an entire ultimate, which means that if someone uses ultimate while you use '''Final Fusion,''' by the time it finishes, their ultimate will have likely run out. * '''BUTCHER''' (Aerial Variant) can be used to catch an opponent from a distance. * '''RUPTURE''' covers a massive amount of range in front of the user. ==='''Cons''' === * His base moveset possesses below-average damage by itself, relying more on creative combos to deal maximum damage. * '''Stone Weaponry''' has a very low finisher execution threshold (8%). * '''Conjure''' (Neutral Variant) has extreme endlag if it is blocked or misses. * The landing shockwave from '''Dirty Work''' can sometimes be easily countered by parry moves. * The pillar created by '''Dirty Work''' deals reduced damage as an I.W.O. compared to standard map pillars. * '''Disfiguration''''s finisher does not pause the ultimate bar, potentially wasting time for the activation of '''Final Fusion'''. * Activating the '''Kaiju''' form requires meeting two conditions: filling the '''DEATH''' gauge (a secondary ultimate meter) and then landing a finisher on another player, which can be difficult to set up. Using a Weakest Dummy may not grant the necessary finisher credit. ==Movesets== |-|Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background: #681C23;" |'''Moves''' ! style="background: #681C23;" |'''Damage''' ! style="background: #681C23;" |'''Cooldown''' ! style="background: #681C23;" |'''Information''' |- |[[File:Human_M1.gif|center|frameless|border|175px]]'''Roundhouse Spikes (M1)''' |16% total |~0.5 sec |The user punches with their right fist, knees the opponent's stomach, follows with a roundhouse kick, and concludes by summoning ground spikes that launch the opponent. |- |[[File:Renovation.gif|center|frameless|border|175px]]'''Renovation''' |11% |20 seconds |The user punches their opponent in the gut, spins, kicks them from behind, then shoots them in the back of the neck with a Quirk-disabling pistol. ---- * Disables a random move on cooldown from the victim, similar to Radio Blast. * Has endlag if blocked or missed. |- |[[File:DirtyWork.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Dirty Work''' |11% |19 seconds |The user of the "Overhaul" quirk summons a rock pillar from the ground, then after a delay, jumps off it and smashes their fist into the ground, knocking nearby opponents off their feet. ---- * The rock pillar can remain on the map temporarily and be used for [[IWO|I.W.O.]] interactions. * Small endlag. |- |[[File:StoneWeaponry.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Stone Weaponry''' |6% |27 seconds |The user stomps to forge a stone dagger, lunges forward with a downward slice, then an upward stab that launches the opponent.
