If any player types "yo angelo" in chat in front of the rock statue before it despawns, one of two images of the character Angelo from [https://jojowiki.com/Diamond_is_Unbreakable JoJo's Bizarre Adventure Part 4: Diamond is Unbreakable] will flash on their screen, accompanied by a sound effect similar to [https://onepiece.fandom.com/wiki/Shanks Shanks'] [https://onepiece.fandom.com/wiki/Haki/Supreme_King_Haki Conqueror's Haki] from One Piece. * The pillar created by '''Dirty Work''' can be destroyed by certain moves from other characters, such as {{Azure Flames}} Infernal Burn. * This character shares the same Aerial Slam gimmick as {{Explosion Hero}}, where landing the slam causes the user to bounce up and backward slightly. * During development, '''Stone Weaponry''' was named '''Odious Reconstruction'''. * '''Stone Weaponry'''’s finisher exhibits weird behaviour when viewed with disabled cutscenes, as the target teleports to the user, instead of the other way around. Along with the user disappearing upon landing the finisher * The spike in '''Conjure (Neutral)'''’s Finisher is abruptly vaporised after the animation ends. * '''Renovation's''' finisher animation appears to be a direct reference to the scene where '''Overhaul''' [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kai_Chisaki/Image_Gallery?file=Magne%27s_Death.png defeats] '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kenji_Hikiishi Magne]''' in the source material. * '''Renovation''' originally had a variant that was later removed. * Certain map objects, particularly trees, can lower the height of the pillar from '''Dirty Work''', causing the user to jump off it faster and cover less distance.. * The ultimate moves '''Calamity''' and '''Creation Field''' were formerly named '''Calamitous Rushdown''' and '''Disgusted Dismay''', respectively. * The character's neutral fourth M1 hit has noticeably more range compared to other characters. * Human Architect is the second character to feature a dual/super-awakening, the first being {{Explosion Hero}}. However, Human Architect is unique for having the intermediate '''Kaiju Mode''' transformation. ** This technically makes Human Architect the character with the most unique attacks, totaling 20 (including M1s). * The '''Calamity''' finisher is the second ultimate finisher in the game to feature a Point-of-View switch to the opponent, the first being Crimson Justice's [[Mastered Hero Slayer#Finishers|'''Detrimental Division''' finisher.]] If including base moves, it would be the third, after Detrimental Division and the '''Conjure''' neutral finisher). * The Kaiju's roar during the last M1 of '''Monstrous Lordship''' might be a reference to '''[https://godofwar.fandom.com/wiki/Jörmungandr Jörmungandr's]''' roar during his fight with [https://godofwar.fandom.com/wiki/Thor Thor] in the game [https://godofwar.fandom.com/wiki/God_of_War_Ragnar%C3%B6k ''God of War: Ragnarök''.] * '''RUPTURE''' is the second move that requires the player to complete a QTE to deal more damage, the first one being {{Decaying Hatred}}'s '''TRUE EVIL'''. * As of today, there's a high chance that '''RUPTURE''' does not work properly. The move hits as normal, but the cutscene plays without the pedestrian, the opponent, and the QTE, with only the user present. Meaning the cutscene would be completely silent for the entirety of its duration. ** This is due to be fixed in a later update. {{Characters}} [[Category:Characters]]

IWO:
Interaction With Objects (I.W.O.) is a game mechanic in '''''<u>Heroes Battlegrounds!</u>'''''

You are able to interact with objects if there are any enemies nearby. You can interact with objects such as the [[Structures and Objects|trash cans]], [[Structures and Objects|trees]], [[Structures and Objects|pillars]] and so on.

==Trivia/Notes==
*The base I.W.O animations are the same for all characters
**They just have same animations with different VFX (except for {{Hero Slayer}} which have two entirely different animations)
*I.W.O's are unblockable
*Finishing with a I.W.O gives way more ultimate than usual.
**This also applies with {{Green Hero}}'s passive, stacking it.
***This can be useful if farming ultimate.
*I.W.O's seem to have no cooldowns, making it possible to put someone in at most 3 I.W.O's.
*One of the best technique to combo-extend.
Mastered Azure Flames:
[[File:NoFilter.jpg|thumb|498x498px]]
'''''Spoilers! Spoilers'''''
[https://blox-fruits.fandom.com/wiki/User:CRY0Mandoid coding credits]
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Mastered Azure FlamesMastered Azure Flames}} {{Quote|''''' "Hey now, don't be rude. You're acting like we've never met! Call me Toya. After all, it's the name you and mom gave me. Yeah, I know, my face is pretty disgusting now. But it's still surprising my own family didn't recognize me. It's honestly kinda crappy, or maybe it's just depressing, you know?|2=- [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dabi Dabi] revealing his true identity to Endeavor and Shoto.}} * * {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Mastered Azure Flames|image1= New Dabi V2 Icon.png|New Icon Mastered Azure Flames Icon.png|Old Icon New MASTERED Azure Flames spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Better Dabi Mastery.png|Base RetributionAwkAura.png|Awakening blueflamemastery.webp|Discord Sneak Peak |awakening_name=Retribution|cost=100% Mastery of Azure Flames|caption-image1=Ultra blue flames|NameOfCharacter={{Mastered Azure Flames}}}} ==Description== {{Mastered Azure Flames}}, also known as '''''Violet Inferno''''' by its '''[[Badges|badge name]]''', is the 11th character released in Heroes Battlegrounds (13th including {{Huge Lady}} and {{The Strongest}}). This character is unlocked by achieving 100% '''[[Mastery|mastery]]''' with the base {{Azure Flames}}, based on '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dabi Toya Todoroki]''' during the '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Final_War_Arc Final War Arc]''', {{Mastered Azure Flames}} embodies Dabi's escalating power and deteriorating mental state. His core gimmick revolves around '''two distinct combat forms: Azure Form and Violet Form'''. The user starts in Azure Form and can transition into the more volatile Violet Form using '''Scorching Surge'''. Violet Form enhances abilities and reduces cooldowns but constantly drains the user's health, referencing Dabi's inability to fully control his intense flames without self-harm. Successfully landing finishers, especially in Violet Form, can grant significant health regeneration, creating a high-risk, high-reward playstyle. This character also features unique passives, such as gaining {{Split Ice}}'s Ice Javelin after a specific finisher ('''Glacial Cinder'''). ==Pros and Cons== ==='''Pros''' === * Amazing Combo Potential due to two distinct movesets and their interactions. * Possesses two movesets ('''Azure Form''' and '''Violet Form'''), offering versatility. * Moves generally have long animations, which can be strategically used to stall and waste an opponent's awakening bar if they are caught in a combo. * Features very low cooldowns, which decrease further while in Violet Form. * '''Cinder Bind''' (Violet Form) has a huge Area of Effect (AoE), excellent for slowing down multiple opponents (teamers) and deterring third-party interference. * '''Cinder Bind''' is difficult to punish even if missed or blocked due to its lingering hitbox that persists until the fire circle disappears. Attempting to M1 trade with the user before the final burst can result in being stunned, creating an opening. * The user can hide within the fire circle created by '''Cinder Bind''' to regenerate health (if passives allow) and wait for cooldowns, as opponents can still be hit by the lingering effect if they enter or were not hit by the initial cast. * Most moves grant invincibility frames (I-frames), making {{Mastered Azure Flames}} a good candidate against teamers or third-parties. * Moves have very high finisher execution ranges, along with great general range and mobility. * Cooldowns recover 20% faster while in Violet Form. * Opponents near the user's moves, such as '''Glacial Cinder''', can also be hit, ragdolled, or launched by the collateral effects. * Depending how many HP the player has, landing a finisher while in '''Violet Form''' ('''Hell Assault,''' '''Phantom Shot''' and the 2nd usage of '''Scorching Surge''') grants more HP regeneration on kill compared to Azure Form kills, making it excellent for clutching situations at low HP, especially against teamers. * Landing the '''Glacial Cinder''' finisher grants the user an '''Ice Javelin''' for their M1s, identical to the one used by {{Split Ice}} and {{Mastered Split Ice}} (including its passive properties if applicable). The same goes for **Hell Shot**, which grants the user the **Frigid Scythe.** * By switching back between '''Violet Form''' and '''Azure Form''' it is possible to use the moves in each form to combo into each other. * Possess one of the most destructive Awakenings. * '''Retribution''' possesses immense damage in his ultimate. (300 HP) * '''Hell-Scorched Execution''' can be confirmed with a Downslam or 3 M1's, making it very deadly. * '''Hellfire Bloom''' has a lot of range (The entire map) making it very easy to hit. * '''Inferno Spider''' can be confirmed by landing '''Cluster Spark'''. * All of the moves in the ultimate have I-frames or Hyperarmor, meaning you can effectively use all the moves raw without being punished. ==='''Cons''' === * Low damage on individual moves without incorporating M1s and chaining skills, compared to other characters. * '''Violet Form''' generally deals less overall burst damage per move than '''Azure Form''', trading it for utility and faster cooldowns. * Can be harder to use than base {{Azure Flames}} due to managing two different movesets. While technically possible, seamlessly extending combos while switching forms is difficult (more feasible switching from '''Violet''' to '''Azure Form'''). * The [[Ultimate Mode|ultimate]] gauge takes longer to fill compared to its non-Mastery counterpart. * '''Violet Form''' constantly drains HP (approximately 0.8% per second), making natural health regeneration impossible without securing a kill. If health isn't regained, the user automatically reverts to '''Azure Form''' with full HP after about 5 minutes. * Cooldowns are shared between both '''Azure''' and '''Violet''' forms (unlike {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}'s stance switch), requiring careful timing and reliance on low cooldowns to chain moves effectively. * '''Cinder Bind''' doesn't bypass blocking, which can be an issue against skilled opponents or coordinated teamers who block effectively. * Unlike '''Phantom Shot''', '''Hell Shot''' (Azure Form) can be escaped easily due to its longer startup animation. * Attempting to combo extend after '''Hell Shot's (Second Use)''' by turning the camera away from the opponent is high risk because of the endlag unless the opponent has high ping (250 or higher). * Heavily relies on the enemy wasting their evasive maneuver before being able to consistently deal significant damage. * '''Violet Form''' is very risky to use due to the immense life drain when you are in the form, making combos more deadly. ==Movesets== |-|Azure Form= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background:linear-gradient(90deg, #00868B, #00868B, #7EF4CC, #00868B, #00868B);" {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Mastered Green HeroMastered Green Hero'''}} {{Quote|"It's not all black-and-white. Most things in this world are in shades of gray. A blend of fear and anger. Which is exactly why... I've gotta extend a helping hand!"|- [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya] AKA Deku to [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kaina_Tsutsumi Kaina Tsutsumi] AKA Lady Nagant during his [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Hero_Arc Dark Hero Arc].||}} * {{Character_heros|GreenHero='''Mastered Green HeroMastered Green Hero'''|image1= New Deku V2 Icon.png|New Icon Mast hero green.webp|Old Icon Vigilante Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Green master.png|Base DEKUomaewa.png|The Ninth GreenHeroMasAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name='''The Ninth'''|Cost=Free (Needs Mastery to 100%)|caption-Herogreen.png=The mastered version of Green hero.|NameOfCharacter={{Mastered Green Hero}}|cost=100% Mastery for Green Hero|caption-image1=The Vigilante Version.|Original Name=Izuku Midoriya}} ==Description== {{Mastered Green Hero}}, also known as '''''Verdant Hope''''' by its '''[[Badges|badge name]]''', is an unlockable character in Heroes Battlegrounds, obtained by achieving 100% '''[[Mastery|mastery]]''' with {{Green Hero}}. Mastery can be gained by defeating players, defeating {{The Prototype}} boss, or completing daily quests. He also is the 5th character added to the game. {{Mastered Green Hero}}''''s''' moveset is designed around close-combat attacks that deal massive damage by locking enemies into deadly combos, and the character itself is based on '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya]'s''' appearance and abilities during Season 6 of the anime, which corresponds to the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Hero_Arc Dark Hero Arc] in the '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga) My Hero Academia]''' manga. ==Pros and Cons== ===Pros=== * All moves can hit or grab ragdolled enemies. * Most moves are grabs, providing extremely safe damage opportunities. * '''Shadow Whip''' has two variants. ** '''Shadow Whip's''' ground variant is an effective combo starter and extender. * '''Quintuple Smash''' can be combined with any move except '''Vicious Grab''' (unless Side Dash + Aerial Variant of Vicious Grab) for easy damage. * '''Vicious Grab's''' aerial variant is good for avoiding damage from [[Ultimate Mode|Awakening users]] and escaping dangerous situations. It also has a shorter cooldown than its ground counterpart. * '''Electric Smash''' can punish opponents who consistently hold block, as well as '''Quintuple Smash's''' last punch. * '''Vicious Grab''' is a useful combo finisher due to the distance it creates between the user and the opponent. ** This is primarily because it is difficult to extend a combo after landing it. * '''Quintuple Smash''' functions as both a combo starter and extender. * '''Electric Smash''' is effective against teamers and incoming third-party attackers. * Every base and ultimate move has a finisher animation. * Three of the four base moves knock back the opponent, which is useful for positioning them into an [[IWO|I.W.O.]] interaction. * The Awakening, namely '''The Ninth''', offers some surprising combo potential, despite how the moves work. * '''The Ninth''' has two abilities with long range, with '''Lightning Crush''' having the furthest reach. * '''Quintuple Smash''' is a high-damage combo extender when used after a front dash. * '''The Ninth''' ultimate offers more combo potential than Green Hero's Awakening, [[Green Hero#Maximum Overdrive|Maximum Overdrive]], though it sacrifices some raw damage output. * '''The Ninth''' Awakening moves chain together seamlessly. * '''Vicious Grab''' (both variants) are useful for knocking opponents back and dealing with teamers or third-party attackers. * Jumping before using '''Electric Smash''' can increase its damage output. * Seven out of eight moves (including those in '''The Ninth''' Ultimate) are unblockable. (For '''Quintuple Smash''', rapidly turning the camera can help bypass the block, as well as the final hit doing the same work). * '''Quintuple Smash''' can reportedly grab an infinite number of people (needs further verification; confirmed for up to 15). * '''Whiplash''' has a lingering hitbox that can hit enemies even after the initial contact. * Can approximately deal 5% damage to {{The Prototype|The Prototype}} boss if all moves land without interruption. * '''Vicious Grab (Aerial Variant)''' is excellent for dodging ultimates and confusing players and can be used to increase the damage output of '''Electric Smash'''. * '''Vicious Grab''' can be used as a spacing move; this makes it effective against teamers. * '''Lightning Crush''' can combo into '''Whiplash, 120% or 45%''' if executed correctly. * '''Vicious Grab''' can be used to hit moves mid air (such as '''Electric Smash''' and '''Shadow Whip''') ** It can also be used to attack opponents midair that were launched by moves like '''Focus Shot (Aerial Variant)''' and against {{Winged Assassin}} players. * '''Vicious Grab (Aerial Variant)''' can be a good setup for unexpected attacks from the air. * The reworked passive gives the user Danger Sense (or, more specifically, 3 dodges), making it useful against teamers. If Burst is used, the user gains 5 dodges instead of 3 dodges, making this character ideal for teamers and third partiers. ===Cons=== * All base moves have very long cooldowns, except for '''Vicious Grab (Aerial Variant)'''. * '''The Ninth''' is easily punishable when moves are used recklessly, so it's the best to fight with the Awakening used in a close-range like all players normally do. * '''The Ninth''' heavily relies on the enemy having wasted their evasive and successfully chaining moves together for optimal damage. * '''The Ninth''' Awakening takes significantly longer to fill its gauge compared to {{Green Hero|Green Hero's}} '''Maximum Overdrive'''. ** The reason of it is that {{Green Hero}} has a special passive for filling the gauge. * Acquiring 100% mastery for {{Green Hero}} can be time-consuming, even with consistent {{The Prototype|Prototype}} farming. * '''Electric Smash''' can be punishable if missed, though not as severely as Hyper (Air Variant) or Overkick from the base Green Hero. * '''Shadow Whip''' has considerable endlag if missed, and even more if it is blocked. * '''Shadow Whip's''' neutral (ground) variant pushes players extremely far away, which can sometimes be a disadvantage. * '''Vicious Grab''' is easily punishable. * The passive ability (Teleport Dashes) is not consistently useful, as it is instant upon activation and can sometimes make landing subsequent attacks difficult. * Awakening moves can be dodged or missed easily if not set up properly. * [[Green Hero|Green Hero's]] [[Green Hero#Maximum Overdrive|Awakening]] deals more total damage than the mastered version's own Awakening (The Ninth) (180% & 117%). * Base moves have longer cooldowns compared to their non-mastered version, {{Green Hero}}. * The reworked passive can be easily wasted by certain moves. ==Movesets== |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! Slightly faster and more fluid animation. |- | '''Close-Range Smash''' | '''Hyper:''' Powerful single punch with hyper armor. | '''Quintuple Smash:''' Rapid multi-hit punch combo. |- | '''Ranged Attack''' | '''Focus Shot:''' Finger flick air blasts. | '''Vicious Grab:''' Multi-hit grab ending in a air blast. |- | '''Aerial Attack''' | '''Overdrive:''' Leaping spinning kick, provides hyper armor and some repositioning. | '''Electric Smash:''' Ground pound kick, damage scales with height. |} ==Trivia/Notes== * Possible references for the base moveset include '''Quintuple Smash''' ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Detroit_Smash:_Quintuple Detroit Smash: Quintuple]), '''Electric Smash''' ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Manchester_Smash?so=search Manchester Smash]), '''Vicious Grab''' ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Delaware_Smash Delaware Smash]), '''Vicious Grab (Aerial Variant)''' ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Delaware_Smash_Air_Force Delaware Smash Air Force]), and both variants of '''Shadow Whip''' ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Blackwhip Blackwhip]). Note that some of these are not direct one-to-one recreations but rather custom animations inspired by these referenced abilities. * The spawn animation is a reference to Deku's characteristic walk during his vigilante phase (Dark Hero Arc), as seen [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vWZ1do0dRwA here]. * '''Vicious Grab's''' aerial variant is generally more advantageous than its ground variant due to a 2.5x faster cooldown and only 1% less base damage. It is also less punishable on a miss. However, for finishing an opponent, the ground variant's higher finisher threshold (13% vs. 9%) makes it preferable. There is a small window where the user can still be hit before the aerial variant fully propels them upwards. * In the '''120%''' finisher animation, the words "second" and "third" can be seen, referencing the "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Gearshift Gearshift]" quirk that Izuku Midoriya utilizes against [[Decaying Hatred|Shigaraki]] in Season 7, Episode 12 of the anime ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chapter_366 Chapter 366 of the manga]), as shown [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R1U-ecSAqoQ here] (relevant portion ends around the 49-second mark). * The '''Lightning Crush''' move features red chains, based on the "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Blackchain Blackchain]" attack that Deku uses by combining "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Fa_Jin Fa Jin]" and "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Blackwhip Blackwhip]". * The '''45%''' ultimate move is likely based on or inspired by Deku's rematch against Muscular ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya_vs._Goto_Imasuji:_Rematch]), where he defeated him instantly with a powerful punch ([https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/File:Izuku_defeats_Muscular_once_more.gif One-Shot Punch]). * '''Shadow Whip''' was originally named "Dark Whip," and '''Electric Smash''' was originally named "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/St._Louis_Smash St. Louis Smash]." * Although this character is based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Izuku_Midoriya Izuku Midoriya], who utilizes multiple quirks from "One for All," the base moveset only incorporates two (Delaware Smash/Air Force and Blackwhip), while the ultimate moveset showcases up to five. * During '''The Ninth's''' awakening animation, only six figures (representing past users) appear behind the player from their friends list, instead of the canonical [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/One_For_All eight past users of One For All]. * In the source material, Izuku Midoriya, during his Dark Hero Arc utilizes the "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Danger_Sense Danger Sense]" quirk, making him nearly untouchable. This is not directly represented in {{Mastered Green Hero}}'s moveset, although during '''The Ninth''' ultimate activation, all players are highlighted with a white circle, negating stealth. * {{Mastered Green Hero}} also possesses the "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Smokescreen Smokescreen]" Quirk in the source material, which is shown in their '''Burst''' ability. * The '''45%''' finisher animation MAY be based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Detroit_Smash:_Quintuple Detroit Smash: Quintuple] or the finisher for '''Shadow Whip'''. * {{Mastered Green Hero}} is believed to utilize the "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Float Float]" Quirk during the '''Shadow Whip''' finisher, as they hover for a few seconds. * The move named '''45%''' does not actually deal 45% damage, which might confuse new players. * The theme song for {{Mastered Green Hero}}'s awakening, '''The Ninth''', is "Verdant Hope." It can be found [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=00cJOHvRvRM here]. * In the '''Lightning Crush''' finisher, the user appears to use 'Float' to keep the debris suspended in the air. * {{Mastered Green Hero}} previously shared the same '''Ultimate''' ({{Maximum Overdrive}}) with {{Green Hero}}. In Update 8.5, {{Mastered Green Hero}} received a personalized '''Ultimate''', '''The Ninth'''. * '''Shadow Whip's''' neutral (ground) variant used to have knockback similar to '''Quintuple Smash'''. The knockback was increased when '''Shadow Whip''' received its ragdolled-target variant. * The '''Lightning Crush''' finisher incorporates three of the quirks within "One for All": an initial reference to Deku's "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GQzTNsBHTsY Faux 100% Overdrive]" (using "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Blackwhip Blackwhip]" and "[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Fa_Jin Fa Jin]" together), and "Float" is used in the after-attack animation while manipulating debris. * {{Mastered Green Hero}} is the first "Mastered Hero" character which was later preceded by {{Mastered Split Ice}}, while {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} and {{Mastered Azure Flames}} are "Mastered Villain" characters. * {{Mastered Green Hero}} was the first character (and mastered version) to have the most finishers in the game at the time of its full release, with a total of nine (four for the base moveset, five if counting the air variant of '''Vicious Grab''', and four for the ultimate moveset), surpassing {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} at that point. * '''Whiplash''' is the only one ultimate move in the game, and its cooldown resets after finishing someone with a finisher in ultimate. * After '''The Ninth's''' awakening animation, the user can actually see everyone on the screen. {{Characters}}

[[File:Maxreal.jpg|thumb|1280x1280px]]
'''''Spoilers Ahead!''''' '''''Spoilers for Hero Killer: Stain'''''
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Mastered Hero SlayerMastered Hero Slayer'''}} [[Category:Characters]] {{Character_heros|image1= New Stain V2.png|New Icon Master killer.webp|Old Icon Mastered Slayer Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Mastttt slayer .png|Base MHSUlt.png|Judgement HeroSlayerMasAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name='''Judgement'''|GreenHero='''Mastered Hero Slayer'''|cost=100% Mastery for Hero Slayer|NameOfCharacter=Mastered Hero Slayer|caption-image1=The hero killer, Stain.}} ==Description== {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}, also known as '''''Crimson Justice''''' by its [[Badges|'''badge name''',]] is an unlockable character in [[Heroes Battlegrounds]], obtained by achieving 100% '''[[Mastery|mastery]]''' with '''{{Hero Slayer}}'''. Mastery can be gained by completing '''daily quests''', defeating the '''{{The Prototype}}''' boss, or accumulating kills (0.5% per kill, requiring 200 kills total without other sources). Released on June 21st, 2024, he is the 8th character added to the game. {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} represents a more potent and versatile version of his base counterpart. His core gimmick revolves around '''stance switching''' between a Katana-focused moveset and a Fists-based moveset, triggered by the '''Katana Throw''' ability. This dynamic allows for varied combo routes and adaptations mid-fight. Furthermore, landing specific finishers grants temporary weapon passives (Knife, Knuckledusters, or both), which alter his M1 attacks and damage, rewarding skillful execution and diverse combo completions. His awakening, {{Judgement}}, is distinct from that of the base {{Hero Slayer}}. ==Pros & Cons== === Pros=== * Possesses amazing [[Combos|combo]] potential due to stance switching and diverse move interactions. * Certain finishers grant temporary weapon passives (Knife, Knuckledusters), increasing M1 damage and altering M1 animations. * '''Dagger Cascade''' has a second use, allowing for more daggers to be thrown compared to the base Hero Slayer's Triple Dagger Throw. * '''Shadow Rush's''' air variant is an easy combo starter if landed correctly. * '''Whirlwind Pierce''' deals extra damage if timed to hit an opponent during their wakeup invincibility. * Has access to a defensive Armor skill ('''Whirlwind Pierce''') that is available in both Katana and Fist stances. * Can access two long-ranged moves in his base (Katana) moveset. * The follow-up to '''Shadow Rush''' can lead into either '''Katana Throw''' (stance switch) or an Attack Dash for continued pressure. * If an opponent lacks ragdoll cancel, using '''Whirlwind Pierce''' immediately after '''Headbutt''' guarantees either the stun or the bonus wakeup damage. * Features four distinct True Combos. * The '''Mixed Martial Arts''' passive (Knuckledusters + Knife) boosts M1 damage to approximately 24% per full chain. * The combined damage potential of {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}'s stance moves ('''Detrimental Division''' and '''Blitzing Talon''' in [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]]) can reach 66-67%, making it one of the highest damaging movesets in the game. * '''One Million Slashes''' breaks block on its initial contact and its final slash. * '''Dagger Cascade''' and '''Katana Throw''' can stun opponents on wakeup, similar to Triple Dagger Throw. * The two distinct movesets (Katana and Fists) greatly enhance combo extension possibilities. * Rewards players who experiment with move interactions, as nearly every move can function as a combo extender with proper techniques. * The '''{{Judgement}}''' ultimate offers great mobility. * '''Blitzing Talon''' teleports the user to the opponent after the Katana hits them. * If an opponent's evasive is baited, the second use of '''Shadow Rush''' can be combo-extended with a front dash by quickly turning the camera to hit the opponent's back, causing a roll that allows for a side dash M1 follow-up. * If used immediately after an uppercut, '''Shadow Rush''' can hit the player if done correctly. * After a downslam, if you wait a bit, you can use '''Whirlwind Pierce''' to hit your opponent and apply the Bloodcurdle effect. ===Cons=== * High learning curve for beginners due to being a complex character with numerous moves and stance mechanics. * A very advanced character; effective use requires a strong understanding of game mechanics. * Takes a long time to unlock due to the mastery requirement (200 kills without daily missions or {{The Prototype}} farming). * Very low base damage on individual skills, forcing reliance on M1 attacks and passive weapon buffs. * Lacks easily accessible, high-mobility moves in his base kit. * Executing most advanced combos requires frame-perfect inputs. * The Black Flash-like M1 passive can be escaped if the opponent times their evasive correctly. * If the Black Flash M1 passive is missed, it cannot be used and goes away. * '''Whirlwind Pierce''' can be punished, countered, and missed easily due to its recovery. * '''One Million Slashes''' and '''Shadow Rush''' (both variants) have significant endlag if missed, leading to easy punishes. * '''Shadow Rush (Air Variant)''' can be easily missed or dodged. * '''One Million Slashes''' does not extend combos on already ragdolled enemies. * '''One Million Slashes''' and '''Shadow Rush (Air Variant)''' have very low finisher thresholds (6-8%), making them unreliable for securing finishers. * '''Headbutt''' has almost no I-frames after the move finishes, leaving the user vulnerable to immediate counter-attacks from teamers. * Attempting the '''Dagger Cascade''' wakeup tech on a ragdolled opponent only grants the fourth M1 hit instead of a full M1 reset. * Obtaining certain weapon passives, like the Knuckledusters and Knife simultaneously, can be challenging, and the individual M1 boosts from single weapons are relatively small. * {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} has less raw base damage than his non mastered counterpart, {{Hero Slayer}}. * {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} can be complex to learn due to the amount of moves and functions of the moveset. * '''Whirlwind Pierce,''' despite having super armor, CAN be bypassed. ** This can be done by hitting the user enough to ragdoll them, or if they are on low health, kill them, or using a grab move or a move that puts you in an animation. *** This can also be said for '''Curdle Pierce,''' one of {{Hero Slayer}}'s moves. ==Movesets== |-|Katana= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" !style="background: #fa0202;" |'''Moves''' Slower startup compared to Curdle Pierce and can be cancelled more easily. |} ==Trivia/Notes== * When performing a finisher with '''Dagger Cascade''', the cutscene references a scene from [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Jujutsu_Kaisen_Wiki Jujutsu Kaisen] Season 2, Episode 3 (“Hidden Inventory Part 3”), specifically when [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rjgf9DN1VOg Toji Fushiguro defeats Satoru Gojo]. * Despite being based on the Hero Killer: Stain, the character features many visual and thematic references to [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Jujutsu_Kaisen_Wiki Jujutsu Kaisen], such as the [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Black_Flash Black Flash]-like effect on the empowered fourth M1 passive. * The reworked '''Whirlwind Pierce''' is a reference to a scene from My Hero Academia: Vigilantes (Stendhal Arc) where Stain (as Stendhal) [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Koichi_Haimawari_%26_Chizome_Akaguro_vs._Akira_Iwako saves Koichi Haimawari from Akira Iwako] and then [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Stendhal_Arc defeats Akira] for being a "sinner". * {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} is the first character to feature two distinct base movesets (Katana and Fists), a mechanic later also implemented for the subsequent masteries, this being {{Mastered Azure Flames}} and {{Mastered Split Ice}}. * He has the most finisher animations of any character, totaling 13 finishers for the base and awakened movesets, with every offensive move leading to a unique, brutal finish. ** This is followed by {{Human Architect}}, totaling at a close 12 finishers. * This is the second character to have a mastery system, following {{Mastered Green Hero}}, and preceding {{Mastered Azure Flames}}. * The character can utilize three distinct weapon styles through passives: Katana (default), Dagger (from Dagger Cascade finisher), and Knuckledusters (from Headbutt/Uppercut finishers). * Similarly, it is currently the only character with three distinct Forward Dash animations in its base moveset (Katana, Fists, and Knucklesusters). * '''Shadow Rush''' (ground variant) is a reference to the "[https://sekiro-shadows-die-twice.fandom.com/wiki/Shadowrush Shadowrush]" combat art from FromSoftware's game, [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sekiro:_Shadows_Die_Twice Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice]. * In the finishers for '''Detrimental Division''' and '''Cull the Evil''', the kanji 死 (which translates to "death") appears on the victim. * The finisher for '''Blitzing Talon''' is a reference to the anime [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Jujutsu_Kaisen_Wiki Jujutsu Kaisen], specifically the scene where [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Yuji_Itadori_%26_Aoi_Todo_vs._Mahito Aoi Todo unleashes a Black Flash against Mahito] during the [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Shibuya_Incident_Arc Shibuya Incident Arc]. * In the source material, [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Stain] uses his [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Quirk Quirk], [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Bloodcurdle Bloodcurdle], to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paralysis paralyze] opponents by ingesting their blood. The duration depends on the victim's [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blood_type blood type] (Type B is longest, followed by AB, A, and O). For game balance, the stun/paralysis in Heroes Battlegrounds has a set duration. * An M1 reset technique can be performed with '''Dagger Cascade''': After three M1s, back up slightly and use '''Dagger Cascade''' twice, then side dash behind them to be able to perform another M1 chain. This does not have to be done in quick succession. If it doesn't work, try backing up more before using '''Dagger Cascade'''. * {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}'s moveset and character were developed in approximately 2-3 months, partly during the development of {{Explosion Hero}}'s ultimate after Exploding Star was released. * This is the first [[Villains|villain]] character to receive a "Mastered" version, preceding {{Mastered Azure Flames}}. ({{Mastered Green Hero}}, despite being the first character with a Mastery system, is a Mastered Hero). * The first hit of '''Shadow Rush''' does not have a finisher animation and thus will not defeat an opponent on its own, even if they are at low health. * {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} currently has the most finisher animations in the game (13 total): four for the Katana moveset (five including the Air Variant of Shadow Rush), three for the Fist moveset, four for the ultimate moves, and one for the Black Flash passive. * Many of Mastered Hero Slayer's animations take notable reference from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_One%27s_Justice_2 My Hero One's Justice 2], where Stain appears as a playable character. This is most obvious with 'Dagger Cascade', as both it and Stain's Quirk Special 1 appear mostly identical. * '''Detrimental Division''' finisher is a first finisher in-game to have a POV switch to the opponent's at least once, the second being [[Human Architect#Finishers|'''Calamity''' finisher.]] * {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}'s '''Burst''' is an obvious reference to [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/S%C3%A9ance_Technique resurrected] [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Toji_Fushiguro Toji Fushiguro.] * This moveset's Black Flash finisher somewhat references the 'Consecutive Black Flash' from Jujutsu Kaisen performed by [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Kento_Nanami Kento Nanami] and [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Yuji_Itadori Yuji Itadori.] {{Characters}}

Mastered Split Ice:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Mastered Split Ice'''}} {{Quote|'''Ever since your brother got taken down... I had my eye on you, because all that building resentment was written on your face. I know that when I see it, and I know just how much grudges like that... can cloud a person's vision.'''|Shoto Todoroki, criticizing Ida for making a fight against Stain vengeful.}} * * {{Character_heros|GreenHero=Mastered Split Ice|image1= New Mastered Split Ice Icon.png|Icon Split Ice V2 Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Placeholder.png|Base Placeholder.png|Awakening Hq720 (1).jpg|Discord Sneak Peek |awakening_name='''Burning Flames'''|cost=100% Mastery of Split Ice|caption-image1=Ultra blue flames|NameOfCharacter={{Mastered Split Ice}}}} ==Description== {{Mastered Split Ice}}, also known as '''''Scorching Winter''''' by its [[Badges|badge name,]] is the 13th character released in the Heroes Battlegrounds. This character can be unlocked after achieving 100% [[Mastery|mastery]] by defeating {{The Prototype}}, completing daily tasks, or accumulating kills (0.5% for each kill, totaling at 200 kills) with its base form, {{Split Ice}}. Based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Shoto_Todoroki Shoto Todoroki] during the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Final_War_Arc Final War Arc], {{Mastered Split Ice}} captures the resolute last stand of Todoroki and his versatility with his unique '''Temperature''' gimmick, allowing him to switch between '''Fire and Ice'''. The '''Fire''' moveset, which stresses the application of pure and aggressive power, is the first moveset the user uses. His '''Ice''' moveset, which centers on freezing and is ideal for combo setups, may be switched by tapping the awakening key (holding it triggers the awakening itself). The user is encouraged to balance between the two types, otherwise the unique gimmick it possesses would backfire and put you at disadvantage. ==Pros and Cons== === Pros === * Once in the right hands, it deals a lot of damage. * Extreme combo-potential and versatility. * Lots of combo variations. * Freeze effect allows for deadly combos. * Freeze effect lasts notably longer than its non-mastered counterpart. * Flame Moveset deals a lot of damage. * Frost Moveset is useful for beginning combos. * The majority of the moves are unblockable, making it very useful for pressure and chipping away health, especially with the Flame Moveset. * Most moves have a high finisher threshold. * '''Arctic Flare, Frozen Aegir, Searing Steps''' and other moves can lead into '''Glacial Effigy'''. * '''Flash Tempo''' is good for neutral. * '''Arctic Flare''' will freeze the opponent regardless of whether they are blocking or not. * '''Glacial Aegir''' can be used as an escape tool, perfect against teamers. * '''Searing Steps''' and '''Inferno Impact''' grants the user super armor and hyper armor respectively during the run. * '''Ignition Drive''' is an easy combo extender with a very fast up" their moves. * '''Flash Tempo''' is not optimal for combos. * Staying around '''Glacial Effigy''' freezes you, so camping around it is not a good strategy. * Moves like '''Inferno Impact''', Mastery:
== Mastered Movesets ==
Mastery is required to unlock new movesets for {{Green Hero}}, {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}, {{Mastered Azure Flames}} and {{Mastered Split Ice}}. To unlock a mastered moveset you must obtain 100% mastery on the base variant of the move set. Mastering a character will grant a [[Badges|Badge]] corresponding to the character.  

== '''Progress''' ==
You can see your mastery progress by clicking the mastery button on the top left of your screen. There are currently 3 ways of getting mastery:

* Quest UI: The Quest UI will give you a quest every 24 hours for 4-5% mastery. This cooldown can be skipped if you spend 35 Robux by speaking to the Quest UI again. You are able to do quests in private servers, There are currently 5 quests to choose from:
*# Defeat 5 players: 5% mastery
*# Defeat 5 villains ({{Hero Slayer}}, {{Warp Portal}}, {{Decaying Hatred}}, {{Azure Flames}}, {{Human Architect}}): 5% mastery
*# Defeat 5 heroes ({{Green Hero}}, {{Explosion Hero}}, {{Split Ice}}, {{Full Throttle}}, {{Winged Assassin}}): 5% mastery
*# Destroy 12 trees: 4% mastery
*# Awaken 3 times: 5% mastery
* Killing players will give you 0.5% mastery; however, this does not work on private servers.
* Killing {{The Prototype}} will give you 5-13% mastery. First place gets 13% mastery, and second place gets 10% mastery. All other places get 5 mastery.

== '''Strategy''' ==
The best and most efficient way of getting mastery is to defeat {{The Prototype}} boss by yourself over and over again in a private server, this guarantees you get the 1st place in damage dealt, and you can come back over and over again even if you die, so you don't miss out on damage. {{The Prototype}} spawns every 30 minutes on private servers, so you can prepare and get your ultimate. The recommended [[Ultimate Mode|ultimate]] to drain its HP is {{Explosion Hero}}'s Tenth Torch, as just the animation itself deals good damage (around 20-26%). {{The Prototype}} '''DOES NOT''' despawn if you die, so you can farm him even if you die.

Quests and {{The Prototype}} mastery still work in private servers.

For doing quests, one thing to do is buying PS+ due to no cooldown and using the "Give awakening" option for [[Gamepasses|PS+]].

There are various ways to make farming the boss easier. You can get awakening with {{Explosion Hero}} and {{Split Ice}} (due to both being high damage ultimates) and damage {{The Prototype}} just enough then switch to the character you are getting mastery for, then kill {{The Prototype}}. Repeating this along with doing daily quests will get you the mastery in about a week. (Depends on how many you kill (3 to 4 a day is good.) By the time you kill {{The Prototype}} he should spawn faster allowing you to kill him once again for more mastery. This is very effective and very quick, and generous to those who cannot afford PS+ and resetting daily quests.

To get the ultimate faster before {{The Prototype}} spawns, it's always recommend to use Base {{Green Hero}}, as his passive allows him to get the ultimate faster, the passive fills the ultimate bar '''VERY FAST''' when doing a [[IWO]] finisher.

== Cons ==
''When fighting {{The Prototype}} boss, you may lose track of time when he appears if you're using a character that deals low damage. Example: {{Azure Flames}} and {{Hero Slayer}}. It's recommended to use {{Explosion Hero}}<nowiki/>'s ultimate to lower his HP then quickly switch to the character you're grinding Mastery for.''

== Pros ==

Pity:
== '''Usage''' ==
Pity is used to unlock the {{Warp Portal}} moveset. You will start at 1 pity (1% chance of obtaining Warp Portal) and if you fail to get the moveset your pity will increase.

== '''Obtainment''' ==
Pity can be obtained by killing {{The Prototype}} boss if you don't have Warp Portal unlocked. The amount of pity you get depends of how much damage you dealt to The Prototype:

* if you have the most damage your pity will increase by 3
* if you have the second most damage your pity will increase by 2
* if you have the third most damage your pity will increase by 1
* If you aren’t in top 3 you won’t get any pity of a chance of getting Warp Portal

== Strategy ==
The best way to get pity is to invite 2 of your friends, get ultimate and buy/wait for the boss. Use characters like {{Hero Slayer}}, {{Split Ice}} and {{Explosion Hero}} since they have good damage and stuns and Hero Slayer can extend his ultimate.

Playstyles:
{{Stub}}


'''Welcome to the playstyles page! In here we'll introduce the different playstyles recommended for each character, how to use them properly, and how to counter certain playstyles. This is a pretty new wiki page, so feel free to help it improve by adding information!'''

== '''Main Playstyles:''' ==

=== '''Aggressive''' ===
This playstyle is based on you doing aggressive approaches to apply as much pressure on your opponent as you can in order to get an opening to attack and combo your enemy. This playstyle almost always gets hard-countered by a passive player due to the purpose of being passive in the first place. Be wary though, Being too aggressive can lead to you making sloppy plays and getting countered.

=== '''Passive''' ===
This playstyle relies on you predicting your opponent's playstyle and moves in order to counter them and grant yourself an opportunity to force the opponent into a combo. Another way to say this is that you would use this playstyle to analyze their movements and capitalize on moments of hesitation or mistakes. However, this playstyle is heavily hated by the community because it's harder to fight against than other playstyles, but it's mostly a skill issue on their part. This playstyle (almost) becomes forced when fighting against multiple people as well. It can get extremely hard to fight when they are heavily passive and only use counterattacks, so it’s recommended to try and side dash behind them, or quickly front dash behind them for a combo.

=== '''Neutral''' ===
A combination between aggressive and passive and switching between the two playstyles during fights in order to adapt to the playstyle of your opponent, but it requires you to master both aggressive and passive to fully take advantage of this playstyle. This is a really good playstyle to have under your belt as you can just switch from being passive to aggressive and vice versa to keep the opponent from understanding you.

=== '''Hit and Run''' ===
This is a playstyle that everyone hates, mostly because of how annoying it is instead of how hard it is to beat. The way to play this playstyle is to mix a bit of sneaky moves/surprise attacks with some quick retreats, and if you're low, immediately run off to regenerate more health. However, despite this playstyle looking like it's a great option, it's easily countered by pros and you won't be able to progress much in fights with this playstyle so just finishing the fight will take quite a long time. This playstyle also isn't reliable when you're playing a character with little to no mobility options, and when you reach low hp you will also get a speed debuff, making the playstyle harder to play than it looks.

=== '''Climax Jumping''' ===
Well it's pretty much in the name, these types of players jump and combo you till you die making it pretty difficult to defeat if you don't know what you're doing. Well I got you, They normally use ''Neutral'' in order to gain the upper hand on you however some of these players can play ''Aggressive'' or over the top ''Passive''. The solutions to beat these players is either play ''Neutral'' and ''Passive'' or gaining another player to help out to make it a 1v1, And they kinda suck when they are is a 1v1 state and will be either ''Passive'' or ''Aggressive'' or if they know what they are doing ''Neutral'' but it's pretty easy once you get these types of players separated.

== Playstyles of Different Characters ==

=== {{Green Hero}} ===
This character has a moveset that's great for performing aggressive plays as all of his moves are unblockable when used right. He also has two hyper armor moves, one that is granted hyper armor immediately upon use (Detroit). And the other will receive hyper armor once in the air (Manchester). He has somewhat decent combo potential and pretty balanced cooldowns. However, it's still recommended to finish off fights quickly, as his inferior combo potential to other characters will catch up to you sooner or later.

=== {{Mastered Green Hero}} ===
This is the mastered version of Green Hero. It fixes most of the base Green Hero's weaknesses, such as having way better combo potential and being able to perform one-shot combos with ease. Unlike the base Green Hero, Mastered Green Hero has three moves that grant I-frames when landed, allowing the user to do large damage and perform fast combos with this character without being interrupted by other players. However, in exchange for this, this mastery character has an extremely high amount of cooldowns on his moves, making him inferior in fights that take way too long. His moves also have slightly less overall damage output than the base variant. So to use this character to the fullest, make sure you don't waste your moves and do careful plays so that you can have opportunities to perform a highly damaging combo. While it has fewer block breaks than the base variant of Green Hero, it's recommended to play aggressively since your moves have long cooldowns and are not meant for long-term fights. And patience is required for the best passive plays so it may not be suitable for mastery Green Hero, but you can still use the passive playstyle to gain openings for you to attack the enemy and perform a combo if your enemy is impatient.

=== {{Hero Slayer}} ===
This moveset is a pretty freestyled moveset with not long cooldowns and LOTS of combo potential that people just don't use. It has a total of 3 combo extenders and he has amazing reach on his move's hitboxes, Such as Thousand Slashes and Triple Dagger Throw as well as slightly increased M1 range. This character can be used for both passive and aggressive thanks to its versatility. Beware though, missing some of his moves can easily allow the enemy to punish your mistake. So don't throw out all of your moves raw. Curdle Pierce is a great combo starter and extender because it has a decent hitbox and applies Bloodcurdle to anyone hit by it, allowing for an easy free opening to attack the enemy. But the drawback is the long startup, which allows enemies to easily dodge and punish the attack. So you have to time it after a Downslam/Uppercut to properly land it. However, if you time it too early and hit them while they are still waking up from ragdoll, it will instead do an increased damage of 12% and not apply Blood Curdle.

=== {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} ===
Unlike the base version of Hero Slayer, this mastery buffs the overall combo potential of the character by a lot. This character also boasts two movesets, Which gives you lots of advantages in fights. And unlike Mastered Green Hero, It doesn't suffer from the cooldowns issue due to having seven moves in total, and the fists moveset has less overall cooldowns. This character's moves by themselves are pretty weak, but the advantage is that almost all of them allow you to combo into an M1 combo if you use techs or chain them with other moves. Due to all the advantages, this character requires a lot of skill to play due to his combos and techs requiring a lot of knowledge about the game as well as skill. And if you throw out random moves raw, Especially moves from the katana moveset, you're going to get punished hard. So don't use moves unless in a combo because this character has a terrible neutral. Almost all of his moves have terrible startup and endlag that is easily punishable if not used within a combo (exp. One Million Slashes). Overall, just be careful when using this character. And learn how the game works so you make full use of this character's massive combo potential.

=== {{Azure Flames}} ===
This character has a pretty decent moveset, one grab move that combo extends through techs (Azure Grab), a AoE attack that travels super far (Infernal Burn), an crowd control AoE move that deals decent damage (Ember Eruption), and a really fast combo extender rushdown attack that grants you hyper armor once you have crouched down (Ember Assault). This character has good damage and combo potential, but other characters like Hero Slayer and Mastered Green Hero easily outclasses this character by a lot, because Ember Assault doesn't grant you a full M1 chain, and Azure Grab Tech will nerf the damage of M1s if used, it's also super easy to punish Ember Assault and Ember Eruption if they're blocked. To use this character properly, you need to learn as much techs as possible in order to any significant damage because his moves are pretty weak by themselves, it's recommended to play neutral, this way your opponents won't be able to easily predict your moves and punish your recklessness.

=== {{Mastered Azure Flames}} ===
The mastered variant of Azure Flames fixes the issue of bad moveset damage and inferior damage in combos. It has two forms that grant different movesets, the '''Azure Form''' grants the user high damaging moves with excellent combos, however, they have super long startups and cooldowns. The '''Violet''' '''Form''' grants the user moves that deal slightly less damage and have less overall combo potential, but in exchange, they have really short cooldowns and allows you to easily rushdown the players and deal high damage due to the versatile moves, but staying in this form can cost you due to it slowly burning you. While in this form, and you might think the damage isn't much, but you DEFINITELY WILL think otherwise once you start playing too reckless with '''Violet Form'''. Overall, it's recommended to learn your timing to make the most out of the combo potential, and play as aggressive as you can in order to not waste any time, especially if you're in '''Violet Form'''.

=== {{Split Ice}} ===
This moveset seems to be a rather boring moveset that doesn't have anything really special about it, yet using techs and mind games can make it into one of the most deadly characters. Because Split Ice is known for its 2+1 combo, most players will try to spam ragdoll/are more prone to ragdolling your first M1 combo you land. Using this high level of evasive stress, the next full combo using the [https://heroes-battlegrounds.fandom.com/wiki/Techniques#Shoto_Todoroki_//_Split_Ice_Techniques Javelin Pierce] (can also use normal 4th M1 into jump Ice Javelin + front dash)  +  [https://heroes-battlegrounds.fandom.com/wiki/Techniques#Shoto_Todoroki_//_Split_Ice_Techniques Freezing Cold] techs can lead to a quick 90% combo. If they also don’t ragdoll your 4th M1 quickly, you can close range Ice Javelin to extend into a true Sub-Zero. Additionally, if you have the empowered Frost Burst you can true downslam which can lead into the downslam Sub-Zero Variant. Then once you get them low and you wait for cooldowns, your next non-evasive 2+1 combo will kill 99% of the time. This also leads into Split Ice having a combo-starting counter, which can streamline his insane offence even more. Split Ice fits into every play style perfectly, can change his combo game in a second, and allows for a 50% true combo with a tech (tech is not available in the tech list yet).  

=== {{Mastered Split Ice}} ===
This moveset requires great game knowledge to handle, like most mastery characters. He relies on set-up and ability management in order to deal lethal amounts of damage through their combos. Their moves deal large amount of damage and have high combo extension potential, but the temperature meter forces the player to switch between which type of moves to use or get punished, limiting their damage and combo potential. They have great neutral, because their moveset and playstyles are very versatile. Be careful with how you play, because you have no actual passive to help you in fights, your passives only inflict debuffs, and you do not gain a passive buff upon kills like other characters.

=== {{Explosion Hero}} ===
This character has a LOT of combo potential similar to the mastery movesets/characters, however, a major weakness of this character is that you are completely unable to do any effective combos if your opponent has their evasive off cooldown. The main combo extenders of this character is the first use of ground cannon, which applies soft ragdoll to anyone it hits(soft ragdoll makes it so the enemy is ragdolled, but is still vulnerable to moves and attacks that doesn't hit ragdoll players, like m1s and explosion combo), and the 2nd use of powered shot, which ragdolls the enemy long enough for a true downslam. It's recommended to play aggressive, since if you wait for too long, the enemy can regain their evasive, which prevents you from doing combos. Don't waste any moves unless it's explosion combo because the rest of the moves have really long cooldowns.

=== {{Decaying Hatred}} ===
This character has really powerful damage in his base kit, but in exchange, it's kinda hard to actually properly combo the moves together, especially void steps. But once you have mastered his combos, you can do easy 90% combos, he also has decently short cooldowns on his moves as well. his first move removes all end lag and also frees you from the animation once the explosions hit the enemy, which means you can easily combo deteriorate into this move. His third move is a high damaging counter that can be used as a combo starter if your enemy has bad ping or you used it next to a wall, but beware, don't spam this move as it can be easily punished since it has lots of end lag if missed. His last base move has a unique feature where if you managed to successfully time the follow up attacks after the initial hit, you can deal extra damage, 2% each follow up timed, and you can do a total 5 follow ups, resulting in a total damage of 18% if you managed to time everything right. You can play both aggressive and passive with this character, as he is pretty much an all rounder, so if you're confident, you can train yourself to try and play the neutral playstyle.

=== {{Full_Throttle}} ===
This character is very good for fighting teamers due to its air combat passive. People who use this character will be a "Neutral" playstyle as the character can do a lot of damage which is good for aggressive players but it has very bad endlag on it's moves which makes people play passively. It's usually not recommended ro charge your 1st move to max charge, since despite the high damage, it's hard to combo extend off of due to the reduced ragdoll time, and you usually can't even hit it in a true combo due to the massive start-up, the only time you should use it is when your enemy won't let go their block, when they have bad ping, or when they're just terrible at the game. Learn all the combos you can, because without them, this character can't really do much.

=== {{Human Architect}} ===

This character is mostly used by pros due to its super high damage moves and hard combos. People who use this can be a "Neutral" or a "Hit and Run" as their moves deal high damage but have also medium cooldowns. You should learn the side dash curving tech as it's an important asset of those that use Human Architect. The character also has 3 ultimates, but they require getting a kill in order to unlock. During the 1st ult, you should try to get a combo off as soon as you can, and once you unlock your 2nd ult, which is an giant Kaiju form, run to the spot with the most amount of players to kill as many as you can, and in your last ult, you're just a speed demon meant to rushdown any remaining survivors from the Kaiju ult to pick off easy kills.

=== {{Winged_Assassin}} ===
TBA

=== {{Warp Portal}} ===
This character has the advantage of a total of 3 grab moves, and they're also really fast, making this character potentially a teamer fighting machine. However, it's major weakness is that three out of the four moves of this character is blockable, and it's overall damage has been nerfed after the rework. It's recommended to learn the best combos for this character, as this will be your main source of damage, play as aggressive as you can, because you absolutely CANNOT allow the enemy to wait off the cooldowns of their evasive. Also do note that spamming abilities raw will get you punished, since they have endlag comparable with Mastered Hero Slayer's endlags.

== Countering Playstyles ==

=== '''Aggressive''' ===
This playstyle is extremely easy to counter as long as you can predict their moves. A lot of aggressive players aren't actually that good at playing the playstyle they chose, so you can take advantage of that and watch out for which direction do they side dash to most of time to easily predict it when they try to attack you with a side dash M1, the reason this works is because once someone used to side dashing to one direction, they'll only be going that direction, otherwise it'll feel weird for them when they try to use side dashes to the other direction. However, sometimes there are way more experienced players, so it could prove difficult to counter them. when this happens, instead of blocking all the time, try to play both aggressive and passive by switching between the two playstyles as a way to try and catch them off guard. This usually works out fine if you're about the same skill level as them, but it won't always work, depending on the player who you're playing against.

=== '''Passive''' ===
Passive players can be hard to deal with, it usually depends on the method they use in their passive combat to punish your attacks and their skill level. Some passive players might try to punish your M1s by predicting which side will you side dash to in order to block before that, but you can counter that if you know how to side dash to the other side instead to counter their strategy. They might also just have enough reaction speed to block your attacks without predictions, which would be harder to deal with, when that happens, it's just gonna be a stalling game to see who will make a move first, and the person who makes a mistake first....loses.

=== '''Neutral''' ===
Neutral players are most likely the most difficult to actually manage to kill. Both mastering Passiveness and Aggressiveness to their disposal.As they could counter any playstyle if they're good enough for the opponent however there is a way to counter such dual playstyles and that is to become passive at first and then change your playstyle at random times and at the right times.This will throw them off and will give you a better chance at beating this playstyle. Unless you make a mistake {Witch is likely as most can't just switch playstyles quick enough to throw people off} then it could be the end of your fight.
Private Server Commands:
{{Gamepass|title1=Private Server Commands|image1=Private Server Commands.jpg|cost=499 Robux}}

'''Private Server Commands''' also known as '''Private Server+''' is a gamepass for the game, [[Heroes Battlegrounds]]. If you buy this gamepass you must own a private server for it to work. The private servers are free.

== What you obtain ==
Here is what you obtain when you buy the gamepass.

=== Commands Category ===

* '''Kick Command''': You can type "/kick (player ID or username)" to kick someone from your server. (Meaning they are forcefully leaving your server but can still rejoin after.)
* '''Kick Players on Respawn''': If you turn this option on, players get kicked from your private server once they respawn.
* '''Lock Server''': If you enable this option, you can make your private server non-joinable.
* '''Disable [[The Prototype|Prototype]] Spawning''': Turning this command on will stop Namu from spawning in your private server.
* '''Game Speed''': Increases the overall speed of the game (Movement speed, speed of the moves, speed of audio, speed of ult activation)
* '''Damage Multiplier''': If you turn this option up or down, you can either make the damage lower or higher.
* '''Hitbox Multiplier''': If you turn this option up or down, you can make the hitbox bigger or smaller. 
* '''Gravity''': If you turn this option up or down, you can have more or less gravity. More gravity can make you much more heavy but less gravity can make you lighter.
* '''PS+ Command Permission''': Gives '''PS+ Commands''' to anyone you select

==== Remove [[Structures and Objects|Objects]] Commands ====
If you turn any of this options on, the game removes them from the server until you turn it back off.

* Remove Trash Cans
* Remove Trees
* Remove Pillars
* Remove Benches
* Remove Tiles

=== Cooldowns ===

* '''No Cooldowns (Only You)''': If you enable this option, you will have no cooldowns on all moves for any character.
* '''Jump Cooldown (Only You)''': The jump cooldown is automatically turned on, if you turn this option off, you will have no cooldown for your jumps.

=== Destruction ===

* '''Max Parts Per Destruction''': This adjusts how much destruction happens if you break something.
* '''Max Destruction Parts''': This will limit the amount if destruction parts in your private server. (i assume)

=== Spawnables ===

* '''Spawn Death Note''': You will be able to spawn the death note if you toggle on the option.
* '''Spawn General's 45''': Gives you a gun
* '''Give Infinity''': Gives the move Infinity from the [[The Strongest|Gojo]] moveset
* '''Spawn Punching Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that is constantly punching.
* '''Spawn Normal Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that is just normal.
* '''Spawn Blocking Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that is holding block.
* '''Spawn 1 HP Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that is 1 health.
* '''Spawn Invincible Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that cannot be hit.
* '''Spawn Evasive Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that uses its evasive upon ragdolling from M1's, or moves
* '''Spawn NAN Dummy''': You can spawn a dummy that has inf hp and never dies

=== Battle Royale ===

* '''Enable Storm''': Toggling this will let you enable the storm. Just like in Fortnite, the storm will move around and if you go inside it, it will kill you.
* '''Storm Time''': Changing this will affect how long the storm lasts. (I am assuming)
* '''Spectate On Death''': This will allow you to spectate others when you die.
* '''Enables Points''': Enables points for you.
* '''Change Points''': Changes your points when toggled.
* '''Set Team''': Allows you to change your team.
* '''Delete Team''': This allows you to remove a team from the game.
* '''Set Health''': This button allows you to change your health to any number you want.
* '''Set Max Health''': This allows you to change how much health you have for your whole health bar.
* '''Regen Times''': This changes how long it takes to regenerate your health.
* '''Health On Kill''': This button allows you change how much health you get from killing people.
* '''Mode Gain Times''': (i don't know)
* '''Give Spectate UI''': Enables the Spectate UI for you.

=== Character ===

* '''Toggle Flight''': Toggling this will allow you to fly.
* '''Toggle Building Tools''': Toggling Building Tools will allow you to build. You will be able to build items such as trash cans, pillars, TVs, and rocks. Enabling this will disable Namu from spawning as this can effect your game.
* '''Infinite Ultimate''': This stops your ultimate from running out.
* '''Invincibility''': This allows you to not get hit.
* '''Give Ultimate''': Toggling this gives you [[Ultimate Mode|ultimate.]]
* '''Give Instant Ultimate''': If someone has full ultimate bar , you can trigger their ult activation (i think)
* '''Remove Ultimate''': This will stop the ultimate or remove all the ultimate from the bar.
* '''Give Random Moveset''': This will allow you to get 4 random moves in your moveset. This could be removed moves, unobtainable moves, etc.
* '''Give Random Tool''': This command gives the chosen user an extra move which applies all the way until 9 moves before it switches to letter keybinds which are currently glitched and don't work. This can be removed by resetting/dying or giving yourself a random moveset which randomizes your moves back to 4 moves. The chosen moves can be anything from base moves, ultimate moves, Gojo's move's, huge lady's moves, and moves that aren't completed which is proved correct because of the turbine drive move being ingame but not doing anything except for a kick.
* '''No Stun''': Enabling this will allow you to not get stunned. This means that during M1s or moves, you can walk freely.
* '''No Attacks''': This command takes away your privileges to use M1 or moves until you turn off the move. Dashes still work which means that front dashes still do damage.
* '''Toggle Noclip''': This will allow you to clip through walls.
* '''Toggle Respawn''': This will allow you to respawn or not.
Ranked:
{{Stub}}

Ranked is a competitive Gamemode in Heroes Battlegrounds that allows you to fight players at a similar skill level as you. To join ranked, click the crossing swords icon on the top left of your screen. When joining, you will be teleported to the ranked lobby. When joining the lobby, there will be two matchmaking boxes to choose from; the 1v1 box or 2v2 box.

==1v1==
On the 1v1 platform of the ranked lobby, you get matched against whichever person is also standing on the platform that has a ranked level most similar to your own. After the queue, you and your opponent will spawn in the arena. There will be a brief period in which you are prohibited from moving or using attacks. During this grace period,  you can and your opponent have the option of changing characters. Then after a few seconds, the match begins. The match ends when either you or your opponent have died two times.

==2v2==
On the 2v2 platform of the ranked lobby, you will be teamed up with whoever standing is also standing on the platform that has entered around the same time as you. Two more people standing on this platform will be matched together by the same principle. (Alternatively, you could send a request for a specific player to be your teammate. If they accept this request, both you and your requested teammate will teleport onto the 2v2s platform and begin to queue ). These two are to be your opponents. Both teams will spawn in the arena. During this you and your teammate can change characters. Then after a few seconds, the match begins. The match ends after every player of at least one team has died two times.

==Trivia/Notes==

*You need 100 kills to play ranked.
*You can change your character mid game and after you die.
*When winning a ranked game without dying once, the “Game” win screen will be replaced with a “Plus Ultra” screen. If this is your first flawless match, you will receive a special [[Badges|badge.]]
*You cannot damage your teammate in the 2v2s mode.

== What is this? ==
Soundtracks are musical tracks that play an important role in ''Heroes Battlegrounds'', helping to enhance the overall gameplay. These pieces of music can be heard and used in several different ways within the game. For example, players might hear them as part of emotes, or acquire them as part of an Awakening sequence. Each soundtrack adds its own unique atmosphere, making the game feel much more vibrant and lively

Split Ice:
[[Category:Characters]] {{Quote|'''''If you wanna stop this, then stand up! Because I've got one thing to say to you! Never forget who you want to become!| - [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Shoto_Todoroki Shoto Todoroki]'s heroic inspiration to [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tenya_Ida Tenya Ida]}}{{Character_heros|GreenHero=Split Ice |image1= New Todoroki Icon.png|New Icon Split .webp|Old Icon Split Spawn.gif|Spawn Animation Splitsz .png|Base theFLAME.png|Awakening SplitIceAura.gif|Awakening Aura |awakening_name=Burning Flames |cost=Free|NameOfCharacter=Split Ice}} == Description== {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Split Ice'''}} {{Split Ice}} is a free-to-play character in Heroes Battlegrounds, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Shoto_Todoroki Shoto Todoroki] from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki My Hero Academia]. He is the fourth character added to the game. This character excels in close-range and mid-range combat, possessing strong combo potential. Players can master this character to unlock {{Mastered Split Ice}}. Achieving {{Mastered Split Ice}} requires gaining [[mastery]]. Mastery points are earned by defeating opponents while playing as Split Ice (0.50%), completing quests from the Mastery UI (4-5%), and defeating [[The Prototype|'''The Prototype''']] boss (5-13%). ==Pros & Cons== === '''Pros''' === * '''Frost Burst''' has a medium-to-large area of effect (it's slightly inconsistent hitbox can sometimes provide a minor advantage to the user). * '''Ice Javelin''' features a ranged variant, activated by using the skill when no opponent is in the user's immediate melee range. * '''Frost Burst''' is an excellent tool for finishing off low-health opponents. * Capable of a high-damage (approximately 50%) true combo using only two moves and M1 attacks. * '''Frost Counter''', if landed, stuns the hit opponent(s) for 4 seconds (up to 6 seconds with the passive-boosted version). * Successfully landing '''Frost Counter''' allows the user to inflict significantly more damage. * '''Frost Counter''' can counter any non-ultimate move in the game. * '''Sub Zero''' has four distinct variants, offering versatility. * The '''Glacial Resonance''' passive, which enhances freeze effects, is useful against multiple opponents (teamers). * The Grab and Uppercut Variants of '''Sub Zero''' grant invincibility frames (I-frames). * '''Sub Zero's''' Uppercut Variant can be timed to hit opponents just before they use an evasive maneuver, leading to high-damage true combos. * Possesses simple combos that can be developed into deadly sequences with proper execution. * Can deal substantial damage when played effectively. * '''Ice Javelin''' provides an M1 reset, though it reduces subsequent M1 damage to approximately 1% per hit. * '''Frost Counter''' is an effective combo starter when successfully landed. * '''Frost Burst''' has a high finisher threshold (14%) and its finisher activates immediately. * '''Heatwave Strike''' grants Super Armor during its animation. * '''Blazing Soul''' has a long duration (26 seconds), which can be used to drain an opponent's ultimate bar if they are caught in the cutscene. * Due to '''Burning Flames''' (both the activation and ultimate moves) dealing significant amount of damage makes {{Split Ice}} a very good choice against the [[The Prototype|T'''he Prototype''']] boss. ** This is also useful for gaining [[mastery]] for this [[Mastered Split Ice|character]]. * '''Ice Javelin (Ranged Variant)''' is a very good combo starter, due to being able to be used at a distance. * The ultimate is very good for boss farming. === '''Cons''' === * '''Blazing Soul''' can be difficult to control and may hit unintended targets. * Using the same '''Sub Zero''' variant multiple times consecutively incurs a longer cooldown. * '''Sub Zero's''' Downslam and Ranged variants can be easily dodged with a ragdoll cancel. * The character's basic M1 combo damage is relatively low. * Opponents can perform a forward dash towards the user during the startup of '''Ice Javelin's''' ranged variant, leading to an easy punish. * The hitbox for '''Ice Javelin's''' ranged variant is the projectile model itself, making it avoidable with a simple jump or side dash. * The ranged variant of '''Sub Zero''' is considered one of its weaker options. * '''Blazing Soul''' has the longest ultimate move cutscene in the game, which can be disruptive if an unintended target is hit. * Both melee and ranged versions of '''Ice Javelin''' severely reduce subsequent M1 damage. * Opponents with fast reactions or low latency can evade '''Frost Counter''' with a side dash. * The '''Burning Flames''' ultimate has only one dedicated mobility move ('''Blazing Soul'''), and its other moves have limited range. * '''Sub Zero'''’s ranged variant is very inaccurate * The base moveset is very basic and does not have much room for creative combos, and does not have much combo potential. ==Movesets== |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background:linear-gradient(90deg, #5372fc, #5372fc, #5372fc, #5372fc, #5372fc);" |'''Moves''' ! style="background:linear-gradient(90deg, #5372fc, #5372fc, #5372fc, #5372fc, #5372fc);" |'''Damage''' ! style="background: #5372fc;" |'''Cooldown''' ! style="background: #5372fc;" |'''Information''' |- |[[File:Split M1.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Glacial Strikes (M1)''' |4% per M1 Full combo: 16% |~0.5 seconds |The user delivers a series of
