This enhanced Frost Burst can reportedly only hit one person. *Using either boosted move consumes the passive effect until another finisher is landed. |- |'''Cooldown Reset''' |N/A |Defeating an opponent with credit resets all ability cooldowns. ---- * This applies to all characters. |} == Trivia== {{Split Ice}} was originally named "Todoroki," his canon surname from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Shoto_Todoroki My Hero Academia]. Prior to that, an earlier development name was "Icy Hot." * The music track for Split Ice's ultimate, '''Burning Flames''', is "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SCD2tB1qILc Frame of Mind" by Tristam and Braken]. It is notably the only ultimate theme in the game (at the time of writing) to feature prominent lyrical vocals. ** However, during the update for [[Winged Assassin|Winged Assassin's]] new moves (10th of October), the music will not play for an unknown reason. * The development of this character reportedly took approximately 9 months to complete. * This was the first character to feature a significant number of impact frames in animations and the first to have an ultimate activation sequence that transitions into a cut scene. * Although Shoto Todoroki in the source material uses his [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Half-Cold_Half-Hot left side for fire and right side for ice], in-game M1 animations (uppercut, normal, etc.) may show different hands covered in ice inconsistently. * The character's ultimate, '''Burning Flames''', utilizes some of the most complex visual effects seen for a character at the time of its release. * '''Sub Zero''' was originally named '''Freezing Variants''', which itself was an evolution from an even earlier name, '''Freezing Grab'''. * '''Heatwave Strike''' and '''Freezeburn Wave''' are based on Shoto's signature moves from the anime/manga: '''[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NJzSS1E05f8 Jet Kindling]''' and '''[https://youtu.be/wpOhT35YtWg?t=127 Flashfreeze Heatwave]''', respectively. ** '''Freezeburn Wave''' was previously named "Flashfreeze Heatwave" in-game. ** '''Heatwave Strike''' was previously named "JET KINDLING". * '''Frost Burst''' may be inspired by Shoto's large-scale ice attack, '''[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B2wDrKWK3io Heaven-Piercing Ice Wall]'''. * {{Split Ice}} exclusively uses ice-based moves in his base moveset. This is a reference to Shoto's initial reluctance and disdain towards using his fire abilities due to his traumatic upbringing and complex relationship with his father, [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Enji_Todoroki Enji Todoroki (Endeavor)]. This stemmed from Endeavor's abusive desire to create a successor to surpass All Might, which led to Shoto's [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Rei_Todoroki mother] suffering a mental breakdown and scarring Shoto's left eye (his fire side) with boiling water due to his resemblance to Endeavor. * The ultimate move '''Blazing Soul''' contains numerous pop culture references: ** The initial walking scene is inspired by a scene from [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jujutsu_Kaisen Jujutsu Kaisen], where '''[https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Yuji_Itadori Yuji Itadori]''' menacingly approaches the cursed spirit '''[https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Mahito Mahito]''', often memed with the song [https://open.spotify.com/track/6VObnIkLVruX4UVyxWhlqm "Skyfall"] by '''[https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adele Adele]''' (as seen [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=38otba7tU24 here]). ** During one impact frame of the walking sequence, the text "press "E" to revive" briefly appears, potentially referencing the revive prompt in games like [https://callofduty.fandom.com/wiki/Zombies_(Treyarch) Call Of Duty: Zombies]. ** There is a small chance for the user's character to say, "Where you go I go..." during the finisher. The opponent hit by this finisher has an even lower chance to exclaim, "'''''HELP ME!!!!!!'''''" or "'''''PLEASE HELP'''''!!!" ** In the final impact frame, the line '''''"Nah I'd win"''''' appears, a reference to Satoru Gojo's famous line from the ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jujutsu_Kaisen Jujutsu Kaisen]'' manga ([https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Chapter_221 chapter 221]). ** During the last attack of the finisher, a brief segment of the song [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FtRp6svwSY4 REMEMBER by Masato (coldrain)] plays during an impact frame. * The move '''Sub Zero''' had a different, non-variant animation prior to its official release. * The "[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FScsExs6WrY A Hero of Fire and Ice Revealed | HBTV #4]" developer video confirmed that {{Split Ice}} would be the fourth character added and the second hero character to receive a [[Mastery]] version of his character in the future. {{Characters}}

== Introduction ==
'''Structures And Objects''' are the unique buildings and materials that make up the Heroes Battlegrounds map. The map includes many different structures and objects, like trash cans, the Villain Bar, and many others. (''Made by [[User:DazeRedeemed|DazeRedeemed]], you are more than welcome to help expand this page, just don't vandalize it.'')

== Objects ==
Objects are small things that are seen everyday, some of them can be interactable, (see [[IWO|IWOs]].) and some of them are just for show.

Here are some of the most common objects ingame:

{| class="fandom-table wikitable"
! |'''<font color="white"> Image </font>'''
! |'''<font color="white"> Description </font>'''
|-
|[[File:Trash can.png|thumb|Trash Can (Found everywhere)]]
|This is an object you see everyday, made mostly of metal, a trash can is an interactable object which results in damaging the enemy when you hit them with an M1. (see [[IWO]] for Trash Can) There is currently no other way to interact with this object other than M1, normally trash cans are used to throw out waste, however in the game, there is no waste and the trashcans are only used for throwing at people and cracking skulls.
|-
|[[File:Tv.png|thumb|TV (Found only in Villain Bar)]]
|This is an object that is only in the Villain Bar (Scroll down to Villain Bar) it is a small TV that is always turned off. You can interact with it by having an enemy and M1 them near the TV. (see [[IWO]] for TV) There is no way to turn on the TV, when you interact with an M1, it will break the TV while turning off the Villain Bar lights.
|-
|[[File:Jukeboz.png|thumb|Jukebox (Found only in Villain Bar)]]
|A jukebox the size of a player, there is no discs that can be inserted and the only music it will make is when you interact it through an M1. (see [[IWO]] for Jukebox) The song that it will make is the drop of the song [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nBpe2YQEzZo Free Bird].
|-

Note: If you have a sit [[Emotes|emote]] (For example: Angel Sit or Criss-Cross) then you can position yourself to legitimately sit on the bench until someone decides to use an M1 or damage you.
|-
|[[File:Rock.png|thumb|Rock (Found everywhere)]]
|A simple large rock, it has three separate rocks with ascending sizes merged together. There is currently no interaction with the rocks, it doesn't have an [[IWO]], and there is no interact button on it.

== Structures Or Buildings ==
These are much larger areas or structures that can be entered, interacted, or just for show.

They include:

{| class="fandom-table wikitable"
! |'''<font color="white"> Image </font>'''
! |'''<font color="white"> Description </font>'''
|-
|[[File:Pillar.png.png|thumb|Pillar (Found in the four corners of the center arena)]]
|A large and tall concrete pillar with four small bars penetrating the top four corners, as well as a large fire on top of the pillar that never dies out. You can only interact with this through M1s, which will damage the enemy instantly. (see [[IWO]] for pillar)


Landing on the fire part of the pillar and staying on it will cause you to take fire damage and be knocked off of it.
|-
|[[File:Villain bar.png|thumb|Villain Bar (Outside)[[File:Inside villain bar.png|thumb|Villain Bar (Inside)]]]]
|A secret building found in the direction behind the leaderboard statues. From the outside, it looks like a metal door with many bricks spreading out of it, as well as a sign above it saying "NOT A SECRET HIDEOUT" and also a sign on a rock next to the door saying "this is not a villain hangout" when you interact with the door by pressing E (On PC/laptop) the user will knock three times, before punching the door away. Inside, there is a large bar with many shelfs full of wine and beer, as well as two sets of chairs and tables in front of the bar, and one set of chairs and tables on the right of the bar. There is a stage on the right front corner of the bar, with a sign saying "secret hideout". There will be many paintings everywhere on the walls featuring fanart of the Heroes Battlegrounds community. In here, there are two objects that can be interacted, the TV, on the right of the bar, and the Jukebox, on the left of the entering door.
|-
|[[File:Treee.png|thumb|Tree (Found everywhere in the map)]]
|A large tree with green leaves, there is no other interaction other that its [[IWO]], which uses an M1 to an enemy next to the tree.
The tree has two variants excluding {{Hero Slayer}}'s exclusive [[IWO]]. One allows you to combo extend by punching the opponent in the stomach and grabbing the head of the opponent and throwing then into the tree.
The other does 3 punches to the right of the opponent and lands one final punch knocking the opponent very far away.
attempting to climb the tree will cause you to get knocked off of it.
|-
|[[File:Leaderboard.png|thumb|Leaderboard (Found at in the direction of the front of the Villain Bar)]]
|The leader board contains two transparent dark boards on rocks with yellow text. The smaller board contains the leaderboard for players in ranked lobbies, while the larger board contains the leaderboard for players in public servers. The larger board will have the top three players as damageable but unkillable statues under the board, the statues will be the avatars of the top three players, the second and third player will have a pose that kneels down at the first player, and the first player will have a champion-like standing pose.
The statues can be killed using hundreds of ultimate moves and will take hours to do. The statues will ragdoll and shatter to pieces.
|}

Techniques (OLD): == Overview== Techniques are combinations of any attacks that act as a combo extender, usually being created and imagined by the community, in this case, the Heroes Battlegrounds community. Techniques always take advantage of hitboxes, though some of them are combinations of moves that won't normally work and require precision to do. Some of the techniques are mostly useless, some of them are mostly useful, but no matter what, using them might give you an advantage. Either it'll make you look '''''flashy & swift''''', or it'll grant you combat benefits, as an example - stunning your opponent to the point they cannot escape your combo extender or giving you abruptness (unpredictability). ==One For All (Will work with each character you'd use.)== |-|Loop Dash= Once performing 3 M1's, you can uppercut your opponent and circle around them when using Forward Dash, thus dealing a little more damage and leaving your opponent mostly confused at first time. '''Tips:''' Use it whenever you're low on health, so your opponent will be rolled away while you have some time to retreat or regenerate your HP. '''Pros:''' If your opponent will use Evasive, you have a slight chance to catch them up with your wasted Forward Dash, allowing you to M1 them more, and if accessible, reel them into a combo. '''Cons:''' Instead of still catching your opponent and M1'ing them again, you might just waste your Forward Dash, if your opponent has Ragdoll Cancel (Evasive). [[File:LoopDash.gif|200px]] |-|Downslam Loop= Similar to the '''Loop Dash''', after performing 3 M1's, perform a downslam and circle towards the opponents back as fast as possible with a forward dash. '''Tips:''' Just like its uppercut version, it's best used to chip damage while creating distance to give you some space to breath. '''Pros:''' Being faster and can be used after a downslam due to enemy bouncing upwards, thus allowing you to hit them to chip away their health; sometimes the opponent might ragdoll cancel during their rollback due to the unexpected loop dash. '''Cons:''' Very inconsistent with high ping and leaves you very vulnerable if performed too late, especially if the opponent has their Ragdoll Cancel (Evasive). The Prototype:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:<span style="background:linear-gradient(90deg,#8B8000,#8B8000,#FFFFFF,#FFFFFF); -webkit-background-clip:text !important; -webkit-text-fill-color:transparent;"><span style="-webkit-text-stroke: .9px black;"><big>'''The Prototype (Boss)'''</big>}}

{{Boss|Bossstuff={{The Prototype}}|image1=NamuMHB.png|spawn_=|speed=20|health=500|caption-image1=Spawns every 5 hours in public and 30 mins in Private Servers}}

== '''Introduction''' ==
{{The Prototype}} is a Boss with 500 health added along with a special character named {{Warp Portal}}, being directly linked to the boss; it is the only way to obtain access to it. Killing {{The Prototype}} will give you a small chance of obtaining {{Warp Portal}}. If you don't get the character, your [[pity]] will be increased by 3, this pity increases your chances of obtaining {{Warp Portal}} (If number in notification in the left of the screen is lower than your pity, you get Warp Portal). For some, it may take around 6 to even 12 tries to obtain [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G8sgJbm9yrQ Warp Portal], as it is all luck-based (There is a initial 7% chance of getting {{Warp Portal}}, as the chance goes up with your [https://heroes-battlegrounds.fandom.com/wiki/Pity pity]). Defeating {{The Prototype}} will also grant any players using {{Green Hero}}, {{Hero Slayer}},  {{Azure Flames}} and {{Split Ice}}, '''5/10/13%''' [[Mastery|character mastery]] based on damage placement.

=='''Information'''==
{{The Prototype}} spawns in 30 minutes in Private Servers, while in Public Servers it spawns every 3-5 hours. {{The Prototype}} will despawn after 40 minutes. It can also be spawned with '''485 robux'''. After spawning it, it has a 5 second cooldown after killing it, and using respawn gamepasses will not work until the cooldown is over. Currently, it has around 500 HP but this goes up with each player.

In private servers, depending on how fast you kill it, it will spawn in around 10-30 minutes, it is based on how fast you kill it, this is because when it spawns is based on server up time, if it spawned at 0:30, it would spawn again at 1:00, as long as {{The Prototype}} was killed before the next spawn time in the server. {{The Prototype}} has 20 minutes cooldown, and this is maximum delay between each {{The Prototype}} if at least three was beaten earlier.''

If any PS+ Settings are on, it will be disabled to make {{The Prototype}} fight fair, making it harder for others to get {{Warp Portal}} as PS+ will be disabled until he's dead.

When you kill {{The Prototype}} you get the badge "Prototype Slayer". Every badge grants you an emote, so that means this badge will also give you an emote.

The top dealer damage to {{The Prototype}} is granted 2 emotes, the 2nd is granted 1 emote.

Sometimes. A bug In the system rarely happens, granting the 1st damage dealer a kill after {{The Prototype}} is defeated. (Happens In Private Servers Too)

=='''Things to note:'''==
*Having teammates can be used to near guarantee a top 3 spot, as they prevent others from damaging during this time as well.

*{{The Prototype}} has 500 HP as of the current update. The move OVERCHARGE does 50% to a normal player, only doing 10% to {{The Prototype}} currently. It previously did 6% way back around the period of when he was first added, making him far tougher.

*M1'ing {{The Prototype}} is very risky, and you can't combo him for too long because he'll just buff himself when stunned by a combo-extender.

* It is best to approach and attack {{The Prototype}} using front dashes, due to normally M1'ing him being risky and dangerous, and front dashes act as a set up for M1's.

*All of {{The Prototype}}'s attacks can be countered when in melee range excluding Furious Roar. (see attacks below)

*You can M1 trade with {{The Prototype}}, but this is risky considering he occasionally does moves after a singular M1.

*{{The Prototype}}'s Raging Strikes attack (see attacks below) can be avoided if you somehow manage to side dash and immediately backroll, although this is unlikely considering how fast the move comes out. For whatever reason, {{The Prototype}} almost ALWAYS does this attack after being hit with Curdle Pierce or Sanguine Symphony as a means to stop you before you can utilize the stun.

*{{The Prototype}} is best fought with a small amount of people, but not a full public server.
*{{The Prototype}} gives players the same ultimate gain as the Weakest Dummy upon dealing damage to it.

*{{Explosion Hero}}'s [[Ultimate Mode|ultimate]] is good for killing {{The Prototype}} because of having two ultimate movesets.

*{{The Prototype}} will occasionally ignore M1 stun and front dash stun, depending on ping.
*{{The Prototype}}'s Thunderclap (See below) is blockable, and will still send you upwards. Use it to maximize the damage of moves that scale with height. (Electric Smash of Mastered Green Hero, Hyper of Green Hero and etc.)
*{{The Prototype}} can use it's moves (Ferocious Slam precisely) while using Relentless Pummel, or while being grabbed itself. Do keep it in mind.

*{{The Prototype}} always tries to pursuit their opponent even if the opponent/player is in I-Frames, so when it gets closer to you, use Tenth Torch on The Prototype for 20% damage.
*{{Hero Slayer}}'s Crimson Parry triggers The Prototype's Furious Roar. Either try to use this move as carefully as possible, or either refrain from using it in general as to evade {{The Prototype}} from healing itself after using the move.

*{{The Prototype}} will be teleported into the middle of the map if it isn't being attacked for a period of time.

* Thanks to his passive ability, you can use {{Green Hero}} to charge your ultimate before switching to another character like {{Explosion Hero}}. only about 75% carries over.

* Due to how {{The Prototype}} handles rollback, it is possible to extend an M1 string by front-dashing after the 3rd M1. 
* Additionally, moves like {{Green Hero}}'s Overkick, {{Explosion Hero}}'s Ground Cannon (Second use), {{Mastered Green Hero}}'s Black Whip, etc. will have their rollback distance decreased significantly, allowing for M1 extension.

== Character Tips to fight the {{The Prototype}} ==
{{Book}}

* '''{{Green Hero}}''': It is recommended to use mainly skills like Focus Shot and Hyper, since these two skills are useful to deal high damage instances in a moment to then retreat easily (Using Focus Shot's Aerial Variant is useful to avoid most of '''''The Prototype's''''' attacks, but damage-wise it's lackluster). It's awakening even though able to do quite a lot of damage requires a lot of spacing to not get interrupted while using all skills except for '''Cosmic Smash''', as all the other moves can get countered by an stray hit from the The Prototype or a grab. If you '''DON'T''' have {{Mastered Green Hero}} its recommended to fight '''''The Prototype''''' with Green hero, '''since you get 5/7/10/13%''' [[mastery]], as it's the most effective and consistent way to do so. 

* {{Mastered Green Hero}}: Having three of the moves that are grabs, it's easy to put out damages without worrying about interruptions. Due to how {{The Prototype}} deals with rollbacks, it's possible to use Black Whip as a combo starter and as an extender at a safe distance (inconsistent with high ping). Quintuple Smash is great for bypassing Ferocious Slam for an easy combo starter. Just like before, the range for the roll is reduced, eliminating the need for side-dashing. Whenever The Prototype performs the Thunderclap and assuming you've blocked it, it is possible to perform Electric Smash at the very peak to dish out maximum damage. While his awakening is not as good as its [[Green Hero|base counterpart]], its still possible to dish out preferable damage. However, it's better to keep a safe distance to performs moves like 120% and Whiplash, because of how {{The Prototype}} constantly approaches the player, thus causing you to miss.

*'''{{Hero Slayer}}''': The base moveset has a good mid ranged move and grab that allows you to score a good amount of damage without worrying of interruptions. Even though Curdle Pierce seems like a bad move, it can actually be really helpful. When {{The Prototype}} uses Thunderclap, see attacks below, since {{The Prototype}} goes under you, you are able to time Curdle Pierce just right so that you hit {{The Prototype}}. You can also do the same with Ferocious Slam, albeit, with a bit harder timing. However, other moves have high chances to interrupt it and cancel the move entirely. The '''[[Ultimate Mode|awakening]]''' is extremely op, as Crimson Parry negates all it's moves if used correctly, as well as good close-mid ranged moves. Just like {{Green Hero}}, you can obtain mastery for '''{{Mastered Hero Slayer}}, which will also give 5/7/10/13% [[Mastery]].'''  

* {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}: While you have two movesets to work with, the Katana stance is much more preferable. This is because how {{The Prototype}} treats rollback. Meaning if you use Shadow Rush Air Variant fast enough after a downslam, it would extend as long as {{The Prototype}} had already used Reckless Plummet, see moves below. Other than that, Katana Throw's "Take Back" becomes a proper extender. Much like its [[Hero Slayer|non-mastered version]], Whirldwind Pierce seemed useless at first, it's very useful upon successfully blocking The Thunderclap. You should also get used to not using One Million Slashes and Uppercut as both move will enrage {{The Prototype}}. Its Awakening is surprisingly good, moves like Detrimental Division and Gekido both have short cooldown, Cull The Evil is best used for last or waiting for the other cooldowns. Do be careful when using your M1s as it could also enrage The Protoype.

*'''{{Split Ice}}''': The abilities {{Split Ice}} has causes the FREEZE debuff, which when applied to {{The Prototype}}, greatly decreases his threat level and creates more time for attacks from others. This also helps if you are attempting to take him on solo, as it will give you more opportunity to land hits and back away from his attacks.

* {{Mastered Split Ice}}: TBA

*{{Explosion Hero}}: His awakening deals a lot of damage (For example, Exploding Star), and the Tenth Torch moveset (and Tenth Torch itself) deals '''extremely high''' damage (Example: Blast Away deals 20% damage). Through the damage of the activation of the first awakening, second awakening, and first three moves of Tenth Torch; will bring it down to less than 10% of its health remaining. 

* {{Decaying Hatred}}: His abilities will all stun {{The Prototype}}, and both Deteriorate and Void Steps can be used to avoid the large explosion caused by Furious Roar, as well as all of the non-boosted moves being counterable (Debris Toss will not deal damage but instead be negated past a certain range). His TRUE EVIL ultimate move can be used to great effect, as it deals incredibly high damage if you can land all of the QTE's. Radio Waves does not disable {{The Prototype}}'s moves, but still deals damage.

* '''{{Full Throttle}}''': While there is absolutely no reason to use it, unless you like challenges, its one of the worst characters to use against {{The Prototype}}. Recipro Assault and Sound Breaker are excellent to dish out easy damage from a safe distance. The same moves (Aerial Sound Breaker) are also good at interrupting the charged attacks. If you front dash fast enough after Recipro Assault towards {{The Prototype}}, it's possible to continue your combo; this is very risky. You should also avoid using Turbine Drive and Jet Stream as much as possible, as the moves don't function properly against {{The Prototype}}. You should only use your awakening when your health is low, because most of the moves are very hard to land aside from Jet Storm. However, when you successfully block the Thunderclap, (see attacks below) and got high up into the air, it's possible to activate Chronos without interruptions.

==Misc Information:==
*It is highly recommended to fill up the awakening bar before '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' spawns, since awakening attacks usually deal high damage (the best ones are {{Split Ice}}, {{Hero Slayer}} and {{Explosion Hero}} (Tenth Torch is very good).

*Don’t try to get close to '''''{{The Prototype}}''''', as there’s a chance that the boss will charge a smash attack that deals ''AOE'' damage, this however can be countered by landing a grab/cutscene move. Sometimes you have a chance to do some mid damage when you are close to it. Using any ranged skill to attack from afar is good for the most part.

* The '''{{The Prototype}}''' will run and focus its attacks towards the nearest player instead of the one who deals the most recent damage instance so, if you were to try and get the boss' aggro off of a player, simply move close by.

* It is not recommended to use combo-extenders on the boss, as it will use an attack (Furious Roar) that will push all nearby players away. After that, it will be enraged (shown via a red outline) which will increase its damage, speed, heal the most recent amount of damage dealt to it, and make him be stunned for less time by all attacks with the exception of grabs/cutscene moves.

* (Only for getting mastery for {{Green Hero}}, {{Hero Slayer}} and {{Azure Flames}}) Then: It is suggested that you use {{Azure Flames}} or {{Explosion Hero}} to deal more damage, or for other players to use {{Azure Flames}} and {{Explosion Hero}} since they deal high damage, then switch back to {{Green Hero}} or {{Hero Slayer}} if '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' is on low health to get the mastery for them.

* For fighting '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' solo it is best to use {{Hero Slayer}}, as his ult is long and has a high damage output.
* If you fought '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' and won (in a private server) and didn't get any reward, try to join a public server and that should probably do the trick.

== Moves ==
* '''Basic M1's''': A series of hits, which is slower than player's M1's, yet they deal more damage. The last hit could be either downslam, which rolls you away usually, either uppercut, which could make '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' strike you from above. However, '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' can replace last hit with '''Raging Strikes''' or '''Brutish Slams''', leading to more damage taken. M1's are obviously blockable, but due to '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' having chance to replace it with one of two early mentioned attacks, blocking is usually useless. When trading M1's with '''''{{The Prototype}}''''', if he hits you, immediately side dash away, because it can then use '''Brutish Slams'''.
* '''Basic M1's Tip''': Never trade M1's with '''{{The Prototype}},''' as his M1's are basically the first thing '''{{The Prototype}}''' will focus on doing, before using Raging Strikes or Brutish Slams.
*

* '''Raging Strikes''': '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' roars, before unleashing a chain of slash-like attacks with red effects, before either slamming the player down, either uppercutting them, which leads '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' to strike the player from above. Unblockable, and is capable of catching more than 1 target.
* '''Raging Strikes' Tip''': Whenever the boss roars, it will be indicated by circle-like shockwave appear in his head, and when the start-up occurs, do your best to dash away backwards, and then to side dash away for not even one hit to get you. When move ends, you can forward dash at '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' freely, unless he'll do '''Wild Lunge''', which could waste your forward dash, and perhaps damage you.

* '''Brutish Slams''': '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' swipes in front of itself, and if any player is near - it catches the nearest player and slams them into the ground repeatedly, up to 4 times. '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' then dangles the player by the leg, and stares at them before throwing them away with a roar. Unblockable.
* '''Brutish Slams' Tip''': There's no real way to avoid it, simply just hope that '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' won't ignore your stun and use it, otherwise you'll get constantly slammed into the ground.

* '''Thunderclap''': {{The Prototype}} reels its arms back, before performing a clap-like gesture to the front, which is powerful enough to send out a huge shockwave in fan-shaped area, which flings caught players upwards. Blockable.
* '''Thunderclap's Tip''': When approaching '''''{{The Prototype}}''''', mostly it will be his first move, so don't block it, just side dash away from the range and forward dash to boss' back.

* '''Debris Toss''': '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' stomps off a chunk of debris from the ground and kicks it away, or rips it off and throws it at player. Blockable, and deals mild DMG(5% HP usually). Cannot kill you, no matter what.
* '''Debris Toss' Tip''': You can not really pay attention to it, just side dash away when the debris reaches the middle of your distance between your avatar and the boss himself. You can also simply block the debris.

* '''Furious Roar''': Whenever you use barrage-like attack (Ex. Blitz, Thousand Slashes, One Million Slashes, Scarlet Frenzy, Sanguine Symphony, Crimson Parry upon successful activation), or stun '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' for a lot of time (Approximately - 8 seconds of stun), he'll roar, sending out a shockwave, which flings everyone nearby away for a considerable distance. The roar will heal '''''{{The Prototype}}''''''s health, depending on how much he lost (Thus barrage-like attacks is useless, cause the boss will just heal the whole damage out of it back), and also will buff his speed and strength, indicated by bright red outline surrounding him temporarily.
* '''Furious Roar's Tip''': It's simple, like really. Just don't use barrage-like attacks and you're fine.

* '''Wild Lunge''': The boss firstly roars, before doing one of variants: FIRST // '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' lunges forward enough to catch up to player's current spot and slams the ground, making the player roll away really far. SECOND // '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' lunges forward and lifts his hand up, which uppercuts the player, and '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' uses the moment then to strike the player from above.
* '''Wild Lunge's Tip''': It is easy to avoid, simply just immediately side dash away. However, both variants are unblockable and sometimes, if dashing at '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' and he'll use the move, consider your Forward Dash spent worthless.

* '''Ferocious Slam''': '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' roars while slowly lifting his hands to the sky, before slamming the ground in a powerful way, which launches players upwards, and strikes nearby one's from above to the ground. Unblockable.
* '''Ferocious Slam's Tip''':Better dash away backwards (Lit. Q + S together) from the range and then Forward Dash back at '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' to continue your combo. If too late, use the moves that give you Armor (Ex. Blackout Clutch, Explosion Combo, Blast Catapult, Subzero's Uppercut & Normal variants, Shadow Rush, Deteriorate, Quintuple Smash, Shadow Whip, Focus Shot Normal Variant, and so on, most ultimate moves that has cutscene or Armor are included, too), or mobility moves that make you escape (Ex. Vicious Grab Air Variant right before the impact).

* '''Reckless Pummel''': Unique stun will force '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' to take a stance and wind his arms back, before lunging directly at the player and changing directions if dodging to the side, and when catching the player, the boss will slam them to the ground and rapidly punch them, before delivering a last, very powerful blow that could deal AoE damage to nearby players.
* '''Reckless Pummel's Tip''': Attacks like 4 M1, no matter what would you do, Downslam, Uppercut or normal last hit, will cause him to activate the move, and when you see his arms pulled back, backdash away. Using Explosion Combo, backdashing is futile - better extend it with immediate Blast Catapult, thus the move will be ignored by cutscene. Also it will be used whenever you land Whirlwind Pierce or Curdle Pierce, because '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' won't let you utilize the stun, so that way - backdash away when hit, and when the '''Reckless Pummel's''' start-up has failed, use the stun and do your combo.

== Trivia ==
* When you learn his abilities, they will be very easy to avoid, that way fighting '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' in private server will be WAY easier, rather than with players in public, that'll do everything to deal at least 1%, ignoring the fact they damage other players, the boss just makes them desperate and reckless to defeat him and take first place.

* Fighting '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' in private server not only saves a lot of time (30 minutes cooldown each spawn), it also makes incredibly faster grinding mastery, rather than kills and quests, thus if you rather to play in private server instead of in public, the public servers could see you as 0 kills player,  who already has all three mastered characters.

* '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' is based off of the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Nomu_(U.S.J.) Nomu]<sup>[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Nomu.]</sup> that debuted on My Hero Academia's USJ Arc.

* It´s Thunderclap move might be a reference to The Hulk's Thunderclap in The Incredible Hulk (2008) movie.

* When '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' spawns, he'll create a huge crater that sends players, and the dummy away, and then roars. Former cutscene was faster and visible, and formerly players could see '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' falling from the sky.

* The Brutish Slams move is based on what The Hulk does to Loki in the "Avengers" movie.

* Formerly it was stated that '''''{{The Prototype}}''''' has his own death cutscene if getting defeated by '''1,000,000%'''.
* In Halloween, {{The Prototype}} has a pumpkin head, and during Christmas, it's a Santa Hat, in which you can claim as an accessory during the event.
The Strongest:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''The Strongest'''}} [[Category:Characters]] {{Quote|Throughout Heaven and Earth.. I alone am the honored one.| - [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Satoru_Gojo Satoru Gojo]}} {{Character_heros|GreenHero='''{{The Strongest}}'''|image1= Strong.webp|Icon GojoTSbase.png|Base TheHonoredOne.png|Awakening |awakening_name='''Awakening'''|cost=499 Robux (Private Server +) Free (If obtained via The Strongest NPC)|NameOfCharacter={{The Strongest}}|caption-image1=The Honored One}} ==Description== {{The Strongest}} is a special character in Heroes Battlegrounds based on the immensely powerful sorcerer [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Satoru_Gojo '''Satoru Gojo'''] from the series Jujutsu Kaisen. True to his inspiration, this character possesses overwhelmingly potent abilities, focusing on high burst damage, unique defensive capabilities and devastating area-of-effect attacks in his awakened state. == Ways to Obtain == There are two methods to obtain {{The Strongest}}: 1. '''The Strongest NPC (Daily Chance):''' Players can interact with the Gojo NPC once every 24 hours. * If the [[NPC's|NPC]] responds with "'''Better luck in 24 hours'''," the player did '''not''' receive the character and must try again the next day. * If the NPC says "'''YES YES YOU ARE... at least for 24 hours'''", the player has successfully unlocked the character for a temporary 24-hour period, usable in both public and private servers. The chance of obtaining the character this way is extremely low (approximately 1 in 1,350,000, or 0.000074%). 2. '''Private Server Plus Gamepass:''' Purchasing the [[Private Server Commands|Private Server Plus]] gamepass for 499 Robux grants permanent access to {{The Strongest}}, but '''only within the owner's private server'''. == Keep in Mind == * If obtained via the NPC, your character will be switched back to {{Green Hero}} (or your default character) if you are playing on a public server when the 24-hour duration expires, and you cannot switch back without obtaining it again. * If {{The Prototype}} boss spawns while you are playing as {{The Strongest}}, your character will be automatically switched to {{Green Hero}}. ==Pros and Cons== ===Pros=== * All moves deal exceptionally high, often one-shot, damage. * M1 attacks ('''The Strongest Punches''') are incredibly powerful, capable of defeating opponents in a single combo. * '''Infinity''' provides a near-permanent counter, reflecting most incoming damage back to the attacker. * Possesses a couple of strong mobility options, especially '''Blue''' (if missed). * Ultimate moves ('''Purple''', '''Red''', '''Decapitate''') are devastating and can eliminate multiple opponents in a single blow. * '''Unlimited Void''' offers powerful crowd control by immobilizing enemies caught in its activation. * Most moves gains massive amounts of ult if used on people/dummies. ===Cons=== * Extremely low chance of obtaining from the NPC, making it highly inaccessible for most players through that method. * Permanent access via gamepass is restricted to private servers only. * '''Infinity''' can be bypassed by specific counter moves (e.g., {{Hero Slayer}}'s Crimson Parry). * Forced character switch if {{The Prototype}} spawns. * If obtained via NPC, access is temporary (24 hours). == Movesets == |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" !Moves !Damage !Cooldown !Information |- |[[File:Gojo M1.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''The Strongest Punches (M1)''' |65% (full chain) |2 seconds |The user performs a combo with white visual effects, launching the opponent far away on the last hit. Each hit feels significantly more impactful than other characters' M1s and deals substantial damage. |- |Landed[[File:Gojo Blue.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Blue'''[[File:Gojo Blue Missed.gif|center|frameless|border]]Missed |100% (instant kill) |3 seconds |The user of the "Limitless" cursed technique pulls the opponent to their hand and instantly vaporizes them with blue energy effects. ---- *If missed, the user teleports in the direction they are facing, providing excellent mobility. |- |[[File:Gojo Infinity.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Infinity''' |Equal to the damage of the reflected move (does not work on some counters) |3 seconds |The user activates 'Infinity', creating a barrier that essentially functions as a permanent counter, reverting most incoming damage back to the attacker. ---- *{{Hero Slayer}}'s Crimson Parry can bypass Infinity. *Infinity can reflect {{Explosion Hero}}'s Exploding Star, though the cutscene for Exploding Star will still play for its user. |- |[[File:Gojo Slap.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Slap''' |72% |6 seconds |The user strikes with their elbow, launching the opponent back, then teleports and performs a downslam. |- |[[File:Gojo Combo.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Combo''' |84% |6 seconds |The user unleashes a devastating barrage of punches at incomprehensible speed, ending by striking the enemy back, causing them to roll away. ---- *Cannot hit a downed/ragdolled player. |} |-|Awakening= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background:#d9d9d9;" |'''Moves''' ! style="background:#d9d9d9;" |'''Damage''' ! style="background:#d9d9d9;" |'''Cooldown''' ! style="background:#d9d9d9;" |'''Information''' |- |[[File:Gojo Awakening.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Awakening''' |N/A |N/A |The user falls to the ground, then stands and begins to float upwards with limbs hanging loosely, before eventually landing back down, awakened and ready to unleash their strongest techniques. |- |[[File:Gojo Purple.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Purple''' |100% (instant kill AoE) |OTU (One Time Use - Loses ultimate immediately after use) |The user of the "Limitless" cursed technique combines the properties of 'Red' and 'Blue' to form a giant purple sphere of energy that evaporates everything in its path. Best used to target multiple opponents. |- |[[File:Gojo Domain.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Unlimited Void''' |0.8% (initial hit, then passive DoT within domain if applicable) |~30 seconds |The user makes a hand sign, one of their eyes flashes blue, and creates a small dome representing their Domain Expansion, Unlimited Void. Players caught in the domain's activation are immobilized. |- |[[File:Gojo Red.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Red''' |100% (instant kill AoE) |3 seconds |The user extends their arm to the side, forming a red ball of cursed energy at their fingertips, then points their arm forward and shoots it out, causing a large red explosion. |- |[[File:Gojo Decapitate.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Decapitate''' |100% (instant kill) |12 seconds |The user teleports behind a nearby target and instantly rips their head off, decapitating them. This move has a limited effective range. |} == Trivia == * {{The Strongest}} is widely considered the most overpowered character currently in the game due to his exceptionally high damage output and unique defensive capabilities. * He is statistically the hardest character to unlock via the NPC method, with an approximate 1 in 1,350,000 chance (or 0.000074%). * The '''Infinity''' move can reflect almost every attack back at the person who used it, including most cutscene-based moves, causing the attacker to damage themselves or get caught in their own animations. However, it is not infallible and can be bypassed by certain specific counter-type moves like {{Hero Slayer}}'s Crimson Parry. * This character is directly based on [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Satoru_Gojo Satoru Gojo] from the popular anime and manga series [https://jujutsu-kaisen.fandom.com/wiki/Jujutsu_Kaisen_Wiki Jujutsu Kaisen]. * The '''Awakening''' (ultimate activation) for {{The Strongest}} is one of three ultimates in the game that does not deal damage to other players upon activation, the others being {{Green Hero}}'s Maximum Overdrive and {{Warp Portal}}'s Quantum Jump. * The character model and some animations were reportedly created from previously unused assets. * As of current community tracking, only a very small number of players (reportedly around 30) have ever successfully obtained {{The Strongest}} through the NPC interaction. {{Characters}} [[Category:Special Characters]] [[Category:Private Server+ Characters]]

Tier List:
==This page is made by mono_gramm ([[User:The1S0ver31gnty|Sovereign]]).==
* '''In this page, you will see how much damage deals one moveset without using any other damaging means (Aerial Downslam, Ultimate Mode start-up (Unless TENTH TORCH, because it's partly a move), M1's and Dash), including absolutely everything except for variants.'''
** '''''If you want to see how much the character deals with surely everything (Including everything except for other damaging means that is listed above), it will be shown here, too.'''''

==Tier List==
 '''''First Place''''' // [[Mastered Hero Slayer]] - '''The moveset is already versatile and powerful, dealing <big>67%</big> with all 7 moves (And Black Flash passive). However, if including both damage variants of Whirlwind Pierce and move variants - it will be <big>102%</big>, thus making the character bear the second strongest base moveset.'''
* '''''Conviction''''' - '''Because the character's Awakening is not done yet, it deals <big>95%</big>, exactly the same damage can deal its normal counterpart.'''

 '''''Second Place''''' // [[Warp Portal]] - '''The worst character, as the community says, and can deal the easiest One Shot combo without waiting for cooldowns at all, dealing <big>62%</big>. If counting in the damage from every single object Matter Absorption can regurgitate, the damage will be <big>141%</big>, thus making the character the strongest.. and the most deranged in terms of damage.
* '''''Quantum Jump''''' - '''The worst awakening, even Sovereign can say so. Deals <big>135%</big> and can dish the easiest One Shot if the enemy lacks Evasive, but the most of the moves can be dodged or blocked.'''

 '''''Third Place''''' // [[Symbol of Fear]] - '''Deals absolutely clear <big>60%</big> damage with its tricky and versatile moveset, and without counting other damaging means, such clear damage is too much.'''
* '''''Everything For One''''' - '''Fearsome and deadliest Awakening with powerful and brutal-looking moves, which deals absolutely clear <big>179%</big> (If not counting TRUE EVIL, it'll be <big>79%</big>, obviously.).'''

 '''''Fourth Place''''' // [[Explosion Hero]] - '''This moveset is powerful, but requires for the opponent to lack Evasive, due to moves lacking True combo potential at most. Overall, the damage is as clear as for Symbol of Fear, but deals <big>51%</big> instead.'''
* '''''Fury Ignition''''' - '''Without TENTH TORCH, the moveset deals <big>89%</big> damage, and with TENTH TORCH - <big>204%</big>. Formerly, it dealt <big>280%</big> damage, resulting into whopping <big>369%</big> damage.
** '''''TENTH TORCH''''' - '''The moveset is naturally the strongest in Heroes Battlegrounds, if not counting [[The Strongest]]. Deals <big>233%</big>, but if counting the damage from BLITZ KICK without Secondary Use, and BLAST AWAY without a grab damage, it will result into <big>325%</big> damage. Not counting NUKE's lesser damage (<big>91%</big> instead of <big>99%</big>), because it's being a One-Time Use.
 '''''Fifth Place''''' // [[Jet Engine]] - '''Despite the character not being finished yet, it still deals a whopping <big>47%</big> damage, and if considering every single variant of Recipro Assault - it will be <big>77%</big> damage.'''
* '''''Beyond Speed''''' - '''Deals the hugest damage <big><big><big><big><big>(DAMN 0%)</big></big></big></big></big> in the game ever possible with just standing still. And on top of that, Jet Engine's Awakening and every single move there does True combos regardless everything.'''

 '''''Sixth Place''''' // [[Green Hero]] - '''First character to be released into the game, yet still deals only <big>44%</big> damage. If counting the variants though, it will deal <big>60%</big> damage, simply put.'''
* {{Ruby|'''''Maximum Overdrive'''''|'''''<sub>(Formerly - "Ultimate")</sub>'''''}} - '''A perfect blend of offense and defense, deals devastating damage with all moves in fair trade of being hard to land, dealing a whopping <big>180%</big> damage, and being able to One Shot with only two moves used maximally.'''

 '''''Seventh Place''''' // [[Split Ice]] - '''Despite the moveset's nature being somewhat clunky and somewhat odd, it still deals mild amount of damage - <big>39%</big>. By including all variants from Sub Zero - it will turn into monstrous <big>86%</big> damage.'''
* '''''Burning Flames''''' - '''Being as clunky as the base, it still deals a lot - <big>160%</big> damage, but requires huge aim precision overall. And as ''Maximum Overdrive'', the moveset can One Shot with only two moves used.'''

 '''''Eighth Place''''' // [[Mastered Green Hero (Vigilante Deku)|Mastered Green Hero]] - '''Both the best and the worst character, being weaker than its normal counterpart and yet being able to One Shot, the moveset deals <big>37%</big> damage, and <big>51%</big> if counting the variants.'''
* '''''THE NINTH''''' - '''Punishing moveset that can punish yourself upon incorrect and reckless use, being able to oneshot from either three, either four moves, depending on what exactly was been used. Being weaker than normal counterpart even here, it deals only <big>117%</big>.'''

 '''''Ninth Place''''' // [[Hero Slayer]] - '''Despite its awful damage <big>(33%)</big>, it still is capable of One Shotting, if the user slows down everything after [[Techniques|Not Done Yet]] Tech. Counting Curdle Pierce damage without stun and Triple Dagger Throw in the air, the character will deal <big>51%</big> damage.'''
* '''''Conviction''''' - '''It's obviously <big>95%</big> damage, sharing the same Awakening with its [[Mastery|mastery]] counterpart. If catching into Sanguine Symphony + Scarlet Frenzy combo, your opponent can be theoretically dead (If they was damaged or you did mono_gramm's Ultimate Combo.).'''

 '''''Tenth Place''''' // [[Azure Flames]] - '''Was getting ridiculous nerfs, resulting into being unplayable at all and being unable to deal with teamers anymore, dealing pathetic <big>31%</big> damage, and besides clearly.
* '''''SPITE''''' - '''The character's Awakening is only thing that makes them be somewhat alive, because the moveset deals ''CLEAR'' <big>138%</big> damage.'''

Ultimate Mode:
{{Stub}}
==Ultimate Bar==
Whenever you deal damage to someone (Player, Weakest Dummy, Leaderboard #1-3 Avatars), you gain charge in your Ultimate Bar, and when it's filled to 100%, you can activate your Ultimate Mode by pressing G on keyboard, D-Pad up on controller or tapping the Ultimate Bar itself on mobile. When switching characters, the Ultimate Bar will be depleted by 1/4, and switching constantly can bring it to complete zero.

Upon entering Ultimate Mode, a cutscene will play (for most characters) on your character along with exclusive music attached to it, with some cutscenes dealing damage to players in the surrounding area. After the cutscene ends, you gain access to your destructively powerful Ultimate Moves. Each character has different Ultimate Moves, devastating attacks that can turn the tide of any battle if used correctly. Also, your Side Dashes and Back Dashes are placed on shorter cooldowns on use.

The Ultimate Mode will last for approximately 50 seconds, and it's visible by the Ultimate Bar slowly draining out to zero, which will make you go back to normal and return your usual moveset. Currently, only Hero Slayer (non-mastered) is capable of extending his Ultimate Mode duration by using the Indomitable Will move.

Example of the Ultimate Bar:[[File:Ult 3.1.jpg|border|left|frameless|168x168px|Example of an Ultimate bar]][[File:New ultimate mode.png|left|frameless|295x295px −|alt=295x295px−]]



==Trivia/Notes==
*{{Hero Slayer}}, as previously said, is the only character being able to extend his Ultimate Mode duration by using Indomitable Will.

*{{Mastered Green Hero}}, {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} {{Azure Flames}}, {{Split Ice}}, {{Explosion Hero}}, {{Decaying Hatred}}, {{Full Throttle}} and {{Human Architect}} are the only characters, who is capable of damaging nearby players with their activation.

* {{Green Hero}}, {{Mastered Green Hero}}, {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}, {{Split Ice}}, {{Warp Portal}}, {{Decaying Hatred}}, {{Full Throttle}} and {{Human Architect}} have finishers for their Ultimate Moves.

*{{Explosion Hero}} is the first character, to have TWO Ultimate Modes whenever the first ultimate is activated and when '''TENTH TORCH''' bar is filled.
**The second one is {{Human Architect}}.

*{{Mastered Hero Slayer}}, {{Mastered Azure Flames}} and {{Mastered Split Ice}} all harness two normal movesets at time, making their arsenal extremely wide in a pay-off for small damage. {{Mastered Split Ice}} doesn't have its personal ultimate and thus is using its base version.

*{{Decaying Hatred}} is the only character, having a damage boosting passive, which is depending on the user's luck.

*{{Human Architect}} was the first character to not have the typical teleport dashes. Instead, having the reduced side-dash cool-down all characters now share.

==Ultimate Encounters ==

=={{Green Hero}}==

* OVERCHARGE - The move is unblockable, although you have chances to evade it by either M1'ing your opponent when the start-up plays, or either by Side Dashing at the middle of start-up. The move's range is 9 tiles forward.

* Cosmic States of Smash - Even though the move is incredibly fast, it's still blockable.

* One Energy Surge - Has a very slow start-up and small range (3-4 tiles.), so evading it is easy unless you are stunned by anything.

* 1,000,000% - Move has incredible mobility (31 tiles forward) and is unblockable, but whenever the attack will emit a sparkling particles, you can take your time and Side Dash, because zig-zag rush cannot be directed into any other side. Tanking it is futile - since it deals 55% out of your HP, you might end up being devoid of full HP in an instant.

=={{Mastered Green Hero}}==

* As the cutscene starts, the only you can really do is walk away to not get damage from energy burst. Hiding is futile, because when the cutscene ends, the user of it will see every single player marked on their screen.

* 120% OVERDRIVE - Don't waste your '''Evasive''' unless you know what you doing. It's easy to dodge, by simply Side Dashing when attacker's fist glows.

* 45% - Start-up is visible, and the attacker won't have enough time to curve to you when you'll Side Dash away after the start-up ends. you will have a 50/50 chance of the attacker hitting you if you side dash away if the user attempts to predict your side dash.

* Lightning Crush - The attacker cannot direct themselves after they are launched, also being very visible, so evading it is no problem.

* Whiplash - Is a risky move that might reel you into the death once the attacker knows what he's doing. Side Dash behind your opponent and then Back Dash away. The only time you would have a hard time evading it is if the user is going to attempt to predict you or if it's a console user with lock on.

=={{Hero Slayer}}==

* The Ultimate cutscene deals little to no damage, and rolls you away for a considerable distance. Due to the user being blinded by a roar the animation emits, they won't manage to see you at all unless you're close.

* Crimson Parry - When your opponent is not accurate with it, he'll reveal the counter by the loud sound it produces, and also due to long counter duration that will reveal the counter pose. Time out when the counter ends and then freely attack.

* Scarlet Frenzy - Slow and is counterable by dash and BLITZ KICK. Although, being hit by it will wipe a lot HP from you. If blitz kick is not available then you can just walk backwards away from it because the mobility of it is just walking distance.

* Sanguine Symphony - Awful move, that's what will say the most who was hit by it. Final knife throw is not blockable, but others are. Time your block and find a perfect position to get your finger off F button and Side Dash away, before instantly Back Dashing to your previous direction, thus confusing your opponent. In close range and when hit by final hit - you're in trouble.

* Indomitable Will - When engaging into aggressive gameplay against Hero Slayer users, you might not notice it firstly, but it's very obvious and noticeable at all. Although, when being caught in it's Blood Curdle effect, the consequences is awful as Sanguine Symphony.

== {{Mastered Hero Slayer}} ==

* The slashes at the end of the cutscene deals damage, but it's possible to dodge the slashes by doing Side-Dashes or Aerial Moves (like Detroit, Delaware, Delaware Grab, etc.)

* Detrimental Division - If used after 3 M1s, it's nearly impossible to dodge because the first slash stun you, and you can't evasive when touched by the first slash, then the second slash is nearly guaranteed to touch you if you get touched by the first slash.
** This ultimate used to be bugged, as having infinite ultimate turned on when {{The Prototype}} spawned would result in you losing ultimate M1's and side dashes but keeping the moves. However this seems to have been fixed.
*
* Gekido - Very easy to avoid, just maintain distance and don't dash too early as the move has a lingering hitbox.
* Blitzing Talon - Pretty much impossible to escape when hit by the sword, but otherwise just side + backdash.
* Cull the Evil - Pretty visible start up and isn't true off M1, very easy to dodge. 

=={{Azure Flames}}==
* Don't play too aggressive or too passive, better counter all the moves with mobility & I-Frames moves, like Delaware Grab // AIR VARIANT, Scarlet Frenzy, Indomitable Will, Detroit, and so on.

* AZURE FLAMES:BLITZ - Is a dangerous, unblockable move to be hit with, although if using '''Evasive''', you have a lot of chances to M1 the user before their endlag ends.

* FLAMEWAVE BURST - Take your time and find a perfect moment to dash away from its range. Hopefully, the user cannot direct the flame-exhaling attack, unless being moved by anything. Sometimes the user might miscalculate the range of the move which will allow you to just barely not get hit by it.

* FROSTFLAME ERUPTION - Could be evaded by keeping away from the user, but the ice and explosion is unblockable. After evading this move, any ranged move or dashing towards the opponent can cancel the move.

* PEAKING SCORCH - The strongest move in the loadout dealing around 52% of your HP. It has a medium startup and is blockable.

=={{Split Ice}} // {{Mastered Split Ice}}==

* FREEZEBURN WAVE - Don't play too aggressively and make sure the user cannot hit you with M1's, otherwise you may lose most of your HP, that will be drained easily.

* JET KINDLING - Can be cancelled by grab moves, and by dodging in a perfect moment. [[Mastered Green Hero|Delaware Grab // AIR VARIANT]] is the easiest variant to evade it.

* FROSTFIRE NOVA - Is unblockable, but mostly the users are using the move to catch you or to trap you whenever you're dashing at them, and even Delaware Grab // AIR VARIANT cannot really help at all.

* BLAZING SOUL - Get M1'ed by the user, and you're screwed. Although, you can use Delaware Grab // AIR VARIANT, in order to dodge it, but you're risking to hit the user with it, which might get you into the cutscene.

=={{Explosion Hero}}==

* Better to play passive and confuse your opponent with dashes, because the character's mobility in Ultimate Mode is comically large.

* Eruption Dive - Don't waste your '''Evasive''' and dash with all your best, because the move is hard to aim at dashing opponents. The hitbox appears in short bursts meaning that you still can not get hit by it if the user just goes through you.

* Exploding Star - Normally is evadeable, although being M1'ed and then pulled into the animation is a sure-healthdrain. 

* Infernal Shot - Is easily noticeable, and could be evaded with exact same ease, if picking the perfect moment. The move cannot be directed once shot.

* TENTH TORCH -  <big>NEVER</big> even dare yourself to walk into an animation, because it takes 280 HP from normal player  (28% HP from Namu).

* MINE - There's no real way to evade it, because Side Dash might still pull you into the painful move, and it's mobility and swiftness is just making it worse. 

* BLITZ KICK - Some people cannot understand the move has second usage, but it is dangerous, yet can be evaded by simply dashing in the way to confuse your opponent. If clashing Explosion Hero with any of {{Hero Slayer}}'s Ultimate, they can use the move to parry your Scarlet Frenzy. DO NOT try to punish the user if you end up using evasive out of the move as said before, it has a second usage which means that you might get hit by it and will waste your evasive entirely.

* BLAST AWAY - There is no form to dodge it in a M1 Chain, But if the opponent use it and for some reason you do not get catch by it, Don´t walk into the move, and waste evasive if it catch you.

* NUKE - Some players cannot aim it very well, so let them fly because they likely will hit the walls. But if clashing professional player who utilizes it as last resort while their '''TENTH TORCH''' is down to almost zero, do your literal best to catch them and M1 them, because they are extremely vulnerable to M1's and other moves before huge circle surrounds them.

=={{Decaying Hatred}}==

* Radio Blast - The move is overall very easy to dodge, if your reflexes are sharp. Keeping it short and simple - you can use Side Dash and then Back Dash to avoid being hit just right when the user will fly upward. Otherwise, if you Forward Dashed at the user, but they used it right when you were close, you got no escape at all, the tendrils can reach you from any height but not from any range.

* Scatter - Don't use your '''Evasive''' when you are hit by it just yet, and when the punching part will go, wait for ≈0.5 seconds before using '''Evasive''' and dashing to the side.

* Reflective Counter - Easy to notice, and has a hugely visible start-up, but skilled players mostly use it in the way you will surely M1 them, getting you caught on the counter in the process, so better play passive and attack when the user drops their guard. Sometimes if the user is extremely reliant on counters, then they will most likely downslam or uppercut you and wait for you to M1 them on wakeup so you will trigger the counter.  It is recommended to block instead on wakeup as a shield which prevents you from doing M1s which can allow you to safely wait for the counter to end.

* TRUE EVIL - Has a too slow start-up, but it can do a sure-hit if the user will time it after downslam (That's mostly the only way to not land it raw), you also can easily dodge it, so you might not worry about the move at all. Although, if you were hit by it, just make sure you hit all Quick Time Event buttons, as the move might deal 91% damage when it got only released, but nowadays it One-Shots. The only way that it will one shot is if the user manages to hit all the QTE and the opponent doesn't hit any of them. If you hit all of the defending QTE and the user hit all of the attacking QTE then you will be guaranteed to survive with 1 hp.

== {{Full Throttle}} ==

* Chronos - Go on top of a rock or run and you are fine. Any characters who have aerial variants of moves can use their variant for safety.
* Mach 5 - Stay out of M1 range of the opponent at all costs.
* Turbo Release - Just be unpredictable, side dash and jump around a lot as the move has a small hitbox and is hard to hit.
* Jet Storm - The move only goes straight but goes super far so just side dash.

== {{Human Architect}} ==

* Calamity - Probably the most dangerous move in first ultimate. You can either pray they have terrible aim, or side dash away instantly.
* Creation Field - Pretty easy to dodge, just keep your distance from the opponent and you'll be fine.
* Disfiguration - Also pretty easy to avoid, just keep distance. But if you get hit by this move you are dead.
* Final Fusion - Cutscene, just start running away before they completely transform into the Kaiju. Once they do transform however, just run away

* RUSH - Can be painful to dodge at close range, but if you have distance you should be unharmed. 
* BUTCHER - Most dangerous move in 2nd ultimate, might just want to pray your enemy has horrible aim.
* CREATE - Just run away from the Golem, you can't deal any damage to it.
* RUPTURE - Again if fighting up close, you will get hit by this. The start up isn't that fast but you will get hit if your caught in a M1 chain. If you get hit, just make sure to hit the QTE to avoid taking 100% damage.

=={{Warp Portal}}==
* Matter Barrage - Can typically be easily dodged with good side dash timing, or getting up close and using a I-frame move.

* Reality Collapse - Don't dash yet when start-up starts. Instead, take your time and while you do, just walk in the range of the circle, before instantly dodging out.

* Crush - Is a painful move, and could finish you off right at your 30% with mere ease. Although, when blocking, you will take less damage from it.

* Spatial Fury - Not so really dangerous, just dash away from the opponent,

=={{The Strongest}}==
* Hollow Purple - Has infinite range and comically large width range. DEAD.

* Unlimited Void - Has extremely huge radius, and slows down everybody in range. DEAD.

* Reversal Red - Is barely possible to dodge due to the huge forward radius, although only being avoidable by Side Dashes, in case the server has No Cooldown on. DEAD.

* Decapitate - When you ever approach the user... DEAD.

==Ultimate Conclusion==

* Green Hero - Has unpredictable and swift attacks, that also lets you play aggressive.
* Mastered Green Hero - Is a complete nemesis whenever your opponent has no '''Evasive'''. Every single move is unblockable and fast.
* Hero Slayer - Waste your '''Evasive''', and the user will have a sure-kill.
* Mastered Hero Slayer - Generally easy to avoid if you know how to manage your evasive, VERY scary once you get caught in an M1.
* Azure Flames - Is considered one of the strongest ultimates, if not considered The Strongest. Each move is painful and M1's range can go about 2 tiles with the last M1 being unblockable.
* Split Ice - Tricky one, and yet - extremely painful. However has little to no mobility.
* Explosion Hero - Two Ultimate Modes, incredible mobility, nerve-wracking immense damage, best for {{The Prototype}}, WHO CAN DARE THEMSELVES TO SAY THE ULTIMATE IS AWFUL?
* Warp Portal - Reminds me of Kurogiri's Quirk usage as well, making him somehow different unlike other ultimates. It is really dangerous, if combining Reality Collapse and Crush. And is considered the worst Ultimate Mode ever, due to the damage.
* Decaying Hatred -  Most of the moves are easy to dodge with '''Evasive''', but if you waste '''Evasive''' on an attack that's not TRUE EVIL, my brother, say goodbye to most of your health (or not).
* Full Throttle - Waste your evasive and you'll get pummeled to death very fast.
* Human Architect - Second character to have Two Ultimate Modes like i beg your pardon?
* The Strongest - No survival. Simple as that. Tip: Just run away and spam dashes until ultimate runs out

* '''Running and Dashing Slowed''' - Your health will now impact your movement speed. The lower your health, the slower you will run and dash.
* '''Downslam Buff''' - M1 Downslam can now hit opponents on wakeup, but not when they are ragdolled.
* '''Ult Music Toggle''' - Check your settings to disable ultimate music.
* '''M1 Speed Balancing''' - M1 speed has been balanced across all heroes.
* '''Shop Adjustments''' - The option to buy shirts has been removed.
* '''New Hurt Animations'''
* '''GUI Update''' - A small GUI has been added to indicate when users escape from ragdoll.
* '''Double Side Dash Enhancements''' - Shorter cooldown between dashes.

VIOLET FORM

*People hit by the first proc will be stunned and slowed momentarily.
*Startup is slightly slower.

====Phantom Shot====
*Can now rotate for a short time before the damage starts.
*Increased initial damage (4 -> 6).
*Decreased followup damage (9 -> 4).
====Hell Shot====
*Doesn't cancel downslam/punches (makes comboing into it easier)
*Added finisher.
====Scorching Surge====
*Max distance for deac explosion increased (15 studs -> 17.5 studs).
*Explosion damage is now a range, dealing 1-5 damage depending on your HP.
*Distance, damage, and extra HP per kill bonuses start scaling up when below 70 HP and stop increasing at 15 HP. Before, this only took into consideration how much HP you had compared to your max HP, making the highest damage/distance unattainable because Violet mode deactivates automatically below 15 HP
*Deac explosion can now hit ragdolled targets.Deals 75% of normal damage, does not extend ragdoll.
*Added hyperarmor on activation and deactivation.
*While in Violet Mode, all attacks deal slightly more damage the lower your HP is.
*HP loss rate increased (0.75/sec -> 0.9/sec).
*HP loss is reduced to 25% of normal rate when ragdolled.
*Particles on deactivation now properly scale with the size of the explosion.

====Infernal Assault====
*Cooldown increased slightly (20s -> 22s).
*Third stance can now block break.
*Added endlag if the move whiffs.

====Hell Assault====
*Added endlag if the move whiffs.
*Added another hitbox at the end of the move to better match the effects.

===Jet Engine===
====Passive====
*Reworked slightly: landing the multikick (4th M1) will now delete your oldest cooldown.
*When this happens, multikick damage is reduced (14 -> 7).

====Jet Stream====
*Now slams down instantaneously, should be easier to hit

===Miscellaneous===
*Added a new version of the Mastery UI that lets you claim quests without going to the shop NPC.Killed the shop NPC forever as a result of this.
*Kill emotes now delay the respawn timer.
*Added the ability to skip kill emotes via a button in the bottom right.
*Extended the time availability of the current limited-time emotes (the next batch we had lined up isn't quite ready yet )
*Fixed numerous bugs, including Custom Move Text not saving and kill emotes not looking right for the victim.

VIP:
{{Gamepass|title1=VIP|image1=VIP.jpg|cost=799 Robux}}

'''VIP''' is a [[Gamepasses|Gamepass]] in the game [[Heroes Battlegrounds]].

== What you obtain ==

* '''Emote Slots''': Get five extra emote slots for five more emotes.
* '''Emotes''': You get 3 new emotes with your VIP.
* '''Unlock Emotes Faster''': With VIP, you can unlock emotes with just 35 kills instead of 50 kills.
* '''Custom Effect Color''': Customize your effect color with VIP
* '''Custom Killstreak Color''': You are able to change your killstreak color or keep it
* '''Custom Awakening Outfits''': You are able to change your awakening outfits in the settings.
* '''Chat Annoucement''': A special text in chat will be announced when you join the game.
* '''VIP Title''': Whenever you talk in chat, you will have a VIP icon next to your name. You will also be able to toggle a VIP Icon above your head.
* '''VIP Cape''': The White color is set to default when you buy VIP. Upon going to settings, you can toggle it if you can equip it or not. To customize your cape color, you have to go to "VIP Cape Image/Color" in settings which you can customize the color and the image you want to put on your cape.
Villains:
Villains are characters based off of the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Villains villains] from ''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_Wiki My Hero Academia]'' (pretty self-explanatory). Below are some key features of the villains:

* [[Mastery]] quests often involve defeating specifically villains to complete them.
* Certain world events such as the Halloween event Trick-Or-Treat doors have given specific unique animations to villains, and villain's Trick-Or-Treat animation were the fastest back then.
* Villains make up 50% of the roster on average (this changes every new character, but Villains and [[Heroes]] stay equal to each other one after another ''generally.)''

The list of Heroes Battlegrounds' current villains includes:

* {{Hero Slayer}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Chizome “Stain”]/[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Stendhal_Arc “Stendhal”] [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Akaguro].
* [[Mastered Hero Slayer|Crimson Justice]] ({{Mastered Hero Slayer}}) ,based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Chizome “Stain”]/[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Stendhal_Arc “Stendhal”] [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Chizome_Akaguro Akaguro].
* {{Azure Flames}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dabi Toya “Dabi” Todoroki].
* [[Mastered Azure Flames|Violet Inferno]] ({{Mastered Azure Flames}}) based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Dabi Toya “Dabi” Todoroki]. during the [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Final_War Final War Arc].
* {{Decaying Hatred}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Tomura_Shigaraki Tenko “Tomura Shigaraki” Shimura], and the person from both spawn animation, [[Ultimate Mode|Everything For One]] and Reflective Counter finisher obviously represents [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/All_For_One Zen “All For One”]/“Demon Lord” Shigaraki.
* {{Warp Portal}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kurogiri Kurogiri].
* {{Human Architect}}, based on [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kai_Chisaki Kai “Overhaul” Chisaki].
Warp Portal:
{{Quote|'''''It's a pleasure to meet you, we are the League of Villains. I know it's impolite, but we decided to invite ourselves into this haven of justice to say hello. And besides, isn't this a fitting place for All Might the Symbol of Peace to take his last breath? I believe he was supposed to be here today and yet I see no sign of him. There must've been some sort of change in plans we could not have foreseen. Ah well, in the end I suppose it doesn't matter. I still have a role to play.|-[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kurogiri Kurogiri´s] introduction to Class 1-A and Thirteen.}} {{Character heros|NameOfCharacter={{Warp Portal}}|awakening_name=Quantum Jump|cost=Pity when killing {{The Prototype}}|image1= New Kurogiri Icon.png|New Icon Wrap.webp|Old Icon WarpPortalSpawn.gif|Spawn Animation New Warp Base.png|Base Portall.png|Awakening WarpPortalAura.gif|Awakening Aura |caption-image1=none}} == Description == {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Warp Portal'''}} {{Warp Portal}} is a character in Heroes Battlegrounds, based on the villain [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kurogiri Kurogiri] from [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/My_Hero_Academia_(Manga)?so=search My Hero Academia]. He was the 6th [[Characters|character]] added to the game (7th including {{Huge Lady}}). As a master of spatial manipulation through his 'Warp Gate' quirk, {{Warp Portal}}'s moveset revolves around teleportation, creating portals for offensive and defensive maneuvers, and disorienting opponents with unpredictable attacks. The {{Warp Portal}} moveset is an unlockable character. It is obtained by defeating {{The Prototype}} boss, with an obtainment chance ranging from 7% to 100% based on the player's accumulated [[Pity]]. Pity is gained when a player is among the top three damage dealers in a successful {{The Prototype}} defeat but does not receive {{Warp Portal}}. The amount of pity gained depends on placement: 3rd place grants 1% pity, 2nd place grants 2% pity, and 1st place grants 3% pity. Warp Portal is awarded if the random number generated (shown in a notification) is lower than the player's current pity percentage. == Pros and Cons == === Pros === * The [[Ultimate Mode|Ultimate]], {{Quantum Jump}}, can effectively separate teamers, allowing the user to engage opponents individually. ** This makes the character particularly strong in [[ranked]] modes and 2v2 scenarios. * Even if the ultimate move '''Crush''' is blocked, its finisher will still activate if the opponent is at approximately 15% health or less. * Possesses very good combo potential. * Timing '''Dimensional Volley''' correctly can allow it to hit opponents on their wakeup, enabling continued M1 combos. (Only after Reality Barrage) * '''Dimensional Volley''' can combo extend into some M1's if close enough and if done with good timing can create a HBG version of [https://the-strongest-battlegrounds-rblx.fandom.com/wiki/Techniques#Kyoto "Kyoto combos"] * If timed correctly, '''Portal Cascade''' can hit a ragdolled (mainly after an uppercut) opponent, to do this you must do it immediately after or wait until they are within head height. * '''Dimensional Volley''' can be a good setup for other moves such as '''Reality Barrage''' and '''Portal Cascade,''' as well as '''Gateway (Both Variants).''' * High damage combos that don't require high skill. * === Cons === * The end of '''Reality Barrage''' lacks invincibility frames (I-frames) and has minor endlag, which can be disadvantageous when fighting multiple opponents. * {{Quantum Jump}} is generally considered a less impactful ultimate compared to those of other characters. * Only two base moves can pick up ragdolled enemies, and both of these moves also cause ragdoll themselves, making extended ground combos somewhat more challenging. * The character relies heavily on the opponent having already used their evasive. * Very punishable when using most moves. ==Moveset== |-|Base Moveset= {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! style="background: #ae00ff;"|'''Moves''' ! style="background: #ae00ff;"|'''Damage''' ! style="background: #ae00ff;" |'''Cooldown''' ! style="background: #ae00ff;" |'''Information''' |- |[[File:Portal M1.gif|center|frameless|border]]'''Quantum Punches M1''' |16% (full chain) |~0.5 seconds | A basic punch sequence seemingly imbued with quantum energy. |- |[[File:Reality Barrage.gif|border|frameless|center]]'''Reality Barrage''' |17% |24 sec |The user grabs the victim and teleports them to a city environment (similar to that seen in Decaying Hatred's ultimate). The user slams the victim into a building wall, then teleports again to slam them onto a car, shattering its window. Finally, the user grabs the victim, teleports back to the original location, and launches them away. ---- *This move can hit grounded/ragdolled opponents. *This move bypasses block. *Possesses a fast startup animation. *Features one of the most visually impactful finishers in the game. *If an opponent uses their evasive just before hitting the ground after being launched, they will only take 12% damage, as the ground impact deals an additional 5%. *Still lacks I-frames at the end of the animation. |- |[[File:Portal Cascade.gif|border|frameless|center]]'''Portal Cascade''' |13% |19 sec |The user grabs their opponent by the neck and walks forward slightly while holding them, then throws the opponent away, causing them to stumble. Shortly afterward, the user raises their arms, and several portals appear.
