total M1 combo ~24% |Upon defeating an opponent, the user's subsequent M1 attacks create portals that deliver a second M1 hit after each of the user's own, effectively doubling the M1 impacts. ---- *Grants increased M1 damage. *Sometimes, when performing an uppercut while this passive is active, it may trigger the additional ultimate M1 effect that launches the enemy away (this does not apply to standard moves). |- |'''Cooldown Reset''' |N/A |After defeating an opponent with credit, all of the user's cooldowns reset. ---- *This applies to all characters. |} == Trivia/Notes== * The appearance of the user during the {{Quantum Jump}} ultimate (metal brace, glowing eyes) is inspired by the appearance of [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kurogiri?so=search Kurogiri] in My Hero Academia. * {{Warp Portal}}'s Ultimate theme music is '''[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vDj7wZ9QOl4 Skull Breaker]''' by '''[https://www.youtube.com/@FirTreeAudio FirTreeAudio - Royalty Free Music]'''. * {{Warp Portal}} is one of the five characters that you can unlock, the other four are {{Mastered Split Ice}}, {{Mastered Green Hero}}, {{Mastered Hero Slayer}}, and {{Mastered Azure Flames}}. * There is a low chance that when using Warp Portal's ultimate attacks, the character will recite quotes from '''[https://ultrakill.fandom.com/wiki/Minos_Prime Minos Prime]''', a boss from the indie game [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ultrakill Ultrakill]. * {{Warp Portal}} can be one of the more challenging characters for beginners to master complex combos with. * {{Warp Portal}} is unique in that its attacks are not directly based on specific combat maneuvers shown by [https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Kurogiri?so=search Kurogiri] in My Hero Academia, as Kurogiri's role in the series is primarily supportive and less focused on direct combat. * There is a slight chance the user will recite the line "WHAT IS THAT MELODY?!" when using '''Reality Collapse'''. This voice line originates from the Overwatch character [https://overwatch.fandom.com/wiki/Sigma Sigma]. (confirmed) * The ability '''Reality Barrage''' seems to be a reference to [https://mob-psycho-100.fandom.com/wiki/Ryo_Shimazaki Ryo Shimazaki] from [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mob_Psycho_100 Mob Psycho 100]. * {{Warp Portal}} is currently the only character moveset in the game that has undergone a complete rework. * The original fourth ultimate move for Warp Portal, "Star Burst," involved teleporting both the user and the opponent to the moon, allowing them to use the lunar surface as an arena. ** This was later reworked into "Spatial Fury," which still teleports the user and opponent to the moon but transitions into a cutscene move rather than free-form combat on the moon. * Features one of the longest finisher animations for a base moveset out of all the characters in the game, being '''Dimensional Volley''''s finisher, lasting for roughly 13,02 seconds. * When the rework originally came out, '''Reality Barrage''' had a strange bug where the I-frame did not apply during the animation. On top of that, both the victim and the user can freely use any moves on each other before the animation even ends. ** That being said, when someone uses '''Reality Barrage''' on a victim, and the victim happens to be {{Warp Portal}} as well, they can use '''Reality Barrage''' on the user mid-animation, resulting in them both being stuck in the city environment (before being teleported back). ** Similarly, when there happens to be another user in the city when performing '''Reality Barrage''', you can grab that user's victim by using other moves like '''Portal Cascade''', resulting in their victim disappearing on their end. {{Characters}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Special Characters]]

Weakest Dummy:
{{Stub}}

== Weakest Dummy ==
The Weakest Dummy is found in the the middle of the map. It can be used to fill your awakening bar and learn new combos. Similar to regular players, the Weakest Dummy has 110 health but only gives you half of the ultimate bar you would gain from fighting a actual player.

==Event Dummies==

===The Weakest Dummy's Secret Santa===
The '''Weakest Dummy's Secret Santa''' ('''W.D.S.S''') is an NPC dummy that can be found behind the igloo that is to the right of the abandoned shack. This dummy acts the same way as the Weakest Dummy and you are able to farm your [[Ultimate Mode|awakening]] off of this dummy. This dummy came apart of the [[Winter Spotlight]] [[Update Log|update]].

===The Weakest Dummy's Archnemesis===
'''The Weakest Dummy’s Archnemesis''' is found on the corner of the map on a tiny arena. That area is to the left the [[Structures and Objects|leaderboards.]] The Weakest Dummy's Archnemesis has the same purpose as the weakest dummy to help fill your awakening bar and learn new combos. Unlike regular players, the Weakest Dummy's Arch-Nemesis has 100 health instead of 110. This dummy was brought into the game in the 2024's Halloween update.

==Special Dummies==
These dummies in this category are from the [[Private Server Commands]] gamepass. These are all from the spawnables category. 

*'''Punching dummy''': The '''Punching dummy''' is only obtainable from Private Server+ in the spawnables section. It does a full M1 chain before taking a short cooldown and then repeating the cycle. It is mainly used to find combos that are not interruptible from the opponent and testing counters.
*'''Blocking dummy''': The '''Blocking dummy''' is only obtainable from Private Server+ in the spawnables section. Once it is spawned it will block the entire time it is alive until it does or its block is broken. It is mainly used to practice side dashing behind the opponent and finding one shot combos.
*'''1 HP Dummy''': The 1 '''HP dummy''' is only obtainable from Private Server+ in the spawnables section. Unlike other dummies, it has only 1 hp hence the name. It is mainly used to find finishers or test new Kill emotes.
*'''Invincible dummy''': The I'''nvincible dummy''' is only obtainable from Private Server+ in the spawnables section. It has a infinite amount of HP and cannot be killed. It is unknown what it is used for due to it just being released other than trying to kill it, or finding infinite combos that never end (Combo ends if enemy regains evasive). (If you wonder, none of the moves of {{The Strongest}} can kill the dummy, even Blue will leave it alive, however invisible. Red will leave it alive, but only with legs, and the same goes for Purple - it won't kill the dummy either.)
*'''Evasive dummy''': The '''Evasive dummy''' is only obtainable from Private Server+ in the spawnables section. Once the dummy is ragdolled, it will evasive dash no matter how many times you ragdoll it. It is mainly used to find true combos.

Winged Assassin:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Winged AssassinWinged Assassin'''}} {{Quote|'''I just wanna enjoy myself, seriously. Just do some leisurely patrolling, put in a few appearances, say «Aw, shucks, no trouble today» and then have a nice sleep, that's my ideal life I can only dream of!|Keigo Takami to Endeavor.'''}} * * {{Character_heros|GreenHero='''Winged AssassinWinged Assassin'''|image1= WingedAssassinIcon.png|Icon HawksSpawn.gif|Spawn Animation Placeholder.png|Base Placeholder.png|Awakening |awakening_name='''Wings of Ruin'''|cost='''[[Early Access]]''' ''(245 Robux)''|caption-image1=|NameOfCharacter={{Winged Assassin}}}} ==Description== {{Winged Assassin}} is a new character released into Heroes Battlegrounds as an Early Access character, currently having four moves with each having a finisher and a passive that allows the user to fly, which alternates three out of the four moves currently. The user of the character gains a pair of wings ''(With them being automatically removed during combat for best gameplay, this can be changed in settings)'' and two feather blades, which can be colorized via '''[[VIP]] [[Gamepasses|Gamepass]]''' ''(Only by Primary Color)''. The character is based on '''[https://myheroacademia.fandom.com/wiki/Keigo_Takami Keigo Takami]''', otherwise known as Hawks. This character is [[Early Access|under development]], and his base moveset is finished; his awakening and potential finishers for his ''Flight''-variant moves are left to make. ==Pros and Cons == === Pros === * Flight allows you to fly high enough for other players to not be able to hit you normally. * Twin Talons can catch opponents off-guard, on top of the first hit being unblockable. * Can easily run away with the passive. * Plume's Flight Variant also travels a great distance if aimed slightly upwards. * Using Awakening will heal you to 100%, making it easier for your survival until it is added. * Easy to team with in "Air Combos" with '''Full Throttle''' users due to the Flight passive. * Easy to evade certain moves using flight, making it easier to heal during fights * Hitting someone with Aerial Twin Talons sends both of you and the opponent far away, perfect against teamers. * Most moves have at least 1 other variant, the only exception being Crimson Heaven * High combo potential * Not terrible for beginners to use, considering that most of the moves are easy to land and chain into combos. * The character is the only moveset that can activate its passive without needing to land a finisher (although if you get the passive via landing a finisher, it lasts longer) * Plume has a decent range, despite the wind-up animation making it look like a close-range only move, making it very effective against new players and players that aren't used to fighting against Winged Assassin * The Wings can be hidden in combat, which can be done in the settings, making it easier to see during fights. === Cons === * No Awakening is built in yet, thus making Ult vs Base confrontations harder. * Wings cosmetic get in the way, forcing you to zoom out. * All moves (besides m1s) end flight. * Relatively low damage output compared to other movesets. * Landing Aerial Twin Talons and Plume after Crimson Heaven can be deemed challenging with high ping. * Twin Talons' Flight Variant, despite being the most damage out of the three variants, is slow and predictable. * The passive is required for most variants of moves, the only exception being Twin Talons' Aerial Variant Winter Spotlight:
'''Winter Spotlight''' was an official ROBLOX event themed around winter, holiday or Christmas. It featured portals to user-created experiences, with a central hub experience. The event started on December 17, 2024 at 10am PT and ended on January 2, 2025 at 12pm PT. [[Heroes Battlegrounds]] was apart of this event.

== Event Tokens ==

=== Token ===
This token is obtained by gaining ten kills.

=== Elite Token ===
The elite token is obtained by winning 3 Christmas games.

== Gamemodes ==
=== King of the Hill ===
They near the Christmas tree to get presents.

==== Gameplay ====
King of the Hill was added in the Winter Spotlight Update. Whoever stays inside the circle and reaches the 8th part of the bar, you will receive about 50 ornaments. Everytime this gamemode is picked, three Christmas trees will spawn. One at the Gojo dummy, another one of where the [[Weakest Dummy]]'s Archnemesis used to be, and the last one by the igloo.

=== Reverse Tag ===
Steal the present from whoever has it, then defend it.

==== Gameplay ====
Two players will be selected for who has the two presents. You will have to run after the player and ragdoll the player who has the present. Whoever has the present by the end of the match will get about 30 to 40 presents.

=== Santa's Sleigh ===
Run around and snag Santa's dropped presents.

==== Gameplay ====
Run around the map and take the Christmas presents before anyone else takes them.

== NPCs ==

=== Santa NPC ===
The '''Santa NPC''' at the Christmas shop acts exactly like the NPC Halloween shop Witch.

=== Weakest Dummy's Secret Santa ===
The '''Weakest Dummy's Secret Santa''' ('''W.D.S.S''') is a NPC dummy that can be found behind the igloo that is to the right of the abandoned shack. This dummy acts the same way as the [[Weakest Dummy]] and you are able to farm your ult off of this dummy.

== Special Locations ==

=== Christmas Shop ===
The Christmas shop is ran by the Santa NPC. It is located to the left of the slide.

=== Igloo ===
The igloo is where the [[Weakest Dummy|Weakest Dummy's Secret Santa]] resides. It is located to the left of the abandoned shack.

=== Christmas Tree ===
The big Christmas tree is located behind the leaderboard. It has presents under the tree.

=== Gingerbread House ===
The gingerbread house is located to the left of the abandoned shack by the far rock.

=== Not A Santa Hideout ===
Not A Santa Hideout replaced the Not A Secret Hideout for the Christmas update. It has special Winter decorations.
